text: Dogs in Portugal — registration, dangerous breeds, tax

Dog registration: SIAC (Sistema de Identificação dos Animais de Companhia) is mandatory after microchip — free at the vet. Municipal licence (licença canina) — €5-15/year at Junta de Freguesia. Dangerous breeds (Category A/B according to DL 315/2009): civil liability insurance of at least €50k is mandatory, owner must be ≥ 18 years old without a judicial record, muzzle + leash in public places. List: Rottweiler, Pit Bull, Staffordshire, Tosa Inu, Fila Brasileiro, Cão de Fila, American Staffordshire, Dogo Argentino, and mixes.
Mandatory for **all dogs** ≥ 4 months (and domestic cats)
Automatically registered in SIAC (centralized database) — free
Submitted at Junta de Freguesia where you reside
Documents: SIAC registration, rabies vaccination, certificado de esterilização (if sterilised)
Cost: **€5-15/year** (depends on the municipality; categories A/B — more expensive, up to €50)
Badge (medalha) — a ring with a number, attached to the collar
The veterinarian also checks blood titer when entering from non-EU
⚠️ Dangerous breeds — Categoria A and B
DL 315/2009 defines “perigoso” and “potencialmente perigoso” — special rules for registration, insurance, and handling.
List of breeds (Categoria B — potencialmente perigoso)
Cão de Fila de São Miguel
Any dog that has shown aggression towards a person or animal (outside provocation) is classified as Category A (dangerous) by decision of the municipal veterinarian
Completion of a special course on 'formação em comportamento canino' (according to some municipalities)
Declaration to Junta regarding the presence of a dangerous dog
On the street: **muzzle (açaime) + lead shorter than 1 m**
Not allowed in playgrounds, public transport, restaurants
In the yard: fence ≥ 1.8 m, sign ‘cão perigoso’
Walk only with an adult handler
No more than 2 dogs of category A/B in one household without a special licence
Repeated offences: seizure of the dog, prohibition of ownership for 5 years
Professional breeding requires registration with DGAV (Direção-Geral de Alimentação e Veterinária)
Breeding of category A/B is prohibited (since 2009) — only sterilisation is allowed
Selling without chip/register — up to €3,750 fine
Kennel clubs: Clube Português de Canicultura (cpc.pt) for official pedigrees
🆘 What to do with an abandoned dog
Call the muni canil (municipal shelter): each municipality has its own
Or GNR SEPNA (nature protection police) 217 653 100 — for wild / potentially dangerous cases
Help: União Zoófila (Lisbon), SOS Animal, Associação Animais de Rua — NGO
In parks / trails, a dog without a handler — report to SEPNA or the fire brigade
An abandoned dog in PT — criminal if intent is proven (up to 3 years imprisonment under Lei 69/2014)
🏥 **Pet insurance**: €10-40/month — Tranquilidade Pet, Médis Animal, PetPremium
Muni canil — check every 2-3 days (dogs are often brought here)
Social media: Facebook group «Animais Perdidos» in your region
Post flyers in the area — with photo, name, phone number (do not disclose that there is a reward)
If you find someone else's dog: scan the chip at the vet (all vet clinics have a reader)
You cannot keep a found dog — this is theft under the law

Cats: SIAC chip is mandatory from 2022 for all cats aged ≥ 4 months. Licence from Junta — €5-15/year (in most municipalities). Exotic pets (reptiles, birds, rodents): microchip + DGAV registration required for CITES/protected species. Prohibition: dangerous wild animals (large primates, big cats, certain venomous snakes). Import from non-EU: AHC, vaccinations, microchip, sometimes CITES permit. Veterinary costs: cat — €25-40 per session; rare animal — €50-80.
Cats have been required to have a chip since **22 December 2022** (DL 82/2019, gradual rollout). Similar to dogs.
🆔 **SIAC microchip** — mandatory from 4 months of age
📋 **Licence from Junta**: ~€5-15/year; not required everywhere (smaller municipalities may not enforce)
🔍 **Identification**: chip number + Junta licence medal (optional on collar)
🚪 **Outdoor cats** — mandatory chip; some municipalities make neutering a condition for outdoor licence
No rabies titer tests for EU-EU
🇷🇺 RU = high-risk country — **rabies titration test** (blood analysis) is mandatory

At the border: control at port of entry (often Lisbon / Porto airport)
Can be done through DGAV import permit in advance, particularly for rare breeds
Hamsters, guinea pigs, dwarf rabbits — no special rules
Purchase: pet shops, breeders, online (OLX has many listings)
Veterinarian - 'exotic': specialists are available in every major city
Import from non-EU: AHC + animal health certificate; rabies vaccine not required for rodents
Ferrets: treated like cats — chip + Pet Passport are mandatory
Aviaries (large cages): comfort requirements according to Lei 92/95

It is prohibited to buy/keep as a pet without ICNF permission. Falconry — requires licence. Found wounded bird — contact Centro de Recuperação RIAS / LPN.
Bearded dragons, leopard geckos, corn snakes, ball pythons (small), tortoises (Hermann's, Russian) — common in pet stores
Aquatic turtles (red-eared sliders): banned for new ownership since 2016 (invasive species)
🚫 **Venomous snakes** (cobras, mambas, etc.): prohibition on owning

🚫 **Some monitor lizards** (Komodo, large savannas): CITES + special licence
⚠️ **Tortoises**: many EU-protected species — cannot catch wild, only captive-bred with CITES
Pet store equipment: €100-€500 for full setup
Vet-‘exotic’: €50-€80 visit (specialised clinics in Lisbon, Porto)
Common health issues: metabolic bone disease (improper UV), parasites, scale rot
Pet stores: tropicalfish.pt, Petline, Fauna&Flora, specialised aquarists

Banned: piranhas, certain cichlids invasive in Algarve waterways
Aquarium services: companies provide setup + maintenance subscription €30-€100/month
Aquarist clubs: Aquariofilia.pt, Facebook groups for trade / advice
EU Invasive Alien Species (Regulation 1143/2014) + national PT extensions prohibit many species as pets.
🐢 Trachemys scripta (red-eared slider), Pseudemys (FL slider) — disposed of correctly or given to authorised rescue
🐦 Indian myna, ringneck parakeet — banned in the wild
🐠 Plecostomus catfish (large) in open water — illegal
**Hospital Veterinário do Porto** — large animal exotics
Specialised diets: only in specialised pet stores or online (Zooplus)
Pet insurance for exotics: limited, usually does not cover reptiles / birds

For dogs, cats, and ferrets — microchip + rabies vaccination ≥ 21 days before entry + Pet Passport (EU) or AHC (Animal Health Certificate for non-EU). EU countries — practically unrestricted. Non-EU — certificate from a state-accredited veterinarian + documents in English/Portuguese.
Rabies vaccination ≥ 21 days before entry (after the first vaccination)
Rabies antibody (for high-risk rabies countries — blood titre, validity 30 days, 3 months wait after the test)
Documents: Pet Passport (for EU/EEA) OR Animal Health Certificate (AHC) — non-EU
For Egypt, Turkey, some African countries — additional requirements. For Russia in 2026 — titre is mandatory.
Pet Passport (European), issued by a veterinarian in an EU country. With it — free entry into PT. Border checks are minimal.
At the veterinary clinic in the RF. Check the compatibility of the chip with the ISO standard.
Mandatory for the RF. Blood is sent to an accredited laboratory (in the EU or some in the RF). Antibodies ≥ 0.5 IU/ml.
From the date of blood collection for the titre — 3 months before entering the EU. Plan ahead.
The state veterinary clinic issues form №1 (for the RF), which is exchanged for form №5e for export → at the state veterinary control PP upon departure — obtain AHC in English.
At the point of entry into the EU (Lisbon, Porto, Faro) — show AHC + passport + chip. Checked by pet border inspection post (BIP).
Airplane — cargo (large) or cabin (small up to 8 kg). Each airline has its own rules.

Ferry — check the rules of the specific line

Obtain a Portuguese Pet Passport — may be useful for trips back

On the beach and in the city — a muzzle + leash is often required

Importing a car from the EU — detailed procedure

Importing a car from the EU (Germany, France, Spain, Poland, Netherlands, Italy) is popular due to a ~20-30% price difference. Procedure: 1) DAV (customs declaration of vehicles) within 20 days; 2) ISV (depends on CC, CO₂, age — for used cars partial discounts up to 65%); 3) IUC annually; 4) Registration (PT number plate) at IMT; 5) IPO (vehicle inspection); 6) COC or certificate of conformity. For new EVs — ISV 0%. For relocation (transferência residência) — full ISV exemption if owned for 6+ months.
💰 **Price**: the German used car market is 20-30% cheaper than the Portuguese
🔧 **Choice**: in PT, there is a limited selection of specific models/configurations
🏎️ **Segment**: premium (BMW, Audi, Mercedes) cars are rare in PT, often bought from DE/NL
⚡ **Electric cars**: a wide selection in NL/DE
🔙 **Not worth it**: mass models (Renault Clio, VW Polo) — the difference is small, transport costs
Estimated budget for a used BMW 318d 2020 bought in Germany for €18,000:
Transport (car carrier DE → PT): **€1,200-1,800** OR self-driving (fuel + motels ~€400)
ISV (vehicle tax on registration): **€1,500-3,500** (depends on CC and CO₂, for a 5-year-old car a 40% discount)
Savings are not always huge — calculate a specific example. For cars <€10,000, savings are often eaten up by transport costs. For ≥€20,000 premium — usually worth it.
Check the car in person (or through a specialist inspector €100-200)

**Discount for used**: 5-20% in the first year, up to 65% after 5 years

With this, you go to IMT (Instituto da Mobilidade e dos Transportes)

If you are moving to Portugal (transferência de residência) — you can import a car with **FULL exemption from ISV**.
📅 **Conditions**: lived in the country of departure for ≥ 6 months before moving
🚗 **Conditions**: your car for ≥ 6 months before moving (prove with: registration card + insurance + maintenance bills)
📅 **Submission period**: 12 months after becoming a PT resident
🚫 **Cannot sell** within 12 months after import (otherwise ISV retroactively applies)
📄 **Documents**: certificado de transferência de residência from MNE (PT consulate in the country of departure) + documents for the car + proof of residence in PT
💡 **Huge savings**: ISV is usually €2,000-5,000 for an average car — free if Isenção applies
⚠️ If you changed the purpose of your trip / bought the car recently — Isenção does not apply
**No COC**: without it, ISV is calculated at the maximum rate + registration is blocked; order from the manufacturer in DE for €200-500, wait 4-8 weeks
**VIN not recognised**: engineering inspection required at IMT — €200-400
**Older than 25 years / vintage**: separate registration category (‘veículo histórico’), cheaper ISV, but restrictions on emissions
**Modifications** (tuning, big wheels): not all accepted in PT, may not pass IPO
**Cars from outside the EU** (UK after Brexit, US): full customs + 23% IVA on top. More complicated than EU
**Delay**: the entire procedure usually takes 4-8 weeks from entry to PT plates. Plan ahead for car rental during this period

For residents, there is a benefit called 'transferência de residência' — import without ISV under the conditions: owned the car for ≥ 6 months before moving, lived in the old country for ≥ 12 months, submitted documents within 12 months after becoming a resident. Otherwise — full ISV (customs duty) + IUC annually.
A. Benefit (transferência de residência) — without ISV
You become a resident of PT (received residence permit / CRUE)
Owned the car for ≥ 6 months before moving
Lived in the country of origin for ≥ 12 months
Submit documents within 12 months from the date of becoming a resident
The car is not sold within 12 months after import
Applies only to ONE car per person

If the conditions for the exemption are not met — full ISV (Imposto sobre Veículos). Calculated based on engine size + CO2 + age. For a new car, it can be €5,000-30,000. For electric cars — free of charge.

IPO (roadworthiness test) — every 2 years for cars up to 8 years old, annually for older ones

Car insurance in Portugal — RC is mandatory, Casco optional

Responsabilidade Civil (civil liability insurance) — mandatory third-party insurance for each vehicle registered in PT (Decreto-Lei 291/2007). Covers damage to third parties. Casco / Danos Próprios — coverage for your own car, optional. Prices 2026: basic RC from €180/year, full package — €400–€1,200. Online purchase at Tranquilidade / Fidelidade / Zurich / Ageas / brokers.
Covers damage to third parties (vehicles, people)
Minimum limit: €1,300,000 per person and €8,000,000 per event

Without RC — fine of €250–€1,250, vehicle seizure
Usually required for leasing or loans
Tow truck + roadside assistance. Not mandatory, but very useful. Often included in packages for €30–60/year. More details in the guide 'Flat tyre on the motorway'.
Passenger life insurance in case of an accident. An additional €30–80/year.

Subsidies and benefits for electric vehicles in Portugal

The government subsidises the purchase of a new EV up to €4,000 for individuals and €6,000 for companies (Fundo Ambiental, application limit is annual — runs out quickly). Plus: ISV 0%, reduced IUC, discounted parking, Mobi.E charging at €0.28–0.40/kWh. Budget 2026 — ~€8 million, online submission from January.
Portugal annually funds the Incentive for the Introduction to the Market of Low Emission Vehicles programme. The budget is reset on 1 January and usually ends within 2-4 months.
Individual, new BEV passenger car (category M1): up to €4,000 (max 2 cars per year)
Legal entity/self-employed, new BEV M1: up to €6,000 (max 4 cars per year)
The car price must not exceed €62,500 (excluding IVA) to participate

fundoambiental.pt → Candidaturas → Mobilidade Elétrica. Applications are only ONLINE with CMD or CC. Opening windows — usually 15 January. Required: invoice for the car (can be pré-factura), copy of residence permit/CC, NIF, IBAN for payment.
30–90 days after approval. Money arrives in the IBAN as a refund.
The programme budget for 2024-2025 ended within 2-3 months. If purchasing an EV in spring — apply in January-February.
PHEV (plug-in hybrid) with an electric range ≥50 km: reduced ISV (discount 25-40%)
HEV (conventional hybrid): standard ISV like petrol/diesel
The duration of the benefit — confirmed annually in OE
BEV: from 2024 NOT fully exempt (was 100% until 2023). Now ~€30-50/year depending on CC / power
PHEV: standard IUC, but with a lower environmental impact
Company EV: full depreciation up to €62,500 (for petrol — €25,000)
Charging (kWh) — 100% VAT deduction for companies (for petrol — 50%)
Key for freelancers: EV is the most effective way for tax relief if you have recibos verdes with high turnover
Portugal has a centralised national Mobi.E network. All public chargers (even from different operators — Galp, EDP, Repsol, Iberdrola) work through a common hub. One account — all of PT.
🏠 Home wallbox: EDP/Galp tariff — €0.16–0.20/kWh at night (tarifa bi-horária)
💳 Payment: Mobi.E card (€5 registration) OR payment through EDP, Galp, Iberdrola apps
🅿️ Free parking: during charging at Mobi.E stations — first 2 hours
EV from 2023 — green frame on the license plate (mandatory at first registration)
Old EVs — can be exchanged for green for €30 at IMT
Green plate = free entry into Lisbon's ZER (low emission zone, expanded from 2025)
PHEV — plate without frame; partial benefits

Petrol and diesel — how to save on fuel

Price at petrol stations in PT 2026: petrol (95) €1.70-€1.90/l, diesel (gasoline) €1.55-€1.75/l. The difference between expensive (Galp / BP / Repsol) and cheap (Prio, Intermarché, Continente, Cepsa / Moeve) — up to €0.15/l. Through the apps ComboMais / IdealGás / Tankpreise — find the cheapest refuelling within 5 km. BP loyalty card, Galp Poupa, Continente/Pingo Doce fuel give a discount of €0.05-€0.12/l. Best refuelling hours: Tuesday-Thursday; Friday-Sunday — prices are higher.
Prices in PT are free but regulated by the market. The government publishes weekly recommended maximum prices — all petrol stations keep below this.
🔴 **Premium** (Galp, BP, Repsol, ENI, Cepsa/Moeve): €1.75-€1.90/l — in cities, on motorways. Sooner or later slightly more expensive
🟡 **Mid** (OMV, Prio, BPCarga): €1.65-€1.80/l — in regions, less in centres
🟢 **Budget** (Intermarché AŽS, Continente Auto, Leclerc): €1.55-€1.70/l — attached to supermarkets, often the cheapest
Motorway (Via Rápida / Auto-Estrada): more expensive by €0.05-€0.10/l
✅ **Rule**: leaving the motorway for a rural ebzs = saving €5-€10/full tank

IPO — how, when, and how much

Inspeção Periódica Obrigatória (IPO) — mandatory periodic inspection. A new car's first IPO is after 4 years, then every 2 years until 8 years old, after which it is annual. It is carried out at IMT-accredited centres (CERTIEL / SGS / Riberalves / Controlauto, etc.). Costs €30–40 for light class B. Bring: DUA, insurance, owner's ID, driver's licence.

IPO is regulated by Decreto-Lei 144/2017 and Portaria 178/2023. Oversight is by IMT (Instituto da Mobilidade e dos Transportes). Certified centres operate under concession, with a unified IMT database.
Passenger vehicles (class B, up to 3.5 t)
New: first IPO after 4 years from registration
Taxis / rental / driving school / commercial fleet: annually regardless of age
First IPO after 4 years, then every 2 years (regardless of age).
Annually from the first year of use.
The same deadlines. Additionally — inspection of high-voltage wiring insulation.
The IPO date is recorded on a sticker under the windscreen and in the Portal das Contra-Ordenações (IMT). Late fees — €250–€1,250.
💻 Websites of centres (Controlauto, SGS, Riberalves, Tecnauto, Auto-Inspeção) — booking 7/7
📞 Often available for walk-in at large centres with a 15–30 min wait
🗓️ Up to 3 months before the end of the previous deadline
📍 Limitation — only in PT, any centre can be chosen
A list of all accredited centres is published by IMT on its website in the section 'Inspeção Técnica de Veículos'.

ePortugal — Carry out the periodic vehicle inspection

Imposto Único de Circulação (IUC) — annual tax on vehicle ownership in Portugal. Payable in the month of the anniversary of the vehicle's first registration. Calculation depends on the class (A–E), engine size, CO2 emissions, and year. For a passenger car with a 1.4L petrol engine — typically €40–€120, large diesel — €300–€700. Payment online via Portal das Finanças, card / MBWay / Multibanco. Late payment — fine of 10–50%.
Owner registered in DUA as of 1 January of this year
Payment deadline — by the end of the month of the anniversary of the vehicle's first registration
Example: vehicle registered on 10 March 2020 — IUC is due by 31 March each year
Change of ownership — payment falls on the person registered in DUA at the time of expiry
Sale of vehicle → it is advisable to settle IUC before the sale, otherwise you will have to deal with it later
If IUC is not paid — DUA is blocked. You will not pass the technical inspection (IPO), and you cannot sell the vehicle without payment either.
Standard category. Rate based on engine size + CO2. 1.0–1.4 l = €40–70; 1.6–2.0 l = €90–170; 2.5+ l = €300+.
Category C — trucks up to 12 t
Exempt from IUC (only Imposto sobre os Veículos VAT when purchasing + nominal fee).
The full formula — Código do IUC, art. 9–15 (dre.pt). A simple calculator — on the Portal das Finanças (tax authority online portal) before payment.
📄 A payment document is generated with entity + reference + amount
📧 Proof is saved in Portal Finanças
💼 > 90 days — case with Finanças → possible salary attachment
🚫 Block DUA — you won't pass IPO, you won't sell the car
When buying a used car, always ask the seller for the IUC-comprovativo (proof) for the last 3 years. Without it, you may inherit debt.
🚗 First year for veterans and large families — discount
🏝️ Madeira and Açores — reduced rates for locals
Checking used cars before buying in Portugal

Five checks before buying a used car: (1) IMT online — match DUA, no liens or debts, (2) Finanças — IUC not in debt, (3) recent IPO inspection, (4) Carfax equivalent Carvertical for history, (5) inspection at an independent workshop. For around €50, avoid buying a car with debts or hidden defects that will later become yours.

The seller is required to show the Declaração sem dívidas for IUC and IMT for the last 3 years. This can be requested at Portal das Finanças → ‘Certidões’ → ‘Certidão de Não Dívida’.

⚠️ If the IPO has not been done for a long time — buy with the condition ‘pass IPO first’
Carvertical (carvertical.com), CarfaxPT, Autobiz — paid services for checking history by VIN. They show accidents, mileage, and owners across all EU countries.

Accidents in Portugal — Friendly Declaration (European protocol) and when to call GNR

In case of an accident without injuries and disputes — fill out the Declaração Amigável de Acidente Automóvel (DAAA), this is a bilateral European protocol. If there is a dispute, a drunk driver, injuries, or third parties — call GNR/PSP (112). The deadline for submitting DAAA to the insurer is 8 days, otherwise you lose the right to compensation.
🚨 Turn on the hazard lights. If on the autoestrada — read the guide 'Flat tyre on the motorway'
🦺 Put on a reflective vest BEFORE exiting the car (mandatory in PT)
⚠️ Place the triangle 30 m away (city street — 50 cm from the pavement is OK, autoestrada — 30 m)
📸 Photograph: positions of vehicles, numbers, road signs, skid marks, damages
🪪 Exchange: ID, driving licences, vehicle document (DUA), Carta Verde (insurance policy)
🚑 There are injured persons (of any kind — even 'just a bruise')
💥 Material damage clearly > €5,000 or destruction of infrastructure
🚗 One of the participants without documents / without insurance
🌍 One of the participants is a foreigner without PT insurance, does not speak the languages
GNR Brigada de Trânsito covers motorways and intercity, PSP — cities. They will prepare an auto de notícia (police report) — a serious document for court/insurance.

European Accident Statement Form — standard across the EU. Each driver has it in their glove box along with Carta Verde. It is a self-copying form.

Diagram of the accident — draw the layout, arrows, signs
Tick 17 checkboxes of circumstances (speed, turn, overtaking, etc.)

How to check a used car before buying in Portugal

The seller is required to provide DUA (Documento Único Automóvel), valid IPO, manual, keys (original + spare). Penalty for the seller for concealment: up to 3 × the value of the car.

A flat tyre on the motorway — correct procedure

Changing a tyre on Portuguese autoestradas is strictly prohibited on the carriageway. The procedure is: hazard lights → pull over to emergency lane → triangle 30 m → vest → call assistance (707 500 501 Brisa or 707 509 510 IP). For regular drivers — travel assistance insurance usually covers towing and shuttle.
🛞 DO NOT brake sharply — keep the steering wheel steady, gradually ease off the accelerator
➡️ Check the right mirror — is there a gap to pull onto the aceite de emergência (hard shoulder)
🛑 Move as far away from the lane as possible, parallel to the barrier
⚙️ Engage the parking brake, turn off the ignition, remove the key
If a tyre bursts at speeds over 100 km/h and you lose control — do not try to stop immediately, gradually reduce speed, steer to the centre of the lane, stabilise the vehicle.
🦺 Put on a reflective vest BEFORE exiting
🚪 Exit from the side OPPOSITE to the traffic (through the passenger door)
🚸 All passengers — behind the barrier, away from the lane
⚠️ Place a warning triangle at least 30 m behind the vehicle (art. 88 Código da Estrada)
📍 Remember the kilometre marker (kp) — assistance will be needed

DO NOT change a tyre on the hard shoulder of the autoestrada even if you have a spare. This is strictly prohibited by the Código da Estrada and is dangerous — every year drivers are killed by passing trucks.

If available (usually in the RC package or separately for around €30/year), call the insurer's number — they will organise a tow truck and shuttle to the nearest garage. Usually free for the first 50–100 km.
There are always emergency posts (SOS) every 2 km
C. GNR — if there are casualties or an accident
112 — universal emergency services number. GNR Traffic Brigade responds to traffic accidents.
🏪 Receipt for a new tyre — if the damage is due to a pothole / debris, you can claim from the concessionaire (707 500 501)
📝 Fill in a complaint on the Brisa / Ascendi website with a photo of the location + KP
💶 Concessionaires often reimburse the cost of the tyre if you prove the cause (photo of the pothole, GNR report)
🛞 Check tyre pressure once a month at the petrol station (free at Galp/BP)
🌡️ In summer, increase by +0.3 bar from the nominal — the Portuguese heat 'deflates' tyres
👀 Inspect tread for cracks and bulges every 2 months
📐 Tread depth must be at least 1.6 mm (strictly enforced by IPO)
🎯 Change tyres in pairs on the same axle
📅 Tyres older than 10 years — replace even if tread is good (deterioração do composto)

Renting in PT — from €20/day (budget compact) to €150+/day (premium). International companies like Hertz / Europcar / Avis are more expensive than local ones but more reliable. Budget: Sixt, Goldcar, Centauro — cheap, but many hidden fees. Requirements: driver age ≥ 21, licence ≥ 1 year, credit card (debit not accepted everywhere). Full insurance (CDW + TP + zero excess) ~€15-25/day — mandatory if you don't have a premium credit card. Deposit €800-2500. Via Verde is usually included, toll roads are selective.

Serious traffic violations — criminal traffic offences

In Portugal, some violations are not administrative but criminal (Código Penal + Código da Estrada). These include: driving with a BAC ≥1.2 g/l, refusal to take an alcohol test, driving after a driving ban, manslaughter / serious bodily harm through negligence, hit-and-run. Penalties: driving ban from 2 months to 3 years, fines up to €15,000, imprisonment up to 5 years. Points deducted from the licence: 4-5 points immediately. A criminal record remains in the certidão do registo criminal.
Alcohol behind the wheel — the main trap
Portugal has one of the strictest BAC control systems in Europe.
🍺 **BAC 0.5–0.8 g/l** (about 1-2 glasses of wine) — **serious offence**: fine €250-€1,250, ban 1-12 months, minus 3 points
🍷 **BAC 0.8–1.2 g/l** — **very serious offence**: fine €500-€2,500, ban 2-24 months, minus 5 points
🍸 **BAC ≥ 1.2 g/l** — **criminal** (art. 292 Código Penal): up to 1 year imprisonment or fine up to 120 daily rates, ALWAYS a ban of 2+ months, criminal record for 5-10 years
🚫 **Refusal to take a test** — equated to BAC ≥ 1.2 g/l + charge of "desobediência qualificada" (up to 2 years imprisonment)
⚠️ For new drivers (<3 years of licence): limit 0.2 g/l — even a pint of beer can be a violation
💼 For professional drivers (taxis, trucks, buses): 0.2 g/l

Since 2016, the points system (carta por pontos). All drivers start with 12 points. Each serious violation results in a deduction of 1-5 points. Reaching 0 means loss of licence, retaking theory + practical tests.
Exceeding speed by 30-60 km/h in the city (60-80 outside the city, 80-100 on the motorway)
Using a phone while driving without hands-free
Failing to yield to a pedestrian on the zebra crossing
No child seat for under 12 years / under 135 cm

Failing to stop when required by GNR / PSP
Driving in an unauthorised area (one-way, pedestrian zone)
Driving without a licence or with an expired one
Driving without a valid IPO (technical inspection)
Refusal to take an alcohol/drug test
Manslaughter / serious harm due to negligence
Points recovery: +1 point for every 3 years without violations, max 12. A 'rehabilitation course' is possible — 2-3 days at IMT — +2 points (paid, ~€250).

Traffic fines in Portugal — types, points, appeals

Fines from ANSR (Autoridade Nacional de Segurança Rodoviária) are divided into light (leves), serious (graves), and very serious (muito graves). A driver has 12 points in the 'carta por pontos' system — points are deducted for violations. At 0 points — driving licence suspension. 50% discount if paid within 15 days. Appeals — 15 days in writing to ANSR or court.
Example: not wearing a seatbelt, crossing a stop line, failing to yield to a pedestrian
Example: speeding 30–60 km/h on the motorway, crossing a solid line, overtaking at a crossing
Example: speeding over 60 km/h, driving under the influence, driving against oncoming traffic, running a red light
Possible suspension of driving rights for up to 2 years

The points-based licence has been in effect since 1 June 2016. Each driver starts with 12 points, which are deducted for violations. Points are automatically restored: +1 for every 3 years without incidents.

Via Verde vs alternatives — all ways to pay for roads

Via Verde — an electronic identifier for toll-free passage. Not the only option. Alternatives: Via Card (one-time), pre-paid Toll Card for tourists, post-payment via CTT / Payshop within 5 days, EasyToll for foreign plates.
Via Verde (the best option for residents)
Electronic identifier (green box on the windscreen)
Works on ALL toll roads, EMEL parking, Galp/Repsol fuel stations (discounts), Transtejo, Soflusa ferries
Installation: order online at viaverde.pt, delivered by post, or at Via Verde/CTT branches
Free (no monthly fee): Via Verde Gratuita subscription for those with <€8/month traffic

Plastic pre-paid card for a specific amount (€5, €10, €20). Used at 'electronic' toll booths on old toll roads (A1, A2) — insert into the Via Verde apparatus on the contact strip. DOES NOT work on Scut (toll-free).

Upon first entry onto Scut, you link your bank card to the licence plate at the EasyToll ports (available at the border and airports). After this, the toll is automatically deducted. It is valid for 30 days.
Card with a balance of €5/€10/€20/€40. Activated via SMS → baixa. Purchase at Galp, Repsol, BP petrol stations. For short trips.

If you drove on Scut without Via Verde and did not link EasyToll — you must pay at a CTT post office or Payshop within 5 working days using the matrícula number. After 5 days — fine + legal proceedings through AT.
What is Scut and how to distinguish them

Scut (Sem Cobrança ao Utilizador) — previously 'free-flow' roads, now toll roads via electronic portals. There are no booths. Do not stop — cameras read the number and should deduct from Via Verde or EasyToll.
Sign: portal above the road with the sign 'Pórtico Eletrónico'
When passing through: green light = passed; red = unpaid (but still went through, now you owe)

If the car has a PT number

Via Verde (electronic toll system) — a complete guide to electronic tolls in Portugal

Via Verde is the electronic payment for tolls via identificador (transponder). Subscription from €5 activation + €1.75/month. An alternative is post-payment through `portagens.ctt.pt` or CTT/Payshop terminals, with a timeframe of 48 hours / 5 working days. Via Verde Portugal — a concession by Brisa / Ascendi / IP (Infraestruturas de Portugal). It is the leading electronic toll collection system: a small device (identificador) on the windscreen that automatically deducts the fee when passing under the portal without stopping.

Opening a bank account in Portugal (for non-residents and residents)

There are about 9 major banks in Portugal with a Banco de Portugal licence. Several offer fully online account opening for non-residents with a Portuguese NIF (tax ID). Typical process: video identification, basic KYC, IBAN issued on the same day, card sent by post in 5-10 days. Basic services are free for most with minimal activity.
Why you need a Portuguese account

A bank account in Portugal with an IBAN starting with PT50 is necessary for most everyday procedures: registering long-term rentals, direct debits (utilities, taxes, traffic fines), receiving salaries, making investments, receiving benefits from Segurança Social (social security), applying for a residence permit (some types require proof of funds in a PT account).

An IBAN from another EU country (IE, DE, LT, etc.) is technically equivalent to a Portuguese one under SEPA regulations, but in practice, several Portuguese counterparts refuse direct debit for foreign IBANs. For full compatibility, a PT IBAN is needed.
Large universal banks: Millennium BCP, Caixa Geral de Depósitos (state bank), Novo Banco, Santander Totta, BPI
Online subsidiaries of large banks: ActivoBank (from Millennium BCP)
Digital neobanks with EU licence operating in Portugal: Revolut (LT), N26 (DE), Wise (BE) — IBAN not PT, but SEPA works
Cooperative and regional banks: Crédito Agrícola, Montepio — often convenient in small towns
Accounts at CTT (post office): CTT Bank — state postal operator with basic banking services
All listed are licensed by Banco de Portugal or the EU regulator; deposits up to €100,000 are insured (Fundo de Garantia de Depósitos for PT-licensed).

Several banks allow fully online account opening for clients with a NIF. The process: video identification with an operator in Portuguese or English, electronic signature via Chave Móvel Digital (government electronic signature) or through video confirmation. This is suitable even if you are not yet in Portugal, but already have a NIF.
The classic route is to visit a branch with your passport, NIF, proof of address, and sometimes proof of income. Large banks often require in-person identification for non-residents.
Passport (valid for ≥ 6 months, for non-EU citizens)
Proof of address — rental contract, utility bill, or bank statement not older than 3 months
Proof of income/source of funds — payslip, bank statement, employer's declaration
Portuguese phone number — not mandatory, but greatly simplifies 2FA and MB Way

On the bank's website or app: full name, NIF, profession, source of income, purpose of the account, tax residency. For non-residents of PT, you need to indicate the country of current tax residency (CRS/DAC2 compliance).
Scans/photos of passport, NIF, proof of address. Quality: all corners visible, no glare.
Video call lasting 5-15 minutes. The operator asks to show the passport, turn it, and asks control questions (according to AML regulations). They speak in PT or EN, sometimes ES.
Electronic signature: either through Chave Móvel Digital or via confirmation code from email + SMS.
The IBAN is active on the day of approval (1-3 days). The debit card arrives by post within 5-10 working days.

Euro account + multi-currency options with some banks
Debit card Visa/Mastercard (free issuance at most online banks)

SWIFT transfer fee outside SEPA: €10-40 + correspondent fees
Banks in PT are required to carry out AML/KYC checks according to Banco de Portugal regulations and EU regulations. Common reasons for refusal when opening an account for non-residents:
Mismatch between the address on documents and in the application
No proof of source of funds for a large initial deposit
Political exposure (PEP) or sanctions filter — refusal or prolonged due diligence
Poor quality video identification — often requested to be repeated
Refusal to provide tax residency (CRS) — automatic refusal
Fraudulent past transactions in international registry
If Bank A refused, it doesn't mean all will refuse — conditions and AML thresholds differ. On average, 2-3 attempts are sufficient.

ATM, cash and limits in Portugal

The main ATM network is Multibanco (MB), with approximately 12,000 devices, working with all PT cards. The cash withdrawal limit is €200–400 per day, depending on the card. Cash payments between individuals are limited to €3,000 (Lei 92/2017). Torn banknotes can be exchanged at banks, partially at Banco de Portugal. A cash deposit limit over €10,000 requires justification of the source under AML.
Operator — SIBS. ATMs are located near all banks, at petrol stations, in shopping centres, and airports. They accept all Visa/Mastercard/Maestro cards plus local PT cards.

Money transfers from the CIS to Portugal — legal channels 2026

EU sanctions (2022+) blocked SWIFT with major Russian banks and limited transfers. Legal channels remain: small Russian banks without sanctions, non-sanctioned banks from the CIS (KZ, AM, GE), neobanks in the EU (via intermediaries), crypto through regulated exchanges. The key is documenting the source of funds (AML/KYC). Anonymous transfers are not possible.
Since February 2022, the EU has introduced a series of sanctions packages against the Russian banking sector. Main restrictions:
Disconnection from SWIFT — Sberbank, VTB, Otkritie, Rossiya, Promsvyazbank, Novikombank, Sovcombank, VEB, and several others (list in Regulation (EU) 2022/328 and subsequent ones)
Limit of €100,000 on deposits from Russian citizens in EU banks (Regulation 833/2014, art. 5b)
Prohibition on accepting deposits over €100,000 from Russian citizens in EU banks (applies since 2022)
Mandatory documentation of the source of funds (AML 6th directive, EU 2018/1673)
Regulation 269/2014 — personal sanctions against individuals on the list (check via EU Sanctions Map)
For Belarusian citizens — a similar regime since 2022
Circumventing sanctions (through proxies, bypassing limits, with forged documents) is punishable by law in the EU. Penalty of up to 5 years in prison + confiscation.
Several banks are NOT included in SWIFT sanctions and allow transfers in EUR/USD to the EU:
Raiffeisenbank (RU) — available, but fees have increased
Monthly withdrawal limit from Russia — $1 million for individuals (for residents of PT)
Check the current status of the bank on the EU Sanctions Map (sanctionsmap.eu). The list changes.

Apostille and sworn translation of documents

Documents from the country of origin for PT must be: 1) with an apostille (for Hague Convention countries) or legalised at the consulate; 2) translated by a sworn translator. Without this, they are not accepted by AIMA, Finanças, IRN, universities.
Apostille = a stamp confirming the authenticity of an official document for use in another country. Established by the Hague Convention of 1961.
Hague Convention countries (most) — apostille applies. Countries outside the convention — consular legalisation is required (longer and more expensive).
What to apostille for moving to PT
Birth certificate — ALWAYS (for NIF, AIMA, citizenship, marriage)
Criminal record certificate — validity 3-6 months, often requires a 'fresh' one.
Diploma + supplement + academic certificate — for nostrification
Passport and residence permit do NOT need to be apostilled — they are internationally recognised by themselves.
Obtain a duplicate certificate from the Civil Registry Office → apostille at the regional Civil Registry Office or Ministry of Justice. 3-30 days, ₽2500-4000.
Ministry of Internal Affairs → apostille through the Ministry of Justice. 2-4 weeks. Can be done remotely via the State Services + consulate, but the consular process is slower.
Federal Service for Supervision in Education and Science → apostille. Or notarised copy + apostille at the Ministry of Justice.
Alternative — legalisation at the consulate (only if apostille is unavailable).

Civil registry — certificates (apostilled at the Ministry of Justice of the Republic of Belarus). Ministry of Internal Affairs — criminal record check (apostille at the Ministry of Justice). Diploma — Ministry of Education of the Republic of Belarus.
The timeframe is usually 2-4 weeks. Expedited services are available for in-person requests in Minsk.

State Registration and Civil Status Acts (ДРАЦС) — certificates + apostille at the Ministry of Justice of Ukraine. Criminal record certificate — Ministry of Internal Affairs → Ministry of Justice. Remote processing is possible via Дія or power of attorney.
In Portugal, there is no institution of 'certified translator' as in some countries. Instead:
Option A — Tradutor Certificado at the Conservatória
The translator makes the translation → certifies it at the Conservatória do Registo Civil. This is the official route.
Option B — Translation by a notary
The notary issues a Termo de Autenticação for the translation.
Option C — Consulate of Portugal in the country of origin
Translation by a consular translator. Convenient if the document is being legalised at the consulate.
Option D — Certified translator in your country
Translation by a certified translator (if such an institution exists, as in Russia/Ukraine) + apostille on the translation — accepted in PT.

Booking at the Portuguese consulate — how to catch a slot

Queues at Portuguese consulates (Moscow, Kyiv, Minsk, Almaty, Istanbul) — 2-6 months waiting time. Slots open unevenly, often at night. Strategies: **check every few hours** (slots appear due to cancellations); **monitor bots** (Telegram bots notify about new slots); **travel to a neighbouring jurisdiction** (Yerevan, Tbilisi, Vilnius — often have more availability); **private advisor** (VFS Global, agencies) — €50-200 but guaranteed slot. Alternative: travel without a visa to PT (visa-free Schengen for 90 days for several countries) and apply for a D visa there.

The PT consulate only accepts residents of the country of accreditation. However, you can apply through the consulate of the country where you are **temporarily located** (tourism, business trip). This is a legal workaround.

🇬🇪 **Tbilisi** — a popular alternative for Russian applicants
Slots appear from cancellations — usually 1-3 per day, randomly
Check **every 2-4 hours** — especially at 9:00, 14:00, 22:00 (when staff process requests)
Less frequently on weekends, but it happens
To book — prepare your NIF and passport in advance, fill in within 3 minutes
⚠️ Community-managed, unofficial — check reviews, do not pay suspicious ones
Notify via push when a slot opens
Usually free for basic functionality, premium for instant-alert €3-10/month

**Unitel, Nexus Portugal**: private agencies with prior appointment for +€100-300
Advantage: guaranteed slot within the agreed timeframe (2-4 weeks)
Disadvantage: deposits, contracts, risk of fraud (check legal status)

How to learn Portuguese — schools, apps, tips

PT-PT (European) differs from BR-PT in pronunciation and partially in vocabulary. Three options: (1) free state PLA courses for residents (150 hours, A2 in 6 months); (2) subsidised IEFP courses for the unemployed; (3) private: Fast Forward, CIAL, CLIC — €350-900 per course. Apps: Practice Portuguese (specialising in PT-PT, best choice), Portuguese With Carla (YouTube), Pimsleur (audio). Duolingo — only BR variant, not the best start for PT-PT.
PT-PT vs PT-BR — what to learn?
The Portuguese language has two main varieties: European Portuguese (PT-PT) and Brazilian (PT-BR). In Portugal, people speak PT-PT.
🗣️ Pronunciation: PT-PT — 'closed' vowels, swallowed syllables ('Lis-bo-a' → 'Lʒbwa'). BR-PT — clearer, more open
📝 Grammar: varies slightly (tu/você, construção com gerundio, estar/ficar)
📚 Vocabulary: bus = autocarro (PT) / ônibus (BR); train = comboio (PT) / trem (BR); you = você (often BR) / vocês (formally PT)
🎬 Films: Brazilian films always have subtitles in PT — PT news are not shown in BR without translation

🎯 **Conclusion: learn PT-PT if you are coming to Portugal.** BR is understood — but for A2 citizenship requirements, PT-PT is needed. BR content (Duolingo, most YouTube) — fine for background exposure, not for exams
150 hours for level A2 (which is required for citizenship)
Free for **all** foreigners with any status (including refugees and non-residents)
Registration: Centro Qualifica at your place of residence (qualifica.gov.pt) or AIMA
Format: evening / weekend classes, 2-3 times a week for 3 hours
The Certificado PLA certificate is recognised by IRN for naturalisation
Employment Institute: Portuguese courses for registered unemployed individuals
Free + bolsa de formação (subsidy, part of IAS), covering transport and meals
Ideal for those who have just arrived and are not yet working
Secondary Schools often offer evening courses "Português para Estrangeiros" as part of PLNM — for a symbolic fee (€10-25 per semester). The teachers are professionals.
Many Juntas organise free conversation clubs "Café Fala Português" — for practice. No certificates are issued, but live practice is valuable.
**CIAL** (Centro de Linguas) — Lisbon, Faro. Classic, €150-600 per month intensive
**CLIC** (Centro Línguas Intercultural) — Lisbon, Porto. €300-800 for 4-6 weeks
**Fast Forward Language Institute** — Lisbon, a good option for digital nomads, group classes €350 / individual €40-60 per hour
**Kiros** — online, €20-40 per hour with a native speaker
**italki / Preply** — online platforms with teachers, €15-35 per hour (look for PT-PT teachers separately)
🎓 **University of Lisbon** and **Coimbra** — summer courses for foreigners, €500-900 for 3-4 weeks intensive, university certificate
Best for PT-PT. Specialises in European Portuguese. Podcasts + grammar + exercises. Subscription ~€10-15/month. Audio-first approach. Good for all levels.

Options: (1) **sea container** 20' (33 m³) — €3,000-5,000 from Moscow/Kiev, 4-8 weeks. 40' — €4,500-7,500, 2 containers per family; (2) **groupage cargo (LCL)** from €600 for 5 m³; (3) **car transporter / furniture van** across Europe — €2,000-4,000 from Poland/Germany; (4) **Mudança within the EU** — specialised movers €800-2,500; (5) **luggage on the plane** — from €50/suitcase extra. Customs: personal belongings exempt from duties when relocating (Isenção por transferência de residência), AIMA certificate + invoice required.
**20' (33 m³)**: about a 2-bedroom apartment — €3,000-5,000 to Lisbon, 4-8 weeks

🛃 Customs — Isenção por Transferência de Residência
When moving to Portugal, you do NOT pay import duties and IVA on personal belongings if you meet the conditions of Regulamento 1186/2009 EU.
✅ You are **really** changing residency (confirmed by residence permit / certificado consular / MNE)
✅ Items — your personal belongings, used for ≥ 6 months before the move
✅ Imported within 12 months after becoming a resident
✅ Will not be sold in the first 12 months after import
❌ Does not apply to: alcohol, tobacco, commercial goods, weapons, large quantities of medicines
💼 Transfer of residency certificate is obtained at the PT consulate of the country of departure BEFORE moving, or in AT PT upon arrival
Complete inventory (detailed list of items with value estimation + photos)
Certificate of transfer of residency (from MNE consulate or AIMA)

Bill of lading (marine waybill) or CMR (road waybill)
If necessary — CITES permits for exotic items, regulations for antiques
Vehicle = separate category ("ISV" = Imposto sobre Veículos)
**Exempt import upon relocation** (Isenção ISV): the vehicle must have been yours for ≥ 6 months before relocation, purchased in the country of departure
Transport: car transport (€1,500-2,500 EU-EU) or RoRo by sea (€1,200-2,000 non-EU → PT)
Registration at IMT: €270 + booking verification + IPO
Alternative: import without Isenção = full ISV (from €2,000 for a small car to €10,000+ for a BMW)

📏 **Measurements** — calculate accurately. Underestimating → you will have to pay an extra €300-500/m³
🧳 **Air Baggage**: take valuable items (documents, laptop, jewellery) with you — containers can get lost or delayed
🐕 **Pets** separately: they cannot be transported with furniture. See the guide ‘pet-import’

Brazilians in Portugal — CPLP and Estatuto de Igualdade

Brazilians in PT: visa-free for 90 days, applying for a residence permit from within through CPLP Mobility. After 3 years of residency — Estatuto de Igualdade (political and economic rights equal to those of Portuguese citizens). After 5 years — citizenship (7 years under the new law, passed on 1 April 2026 and awaiting the signature of President Segura). This is the easiest immigration path to PT of all.
✈️ Direct flights TAP/LATAM/Azul: São Paulo/Rio/Recife → Lisboa/Porto
🛂 Visa-free for 90 days in Schengen with a Brazilian passport

The main route for Brazilians — CPLP Mobility. Process:
Through the official AIMA portal or by calling 217 115 000. Select 'Acordo de Mobilidade CPLP'.
Atestado de residência from Junta de Freguesia (parish council)

Antecedentes Criminais do Brasil (apostille + translation — although Portuguese is understood, translations are often required)
Initial for 1 year. Renewal for 2 years × 2 → total of 5 years.

Political: voting in municipal elections, working in public service (except for senior positions)
Civil: the same civil rights as Portuguese citizens
Basis: Treaty of Friendship, Cooperation and Consultation Brazil-Portugal (2000)

CPLP citizens find it easiest to obtain Portuguese citizenship:
According to the current law (Lei 37/81) — 5 years of legal residency

According to the new law (Decreto AR 17/XVII, re-adopted by parliament on 1 April 2026 after Marcelo's veto and the unconstitutionality of TC Acórdão 1133/2025) — 7 years for CPLP+EU / 10 years for others. Awaiting President Segura's signature
The waiting time for MdI / first residence permit no longer counts towards the residency period (under the new law)
Language A2 — not required (Portuguese is native)
With a Portuguese passport — EU citizenship

Dual citizenship — a nuance. Portugal allows any dual citizenship. The Brazilian Constitution (art. 12, §4) permits dual citizenship in two cases: (1) you were born a Brazilian citizen (nato) and acquire Portuguese citizenship — Brazilian citizenship is automatically retained; (2) you are a naturalised Brazilian and voluntarily acquire another — Brazilian citizenship is lost. If PT is your country of origin and you obtained your Brazilian passport through naturalisation — you must either renounce one or remain solely Brazilian.

Portugal is one of the most LGBTQ-friendly countries in Europe. Same-sex marriage since 2010 (Lei 9/2010), child adoption since 2016, gender change in documents without medical procedures since 2018 (Lei 38/2018), ban on gay conversion therapy since 2024. Asylum for persecuted LGBT refugees. Pride Lisboa and Porto from June to September. Discrimination based on orientation/identity is a criminal offence (up to 5 years imprisonment).
✅ **Civil partnership** (união de facto) for same-sex couples since 2001
✅ Full equality in property rights, inheritance, alimony, divorce
✅ Foreign same-sex marriages — recognised in PT
✅ Same-sex spouse via reagrupamento familiar — same rights as heterosexual
✅ **Reproductive treatment**: lesbian couples since 2016, single women since 2017
✅ Surrogacy: only for medical reasons (not commercial), regardless of sexual orientation
⚠️ Trans people: parental rights complicated if transition occurs after the birth of children — case-by-case in court
✅ **Self-determination** since 2018 (Lei 38/2018) — changing gender in documents WITHOUT medical procedures, WITHOUT court, WITHOUT psychiatric reports
✅ ≥ 16 years: application in Conservatória do Registo Civil with declaration → new documents issued within days
✅ < 16 years: with parental consent + psychologist evaluation
✅ **Gender-affirming surgery**: SNS covers (long waitlist) or privately
✅ ❌ Ban on conversion therapy from 2024 (Lei 23/2024) — up to 5 years imprisonment
✅ **Update of academic, employment, banking records**: automatically with application
**Código Penal art. 240**: discrimination based on sexual orientation, identity, expression — criminal
Hate speech / hate crime: up to 5 years imprisonment + fine
Workplace discrimination: ACT (labour inspection) accepts complaints; civil compensation is possible

👨‍👨‍👧 Same-sex families in schools — no problem, no questions

The USA and Portugal have a double taxation avoidance agreement. However, the USA taxes citizens worldwide (citizenship-based taxation) — FATCA requires PT banks to report. IRS/PT declarations need to be submitted to both countries. FBAR (>$10k in foreign accounts) is mandatory.

The USA is one of the few countries that taxes its citizens worldwide regardless of residency. The Treaty PT-USA agreement from 1994 helps avoid double taxation but does not exempt you from declaring.

Condominium — how contributions, meetings, and tenant rights are structured

Condomínio — mandatory legal entity of all owners in a multi-apartment building (Lei 8/2022 updated the regime). The contribution covers cleaning, lift, insurance, reserve fund. Paid monthly or quarterly. The general meeting approves the budget at least once a year. Without payment — lawsuit through Julgado de Paz (small claims court).

The horizontal property regime is regulated by the Código Civil (arts. 1414–1438-A) and Lei 8/2022 (came into force 10 April 2022). The law updated meeting procedures, digital voting, penalties for late payment.
What is included in the condomínio
Cleaning of stairwells and common areas
Fundo Comum de Reserva — 10% of the monthly contribution by law
Optional expenses (by decision of the assembly)
Average price in Lisbon/Porto for a 70 m² apartment — €30–80/month
Luxury condos (Quinta do Lago, Vale do Lobo) — €300–1000/month
The contribution is proportional to the permilagem (thousandths) of the apartment recorded in the Escritura

The contribution is NOT rent, but an obligation of the owner. Even if the apartment is vacant or rented out — the owner pays. In the rental contract, it can be passed on to the tenant, but the owner is solely responsible to the condomínio.
📅 Annual mandatory — between 1 January and 30 June (according to Lei 8/2022)
📬 Notice — written notification 10 days in advance with the agenda
🧑‍💼 Presided over by the administrador (elected for 1 year, minimum wage or volunteer)
✅ Quorum — 1/2 of the voting rights in the 1st call, no quorum in the 2nd
🗳️ Simple majority for the budget, 2/3 for major works, UNANIMOUS for changing the título constitutivo
📱 Digital voting is allowed if provided for in the regulamento
📝 The ata (minutes) — signed and sent to everyone within 15 days
If you disagree with the decision — there is 60 days to appeal in court. If you miss it — the decision is in force.
Right to vote proportionally to permilagem
Right to see ALL condominium accounts (request from the administrator)

Right to change the administrator by voting

Do not block emergency exits and common areas
Do not change the facade, balconies, window colours — without approval
💰 Delay > 3 months — the administrator has the right to take legal action (Julgado de Paz, fast process)
⚖️ The contribution becomes a **título executivo** — enforcement is possible without a lengthy process
🔒 The non-payer is restricted in voting rights at the assembly
🏠 A chronic non-payer — may lead to property seizure

Warranty on goods — 3 years for new, 18 months for used

Since 2022, **Decreto-Lei** 84/2021 is in effect in Portugal: the warranty on new goods has increased from 2 to 3 years, for used — a minimum of 18 months. Upon discovering a defect (**falta de conformidade**), the seller is obliged to repair or replace the item free of charge. If this is not possible — to reduce the price or refund. Keeping the receipt and UPC code is mandatory.

Decreto-Lei 84/2021 (implemented from Lei 16/2023) transposed EU Directive 2019/771 on the sale of consumer goods. Applies to ALL B2C purchases in Portugal regardless of the country of origin of the goods and the channel (store, online, marketplace).
Used goods: minimum 18 months (the seller can offer more, but not less)
Digital content / subscriptions: 2 years from the date of purchase
Repair / replacement: with the seller, NOT with the manufacturer (this is key)
Free for the buyer — no 'pay for diagnostics' in the first years
The seller often tries to refer you to the manufacturer — this is illegal. The primary obligation lies with the one who sold it.
What is considered a 'defect' (falta de conformidade)
The product does not match the description in the catalogue/on the website
A function claimed by the seller or generally expected does not work
Accessories listed in the package are missing
Software specified in the specifications is not installed
Defects occurring within the warranty period are presumed to be factory defects in the first 2 years (the burden of proof is on the seller)

After 2 years (for new products), the burden shifts to the buyer — but only regarding whether the defect was factory-made and not due to wear and tear. In practice, the official service centre provides an assessment.
1. Repair or replacement (at the buyer's choice)

The seller must carry this out within a 'reasonable time' — usually 30 days. Transportation is at the seller's expense. Providing a loaner is not mandatory, but large chains (Worten, Fnac, MediaMarkt) usually offer a 'loaner' for large appliances.
If repair/replacement is impossible or takes too long — demand a discount proportional to the defect.
Full refund for a serious defect that renders the product unfit for use, or if the defect reoccurs after repair. The seller refunds the money in the same way (card → card).
🧾 Gather evidence: receipt (fatura), serial number, photo/video of the defect
📧 Written complaint to the seller (email is sufficient) describing the defect and the request: 'Reclamação de falta de conformidade nos termos do Decreto-Lei 84/2021'
📋 Livro de Reclamações (complaint book): paper in-store or electronic at `livroreclamacoes.pt` (.gov.pt) — a copy is automatically sent to the regulator
⏰ Allow a reasonable period (15–30 days) for a response
⚖️ If denied/silence — file a complaint with DECO Proteste or CASA (Centro de Arbitragem de Conflitos de Consumo) in your region
🏛️ As a last resort — Julgado de Paz (up to €15,000, quick civil court)

A battery is NOT considered a consumable in the first few years — a defective battery within warranty must be replaced. Apple / Samsung are more likely to recognise a bricked device from updates as a defect.
Worten, Radio Popular, Fnac — large chains prefer quick replacements for small appliances (< €300) without inspection. For refrigerators/washing machines, they send service to your home.
The same 3 years, but in practice — every defect goes through diagnostics at the dealer. For used cars from private sellers, a minimum of 18 months applies (Código Civil).
Decreto-Lei 84/2021 applies only to B2C. When buying from a private seller (OLX, Standvirtual), article 913 of the Código Civil applies — 6 months for 'hidden defects'.
fixed fee for claims up to €15,000
Decreto-Lei n.º 84/2021 — consumer goods sales regime
Cost of living in Portugal 2026 — complete breakdown

For a single person to live comfortably in Lisbon/Porto in 2026: €1,800-2,800/month. In the countryside (Coimbra, Braga, Alentejo): €1,100-1,700. For a family of four in Lisbon: €3,500-5,500. The main expense is rent (40-60% of the budget). Minimum wage in PT from 1 January 2026: €920/month (x14 = €12,880/year).

💰 Minimum for D8 visa: 4× SMN = €3,680/month (income from remote work; SMN — minimum wage €920)

🦷 Dentist (not covered by SNS): €30-150 per appointment, more for complex work

All packages from non-EU (Russia, USA, China, UK post-Brexit, Ukraine until TPD) undergo customs control.
**IVA (VAT) 23%**: applied to ALL packages from non-EU since 1 July 2021 (previously the threshold was <€22)
**Duty**: on goods > **€150** (value + shipping). The rate depends on the TARIC code of the product — 0-17% for most, up to 50% for certain categories of clothing
**Additional CTT charges**: €5 handling fee + €1-3 per additional operation
Total for a package valued at €100: €23 IVA + €5 CTT = **€128** total tax
For a package valued at €200 (value + shipping): 23% IVA + ~12% duty + €5 CTT = ~**€77** tax
🛂 How to go through customs clearance
**Automatically** up to €150: CTT issues IOSS (if the sender is a large retailer like AliExpress/Amazon with IOSS registration) → you only pay for shipping, the tax is already included in the price
**Through CTT Desalfandegamento Online**: if the sender does not have IOSS — you receive an SMS/email with a link for processing

IOSS (Import One-Stop Shop) — a system since 2021 that allows non-EU retailers to collect IVA at the time of purchase. For consumers = hassle-free receipt.
✅ **Amazon.com, AliExpress, eBay, Temu, Shein** — IOSS-registered, IVA included in the price, package passes automatically
❌ **Russian stores, Wildberries before sanctions, private sellers** — without IOSS, customs clearance still applies

CTT = state postal service of Portugal (like 'Russian Post' or USPS). Offices in every city, banking services CTT Bank, parcels, tracking. For AIMA, a Carta Registada com Aviso de Receção (registered mail with acknowledgment of receipt) is often required. Parcels from the EU without customs, from non-EU — declaration + duty if >€150.

Customs clearance for non-EU parcels — how to pay less

As of 1 July 2021, the €22 threshold has been abolished — now any parcel from non-EU is subject to 23% IVA + customs duty (if > €150). Processing — through the recipient: CTT / courier (DHL, FedEx, UPS) send an SMS with a payment link. Payment online by card / MB Way / Multibanco. Customs handling fee CTT ~€1.80, with couriers €10-40. Cheaper through Portal das Alfândegas, but more complicated.
EU Directive 2006/112 (VAT) + Regulation 952/2013 (Customs Code). In PT implemented as — Código do IVA, Código das Alfândegas.
🎁 Gift from individual to individual: exemption up to €45 (if not commercial)
📦 Commercial parcel < €150: only 23% VAT, no duty
📦 Commercial > €150: VAT + customs duty (0–17% based on HS code)
📄 Any goods parcel from non-EU must be declared via IOSS or import
🌍 Exception: special zone Madeira + Azores — reduced VAT (16% / 9%)
🍷 Excise duties (tobacco, alcohol, perfume) — plus VAT
How a parcel passes through CTT
The parcel arrives at the CTT sorting centre in Cabo Ruivo (Lisbon). Customs assesses based on the invoice and CN22/CN23 form.
The recipient receives an SMS + email with a link to the Portal CTT, where the customs status + payment amount is entered.
Methods: card, MB Way, Multibanco, SIBS MB
Payment deadline: 10 days, then returned to sender
Certo pessoal signature — your CC/residence permit scan
CTT will deliver to your home (signature required) or to a Loja CTT (nearest post office). Time after payment — 1–3 days.
Couriers (DHL, FedEx, UPS) — more expensive, but faster

⚠️ A commercial parcel requires an invoice with an HS code
Through Portal Aduaneiro at portaldasfinancas.gov.pt → «Alfândegas» → «Declaração H7» (for parcels < €150).
📝 Fill in H7: product description, CN code (HS), value, country

📬 Deliver at the pickup location (CTT Loja, customs warehouse)
Requires Portuguese and understanding of HS codes. For complex cases — better to leave it to CTT / courier.
Gift from a friend — do I have to pay?
Formally, there is an exemption up to €45, but only if the sender marked «GIFT» on CN22/CN23 and the declared value is < €45. Above that — normal rules apply.
AliExpress / Shein — do they pay IVA?
Large marketplaces after IOSS (since 2021) pay IVA at the point of purchase. If you see the field «VAT included» on the receipt — all good, you don't pay in PT. If not — CTT will notify.
Parcel has not arrived / is lost
CTT — tracking + complaint through Livro de Reclamações (complaints book). Couriers — insurance. Search period 30–60 days.
ePortugal — Importing goods from non-EU countries

In PT, there are approximately 13 national holidays + 1-2 municipal ones. Key dates: New Year's Day (1 January), Carnival (February), Easter (March/April), 25 April (Revolution), 1 May, 10 June (Camões), Santos Populares in June, Assumption (15 August), 5 October (Republic), All Saints' Day (1 November), Restoration (1 December), 8 December, Christmas (25 December). A 'Ponte' (bridge) occurs when a holiday falls on a Tuesday/Thursday, often resulting in a long weekend on the preceding Friday or following Monday.
1 January — Ano Novo (New Year's Day) — Thursday
17 February — Carnaval (Tuesday) — unofficial, but many do not work
25 April — Dia da Liberdade (25 April, Carnation Revolution) — Saturday
1 May — Dia do Trabalhador (Labour Day) — Friday
4 June — Corpo de Deus (Body of Christ) — Thursday
10 June — Dia de Portugal (Day of Portugal, Camões Day, Portuguese identity) — Wednesday
5 October — Implantação da República (Republic Day) — Monday
1 November — Todos os Santos (All Saints) — Sunday
1 December — Restauração da Independência (Restoration of Independence) — Tuesday
24 June (in Porto — São João) and 13 June (in Lisbon — Santo António) — municipal holidays, non-working in the respective cities.
Santos Populares (June) — the main cultural month
Throughout June, the country celebrates the 'popular saints':
13 June — Santo António (Lisbon). Street festivities in Alfama, sardines on the grill. Marchas Populares on 12 June at Avenida da Liberdade
24 June — São João (Porto). Battle with plastic hammers, alcatruz on the head, fireworks over the Douro — legendary
For residents: sardines, Portuguese songs, garlands, parks full of people. A great way to feel the culture.
Pontes — how the Portuguese extend their weekends
‘Ponte’ — a bridge between a holiday and a weekend. If a holiday falls on a Tuesday, many take Monday off → a 4-day weekend.
10 June (Wed) — +Tue or +Thu may be days off
8 December (Tue) — many take 7 December off → 4-day weekend
Public holidays — banks, Finanças, AIMA, schools are closed
Supermarkets (Continente, Lidl) usually operate, limited hours
Restaurants are open, popular tourist spots are operational
Transport operates on a weekend schedule
By law, an employee in PT has:
22 working days of leave per year
Subsídio de férias (holiday pay) — 1 month's salary additionally in July
Subsídio de Natal — 1 month's salary in December
3 days off for a wedding / death of a close relative
Day off on your birthday (if the employer agrees, common practice)
Governo de Portugal — Código do Trabalho (Labour Code)
Diário da República — Lei 7/2009 (Código do Trabalho)

SNS specialists — waiting time 1-12 months. For emergencies — Hospital SNS (Urgência). Private clinics (CUF, Lusíadas, HPA) — no waiting for a fee (~€50-80 for the first appointment).

You arrive → the nurse assesses → assigns a priority colour (red = 0 min, orange = 10 min, yellow = 60 min, green = 120 min, blue = 240 min, white = not urgent, go to Centro).

In a private clinic (CUF, Luz) — no waiting, but €80-150 for the first appointment. Faster if SNS wait is 4+ hours.

How to survive winter in Portugal — heating and humidity

Winter in Portugal is treacherous. Homes are often colder than the street — central heating is NOT available in most houses. Walls are poorly insulated, humidity ~80%. Options: gas water heater, air conditioning with heating, electric heaters, pellet stove. The electricity bill in January can double.
Northern Europe thinks 'Portugal = warm all year round' — this is a myth. Lisbon: +5-15°C, Porto: +2-12°C, north: it can snow.
But the main problem is not the temperature, but: 1) poor insulation of homes (single glazing, thin walls), 2) lack of central heating, 3) high humidity → mould.
If there is a gas contract. Rare in old buildings. Most effective.
Air conditioner with heating (ar condicionado reversível)
Popular. Installation price €1,000-2,000 for a split unit. Efficient for mild cooling (+5°C). At -5°C it works poorly.
Óleo (oil-filled) — €40-80 at Continente/Leroy, safe but slow
Convector — fast, but dries out the air
Electric towel rail (for the bathroom) — locally
The main downside is that electricity in PT is expensive (~€0.18/kWh), bills can be 2-3 times higher in winter
Wood pellet stove. Popular in villages and country houses. Pellets cost €4-7 for a 15 kg bag.
Ventilate rooms in the morning for 10-15 minutes even in frost
Dehumidifier (desumidificador) — €50-150, collects 10-20 litres of water per day
Do not dry laundry indoors → mould
Check windows for condensation = mould in a month
Ventilation in the bathroom (mechanical or natural) is critical
EDP, Iberdrola, Galp, Endesa, Repsol — main ones. Prices comparable in 2026.
For active heating in winter at night — bi-horário is advantageous
Potência contratada — power 1.15 kVA, 3.45, 6.9, etc. Higher power means higher subscription fee. For heaters, 3.45-6.9 is usually sufficient.
🧥 Dress warmer at home than you are used to in the Central Federal District/Belarus
🧊 October is already cold in the evenings — prepare your heater in advance
🏠 When renting — ask about the windows (janelas de vidro duplo = double glazing)
💰 Budget an extra €100-200/month for electricity in winter
🌧️ November to February is the rainiest — prepare good footwear
☀️ On sunny winter days — OPEN the curtains, the house warms up

Junta de Freguesia — what is it and what services does it provide

Junta de Freguesia (local parish council) — a local self-government body, like a 'village council' or 'housing management'. Issues atestado de residência (proof of residence, needed for AIMA/SNS), registers união de facto, addresses local street maintenance issues, organises events for seniors and children.

Portugal is administratively divided into: Distrito → Concelho (municipality, Câmara Municipal) → Freguesia (parish). Junta de Freguesia is the executive authority of the freguesia. In Lisbon, there are 24 freguesias, and in Porto, there are 7.
Junta is the closest to the resident. Usually in the neighbouring building, short queues, human approach.
Atestado de Residência (proof of residence) — a certificate that you live here (for AIMA, SNS, school, bank, embassy)
Atestado de Agregado Familiar — family composition
Atestado de Condição Económica (for social assistance)
Registo de União de Facto — registration of civil partnership
Autenticação de cópias — certification of document copies
Atestado de Incapacidade Económica — for free legal assistance
Recenseamento Eleitoral — inclusion in the electoral roll (for CC holders)
Registo de Cidadão Europeu (CRUE) — in some Juntas
Permissões for noisy works, removal of construction waste

Proof of residence: rental contract / utility bill / IRS with address
2 witnesses-neighbours (Portuguese or CC holders) — BOTH MUST BE PRESENT (some Juntas)

No appointment needed, usually a queue of 10-30 minutes. Open 9:00-17:00, lunch 12:30-14:00.
Issued on the same day, €2-5. Valid for 90 days.
Search on the municipality's website: 'Junta de Freguesia' + your area

In Lisbon: Olivais, Lumiar, Marvila, Alvalade, Campolide, etc.
In Porto: União de Freguesias do Centro Histórico, Aldoar-Foz-Nevogilde, etc.
