Noise and neighbours — what is legal, where to complain

In Portugal, quiet hours are from 23:00 to 07:00 (Decreto-Lei 9/2007, Regulamento Geral do Ruído). Music, drilling, dog barking — everything is regulated. Complaints: first peacefully, then to PSP/GNR, Câmara Municipal, and Inspeção-Geral da Agricultura, do Mar, do Ambiente (IGAMAOT). Fines up to €2,500 for individuals, €24,000 for companies.
🌙 Rest period (período de repouso): 23:00 – 07:00 on regular days
🌙 Weekends and holidays: often extended to 09:00 in municipal regulations
☀️ Daytime hours: noise is permitted, but not 'excessive'
🔨 Renovations: usually 08:00–20:00 on weekdays, 09:00–18:00 on weekends (depends on município)
📏 Maximum level: Lden 55 dB(A), Lnight 45 dB(A) for residential zones
Hours may be tightened by municipal regulamento. Check the Câmara Municipal website of your city in the 'Ambiente' or 'Ruído' section.
During the rest period, it is not allowed. During the day — permitted if it does not exceed dB limits. Measured by PSP with a sound level meter.
Most regulamentos allow 09:00–19:00 on weekdays. On Saturday — limited, Sunday — PROHIBITED (except for emergencies).
Constant barking at night = disturbance. The owner is required to take measures (behavioural training, dog care). Otherwise — a fine and, in extreme cases, forced relocation of the animal.
Must comply with dB limits specified in the device's passport. Installation is sometimes regulated by the condomínio (owners' association) regulations.
Have a licença especial de ruído. Complaints are recorded with ASAE and the municipal 'Fiscalização'. Mass complaints lead to the revocation of the licence.
Are exempt from dB level restrictions (religious activity), but not from quiet hours — they can only ring during the day and on holy days.
Usually, the first attempt resolves 70% of cases. Knock, politely explain. In the condo — through the administrador.
During an incident, if the noise is excessive
They will arrive with a sonómetro, measure
They will prepare an auto de notícia — the basis for a fine
The local officer in the city is PSP, in the village/town — GNR
Written complaint via the municipal site or in person
Municipal Fiscalização (Inspection) will come for measurement
For bars/restaurants/stores with a licence. A copy automatically goes to the regulator ASAE.
If noise caused damage (lack of sleep, health issues, rent decreased) — Julgado de Paz (small claims court) for claims up to €15,000. Evidence — recordings, measurements, medical certificates.
Without a noise licence (bar): immediate suspension of activity
Failure to comply with municipal order: fine + reincidência (recidivism)
📱 Download an app to measure dB (unofficial, but useful to record)
📹 A short video recording with a timestamp — better than 'my neighbour's words'
📋 Write down the date/time of each incident — 5+ episodes are convincing for court
🗣️ Complaint always in Portuguese — English/Russian delays the process
🏢 In a condominium — always through the administrador, ata da reunião — solid evidence
14 days for returns — how it works for online vs shops

For distance purchases (online, by phone, catalogue), you have an unconditional 14 days to return without giving reasons (Decreto-Lei 24/2014 + EU 2011/83). In a physical store, returns are at the seller's discretion, unless there is a defect. Exceptions for online: food products, sealed cosmetics/hygiene, digital content after download, personalised goods.

Distance purchases (online, by phone, by catalogue, at exhibitions outside the store) are covered by Decreto-Lei 24/2014. The right of withdrawal (direito de livre resolução) is valid for 14 calendar days from the moment of receipt of the goods.
No need to explain the reason
Counting starts from the day of physical delivery
For digital services — from the moment of signing the contract
For sets of goods (delivered in parts) — from receipt of the last part
You can return partially unpacked items if you inspected them as in a store
The seller is obliged to inform about the right of withdrawal. If not informed — the period is extended to 12 months.
How to return an online purchase

The form is flexible: email, form on the website, call with recording. The phrase in Portuguese: «Exerço o meu direito de livre resolução nos termos do Decreto-Lei 24/2014». You can use the standard form from Annex B of the law.
The deadline for sending back is 14 days from notification
Return shipping is at the buyer's expense (unless the seller has taken it on themselves)
Keep the tracking number and receipt — this is your proof
The item must be in reasonable packaging, with accessories

The seller is obliged to refund within 14 days from the receipt of notification (they may withhold until the item is received). The method of refund must be the same as the payment. A processing fee cannot be withheld.
When the 14 days DO NOT apply
💧 Sealed hygiene/health products after opening (cosmetics, underwear, contact lenses)
🎟️ Bookings with a fixed date (hotel, flight, concert)
💻 Digital content after download begins, if the buyer has agreed
🏠 Alcoholic beverages with delayed delivery > 30 days
🧾 Goods mixed with others after delivery (fuel, gravel)
Purchase in a physical store without defect — return NOT guaranteed by law. Depends on the store's policy.
🏬 Large chains (Continente, Pingo Doce, Worten, Fnac, H&M, Zara) often give 15–30 days
🧾 Receipt and item in original packaging required
💳 Usually refund to the card (if paid by card) or voucher for the same amount
⚠️ Without a receipt — chances are practically zero
The existence of a '14-day return right' in a physical store is a myth. It's the seller's goodwill or a promotional promise.
Seller ignores — complaint in Livro de Reclamações (paper or `livroreclamacoes.pt`)
Automatic sending of a copy to ASAE or the industry regulator
At the same time — complaint to CASA (regional Centro de Arbitragem de Conflitos de Consumo)
Foreign seller from the EU — European Consumer Centre Portugal (cec.consumidor.pt)
Julgado de Paz (small claims court) for the claim part (if dispute > €200)
CTT / MRW standard, seller can pay for it themselves
Pharmacies in Portugal — Farmácia and Parafarmácia

Farmácia (green cross) — sells prescription and over-the-counter medicines, pharmacist consultation. Parafarmácia — only over-the-counter, skincare cosmetics. With NIF — 37-90% SNS discount on prescriptions. “Farmácia de serviço” — on-call pharmacy at night, operates 24/7 in the area.

The green cross on the sign. INFARMED licence. Only prescription medications are purchased here. The pharmacist provides consultations for free. Usually open from 9:00 to 19:30 + Saturday until 13:00.

In supermarkets, separately. Only over-the-counter products: vitamins, personal care, some OTC medications (ibuprofen, cough syrup). Prices are 10-20% lower than farmácia. Very popular are Auchan Bem-Estar, Celeiro, Wells.
You get the prescription from a doctor (SNS or private). Electronic — you receive an SMS with a code and password. Paper — if issued by a private doctor without SNS connection.
Show the prescription code (SMS) + passport/CC. The pharmacist can see it in the system. Payment with SNS discount (10-90% depending on the medication).
Children under 12 years old - additional discounts
Many medications (OTC) are sold freely. But antibiotics, sleeping pills, and strong painkillers are only available by prescription, unlike in some countries.
Farmácia de Serviço - every night in each district, one pharmacy is on duty 24/7. The list is on the door of any farmácia or on the INFARMED website.
📍 farmacias.pt - website with a list of on-call pharmacies by postal code
📞 112 in case of emergency - they will direct you to the nearest one
💊 After 10 PM, call via intercom - the pharmacist will hand out through the window
💰 There is a surcharge of about €2.50 at night
Parafarmácia or Farmácia: Ibuprofen, Paracetamol (Ben-U-Ron), Bisolvon, Cegripe.
Loratadine, Aerius, Cetirizine - available without a prescription at farmácia.
Only with a prescription. Amoxicillin, Azithromycin.
Sunscreen and skincare — any parafarmácia. Avène, La Roche-Posay, Uriage, Nuxe are popular.
Condoms — everywhere. Birth control pills — with a prescription (but SNS subsidiary is cheap).
Portuguese — basic phrases for the first weeks

European Portuguese differs from Brazilian: different pronunciation (vowel reduction), 'tu' is less common, 'você' is formal. English is understood in tourist areas of Lisbon/Porto, but less reliably in Finanças/AIMA/pharmacies. Learning at least 20-30 phrases BEFORE arrival pays off greatly.
🌅 Bom dia (bon dia) — good morning / day (until 12:00)
🌙 Boa noite (boa noite) — good evening and good night
👋 Até logo (até logo) — see you later
👋 Até amanhã (até amanhã) — see you tomorrow
💝 Com licença (com licença) — excuse me (to pass, to address)
🙏 Faz favor / Por favor (faz favor / por favor) — please
❓ Fala inglês? (fala inglês) — Do you speak English?
☕ A galão — large coffee with milk
☕ Uma meia de leite (úma mɛ́ɪa di lɛ́ɪti) — half milk coffee
🥐 Um pastel de nata (um paʃtɛl di nɐ́ta) — custard tart
🍞 Sem couvert, obrigado — no starter, thank you (when the waiter brings bread that is later charged)
🍽️ A conta, por favor (a kɔ̃ta) — the bill, please
🛍️ Um saco, por favor (um ˈsaku) — a bag, please
🎁 É para presente (ɛ paɾɐ pɾeˈzɛ̃tɨ) — it's for a gift (requesting wrapping)
🧾 Com NIF, por favor (kɔ̃ NIF) — with NIF, please (so that the invoice goes to Faturas.pt for IRS deduction)
📅 Quero marcar uma consulta (ˈkɛɾu maʁˈkaʁ úma kɔ̃ˈsultɐ) — I want to make an appointment
📋 Preciso de um atestado de residência (pɾeˈsizu dɨ ũ atɛʃˈtadu dɨ ʁeziˈdẽsjɐ) — I need a proof of residence
💻 Tem acesso online? — is there online access?
📄 Posso ter o documento em inglês? — can I have the document in English?
🔑 Não consigo entrar (nɐ̃w kɔ̃ˈsigu ẽˈtɾaʁ) — I can't log in (to the portal)
📞 Posso falar em inglês? — can I speak in English?
📧 Pode enviar por email? — can you send it by email?
🏢 Where is the nearest Espaço Cidadão? — где ближайший Espaço Cidadão?
🎫 I have a ticket (тэ́ню у́ма сэ́нья) — у меня талон (в очереди)
🚨 Call the police! (ша́мем а полисия) — вызовите полицию!
🚑 I need an ambulance (преси́зу ди у́ма амбуланси) — мне нужна скорая
🤕 I'm feeling unwell (ищто́у а сентир-ми мал) — мне плохо
💊 Where is the nearest pharmacy? — где ближайшая аптека?
🏥 Where is the hospital? — где больница?
📞 I need help (преси́зу ди ажуда) — мне нужна помощь
💔 I've lost my passport (пэрди у пасапо́рти) — я потерял паспорт
European Portuguese swallows unstressed vowels. 'Desenvolvimento' sounds like 'дзнвлвимэ́нту'. This makes it harder to understand than Brazilian Portuguese.
«ão» (pão — bread) — nasal «a-u». «em» / «ens» — nasal «e». The tilde over the letter is a marker.
Between vowels — «z» (casa = káza). At the beginning / after a consonant — «s» (sol = sól).
LH = «lh» (trabalho), NH = «nh» (senhor). Like the Spanish ll and ñ.
«X» = «sh» (caixa — kaisha) in most cases. But sometimes «ks» (tóxico), «z» (exemplo), «s» (próximo). Remember the exceptions.
⚠️ «Embaraçada» — EMBARRASSED (not embarazada = pregnant as in Spanish)
⚠️ «Bicha» — QUEUE in PT (in Brazil, it’s an insult; be careful)
⚠️ «Rapariga» — GIRL in PT (in Brazil, it has a negative connotation)
📚 Programa PLA (Português Língua de Acolhimento) — free courses from ACM in municipal centres
🏫 CIAL / CLIC / Fast Forward — private schools in major cities
📺 RTP Play — state television for free
💬 Tandem / ConversationExchange — language exchange with natives
🎫 CIPLE A2 exam — for citizenship (see separate guide)
CAPLE — Centro de Avaliação de Português
Waste separation and disposal in Portugal

In Portugal, the EU standard is separate collection. The main colours of the bins are: yellow (ecoponto amarelo) — plastic and metal, blue — paper and cardboard, green — glass, brown — organic (where available), grey/black — general waste. Bulky items and electronics are collected separately, through the municipality. Violations result in fines.

Municipalities (câmaras municipais) are responsible for waste collection in PT through contractors. Payment is included as part of the water bill. The system is based on EU Directive 2008/98/EC and national Lei 102/2020.
The main principle is separate containers (ecopontos) in residential areas. In apartments, you sort at home and take it to the ecoponto near your house.
Colours and what to put in them
❌ DO NOT put dirty pizza boxes, napkins, waxed paper
❌ DO NOT put: cups, light bulbs, mirrors, ceramics — they go in general waste
NOT everywhere — depends on the municipality. Lisbon and Porto — being implemented in some areas
Everything that is NOT suitable for recycling
Batteries — a separate red container (Pilhão) at municipal ecopontos
Also in supermarkets, pharmacies (Continente, Pingo Doce, Worten)
If there is no red bin — see the section 'Special disposal' below

WEEE (used electrical and electronic equipment) — separate containers in stores (Worten, Radio Popular, Fnac) or in municipal Ecocentros. By law, the store is obliged to accept old equipment when purchasing new.
Pilhão — containers in many shops (supermarkets, pharmacies). Required by law.
Oleão — containers in supermarkets and municipalities. Used oil in a plastic bottle. Recycled into biodiesel.
Recycling bins — containers on the streets (orange). Also, H&M/Zara accept old items in exchange for a discount.
Report to the municipality via phone or online form câmara. Usually free 1-2 times a year. Do not leave on the street — fine.
Separate ecocentros. If you are doing renovations yourself — request permission from the câmara, often paid disposal.
Valormed — containers in pharmacies. Accept expired and unwanted medicines.
According to Lei 102/2020 and municipal regulations, fines up to €200,000 for legal entities, for individuals €50-2000.
⚠️ Incorrect sorting — formally a fine, in practice, the bag will not be collected.
⚠️ Large items on the street without a request — fine of €50-250
⚠️ Throwing out rubbish not according to schedule (in some areas there are specific times)
⚠️ Throwing out from a vehicle — up to €750
⚠️ Ecocentros accept citizens with atestado for free; commercial entities pay
🚮 Rinse packaging from leftovers — clean packaging is better recycled
📦 Flatten cardboard boxes — more fits in the bin
🔋 Do not throw batteries in regular rubbish — it is dangerous
🛍️ Reusable shopping bag (saco reutilizável) — since 2021, plastic bags in supermarkets cost 3-10 cents
🏭 Ecocentro — available in every large municipality, you can drop off all unusual items
♻️ Composting on balconies/backyards — increasingly common among residents, municipalities give out composters for free
📱 App “Sociedade Ponto Verde” — explains where to put what
Sociedade Ponto Verde — packaging recycling operator

Plus smaller outlet points in Lisbon (Fashion Clinic, Outlet Amoreiras), Sintra (Sintra Retail Park), Cascais. Best to go after Christmas and at the end of August — final sales.

Vila do Conde The Style Outlets

Tipping is not mandatory but is appreciated: 5-10% in restaurants, rounding up in cafés, €1 for luggage to porters, 5-10% for Uber/taxis. 'Bom dia' / 'Boa tarde' — always greet. 'Obrigado/Obrigada' — thank you (depending on the speaker's gender). At a pedestrian crossing — drivers will stop, but be cautious.
🍽️ Restaurant — 5-10%, rounding up, if the bill is €45 → leave €50
IMPORTANT: ‘gorjeta’ (tips) are usually NOT included in the bill. ‘Iva incluído’ — VAT included, do not confuse.
🤝 Se faz favor / Por favor — please
👋 Até logo — see you later, Até amanhã — see you tomorrow
💝 Com licença — excuse me (to pass by)
When entering a shop / pharmacy — it is essential to greet the seller. When leaving — ‘Obrigado, até logo!’
👨‍👩 When meeting a man with another man — handshake
👩‍👩 Woman and woman, man and woman — 2 kisses on the cheeks (first right, then left). Between friends, acquaintances, in groups
🚶 Pedestrian crossing without traffic lights — the driver MUST yield (and usually will), but watch out
🐕 Dogs are allowed in most outdoor cafes
🚬 Smoking has been prohibited indoors since 2008, even in clubs

🎤 They speak loudly and emotionally — this is normal
❌ Do not compare Portugal with Spain — the Portuguese get offended
❌ Do not speak in Spanish — speak in English, then they will speak in Portuguese

In PT, there are 3 essential services: electricity, water, gas (if connected). Electricity / gas — liberalised (you choose the supplier): EDP, Galp, Iberdrola, Endesa, etc. Water — always municipal (EPAL in Lisbon). Changing suppliers — free and without interruptions. Address transfer of contracts when moving — 5–15 days, without penalty, according to law Decreto-Lei 38/2017.
Electricity and gas in PT have been liberalised since 2015 — anyone can be a supplier, prices are free. Since 2021, a regulated tariff has returned for vulnerable clients (seniors, low income).
💡 Electricity: 20+ suppliers (EDP Comercial, Galp, Iberdrola, Endesa, Goldenergy, Coopérnico, Luzigás, etc.)
💧 Water: municipal (EPAL, SMAS, Águas do Douro, etc.) — cannot choose
📜 Regulator: ERSE (Entidade Reguladora dos Serviços Energéticos)
Average flat of 70 m² for 2 people: 150–250 kWh/month (electricity), 50 m³ of gas in winter. Larger families: 500+ kWh.
3.45 kVA — small flat without high-power appliances
6.90 kVA — 3-bedroom with air conditioning + electric stove

📞 Payment: monthly DD (direct debit) or MB reference
📊 Meter reading — every 2 months, either self-reported or by phone
A new connection requires a Certificado Energético (Energy Certificate) for the building (updated every 10 years). In older buildings, it is often not updated — resolve this with the landlord.
💻 Online at the new supplier's website — takes 5 minutes
📝 Required: NIF, CPE / CUI (on the account), IBAN
📅 Transition is automatic — maximum of 3 weeks
💡 Electricity is NOT interrupted for even a minute

📊 Final bill from the old supplier — after the last reading
Keep the old supplier, new address
Request through the app: **Alteração de morada do contrato** (Change of address of the contract). The tariff and contract number are retained. Duration 5–10 days.
New provider in the new home
The previous tenant left the contract — you close it with an application and immediately set up your own. No delays.
Reactivation needed: €50–100 + technician on site. Duration up to 15 days. If the meter is old — mandatory replacement.
📋 **Livro de Reclamações** (complaints book) — paper or livroreclamacoes.pt
💰 **CTC** (Centro de Arbitragem de Conflitos de Consumo) — at the regional level
🚫 Disconnection of electricity without notice — prohibited; compensation
📅 Delays in reimbursement/credit >30 days — fine for the provider

Offers discounts of 20-50% on transport (MobiCascais), parking, swimming pools, sports clubs, cultural events, and libraries. Chave Móvel Digital (state electronic signature) — activation

CMD — digital signature from the Portuguese government. Without it, you cannot: access the AIMA portal for renewals, Segurança Social Direta (social security online portal), Finanças, sign documents online. It can be activated for free at Loja de Cidadão or using your Cartão de Cidadão (Portuguese citizen card).
Chave Móvel Digital (CMD) — government login + digital signature. Similar to "Gosuslugi" or BankID. Used for accessing AIMA, SNS, Finanças, IRN, court, ePortugal.
Works like a combination of PIN + SMS (or push in the Autenticação.gov app).
Cartão de Cidadão (CC) or passport with residence permit
Foreign phone number for activation is not suitable — a SIM with a PT number is required.
1. Option A — online via CC + reader
If you have a Cartão de Cidadão with PIN and a USB reader (or mobile NFC) — activate at autenticacao.gov.pt without a visit.
1. Option B — in person at Loja de Cidadão / Espaço Cidadão
Book an appointment at eportugal.gov.pt or join the live queue. The operator checks documents and activates CMD on your PT number.
Network PIN 4-8 digits. Used for each authorisation together with the SMS code.
App for iOS / Android. Eliminates SMS codes — uses push instead. Recommended.
Separate option in the profile. Allows signing PDFs without a visit to a notary (for many purposes).
Portal das Finanças (tax portal) — IRS, NIF (Portuguese tax ID)
Qualified electronic signature — Chave Móvel Digital (government electronic signature) and CC

In PT, the 'Assinatura Digital Qualificada (qualified electronic signature)' (QES) operates — an electronic signature with the same legal validity as a handwritten one. Two official tools: Chave Móvel Digital (mobile) and Cartão de Cidadão (Portuguese citizen card) + USB reader/NFC. With QES, you can sign rental contracts, notarial documents, and submit to AIMA/Finanças/IRN without paper.
Electronic signature in the EU is regulated by the eIDAS regulation (Regulation (EU) 910/2014). In PT — Decreto-Lei 12/2021 adapts eIDAS into national legislation.
Three levels of signature under eIDAS:
• Simples (SES) — email, SMS code, etc. Basic evidential strength
• Avançada (AES) — cryptographically unique, linked to the signer
• Qualificada (QES) — AES + QTSP certificate + QSCD device. Legally equivalent to handwritten in ALL EU
🇵🇹 PT provides QES through two official tools: CMD and CC digital
If CMD is activated, the signing function is enabled separately. Go to autenticacao.gov.pt → ‘Ativar Assinatura Digital’ → confirm PIN and SMS. Free of charge.
Open the document on the Autenticação.gov Digital Signature portal
Confirm with SMS code (or push notification via the Autenticação app)
Download the signed PDF — with cryptographic certificate
Anyone can verify the signature at autenticacao.gov.pt/validacao-de-assinaturas — they can see the signer's name, time, and status.
For PT citizens with CC (this option is not available for non-residents — they have a residence permit without a signature chip). A USB reader or NFC phone is required.
Upon first receiving CC, the signing function is often disabled. Activation can be done at any Espaço Cidadão or online via Autenticação.gov with a USB reader.
Download the «Autenticação.gov» desktop app (macOS/Windows/Linux) — the official programme of the Portuguese government.
Insert CC into the reader → open PDF in the Autenticação.gov app → sign → enter CC PIN → done.
PIN Autenticação (access to government portals) — 4 digits
PUK (unlock) — 8 digits, in the CC envelope
Forgot PIN → up to 3 attempts → block → unlock at eportugal.gov.pt with PUK
📄 Contrato de arrendamento (rental agreement) — without appearing before a notary
📋 IRS declaration and other fiscal declarations
🏛️ Requests to AIMA through the online portal
📜 Power of attorney (procuração) with digital signature
💰 Banking operations (some banks accept QES instead of personal presence)
Under eIDAS, a signature from one EU country is recognised in all others. A CMD signature from PT has full legal validity in Germany, Spain, France, Italy, and more.
Verification: a signed PDF can be validated on any EU portal (DSS Validator from EU Commission — dss.nowina.lu).

Outside the EU (Russia, USA, China) — recognition depends on local laws and agreements. In Russia, eIDAS signatures are not automatically recognised — for documents intended for Russia, a separate Russian digital signature is required.
In addition to state CMD and CC, there are commercial quality trust service providers operating in PT. Most do not need them — CMD covers 99% of cases.
List of all QTSPs in the EU — EU Trust List (eidas.ec.europa.eu/efda/browse)
AMA — Agência para a Modernização Administrativa
Drones in Portugal — registration, rules, where to fly

In Portugal, the EU 2019/947 regulations apply: operator registration with ANAC (free) for drones ≥250g or with a camera + theoretical training. You can fly up to 120m in the Open category. You cannot fly over people, within 5 km of airports, or over national parks without permission. Fines of up to €2,000 for violations.
Portugal applies EU regulation 2019/947 through the national agency ANAC (Autoridade Nacional da Aviação Civil). Registration is mandatory for:
Any drone with a camera/sensors (even <250 g)
Any drone ≥ 250 g (with or without a camera)
Registration is NOT required for: toys <250 g without a camera (DJI Neo in its pure form — yes, registration is required, as it has a camera)
1. Create an account on the ANAC Drones Portal
voanouceu.anac.pt → Register operator → CMD / CC / NIF + password. Enter your full name, address, NIF, contact.
The system issues a UAS Operator ID in the format PRT-RP-XXXXXXXX. You must stick this number on the drone (sticker or engraving).
3. Take the online exam (for Open A1/A3)

On the same portal — 40 questions, free of charge, can be retaken. Upon completion — A1/A3 certificate (Open category), valid for 5 years. For A2 (closer to people) — an additional in-person exam for €90.
Mandatory for commercial operators and drones ≥ 20 kg. Recommended for recreational use. Insurers: Fidelidade, Generali, AXA — civil liability up to €750,000, €50-150/year.
Where you can and cannot fly
A1 (C0 <250 g): over people, but not over mass gatherings
A2 (C2 <4 kg): 30 m from people; 5 m in ‘slow mode’
A3 (C3/C4 <25 kg): 150 m from residential/commercial areas, only over uninhabited places
Up to 120 m height AGL (above ground level)
Only during the day and within visual line of sight (VLOS)
Within 5 km of any airport/aerodrome (Lisboa, Porto, Faro, Cascais, Tires, etc.)
In NOTAM/geozone areas — see the map at voanouceu.anac.pt/geozonas
Over national parks and nature reserves (Gerês, Serra da Estrela, Sintra) without written permission from ICNF
Over palaces, castles, churches, UNESCO historical zones
On beaches during the season (June-September) — ICNF + municipalities ban
Over mass gatherings (concerts, football, markets, demonstrations)
In weather conditions: wind >12 m/s, rain, visibility <500m

Lei 61/2025 (came into effect on 23 October 2025) tightened entry rules for CPLP citizens, except for Brazilians: a CPLP visa is now required at the consulate BEFORE entry. The only exception is for Brazilians (visa-free entry for 90 days). Check the current requirements at vistos.mne.gov.pt.

9-digit tax number. Issued by Autoridade Tributária (tax authority) and Aduaneira. Without NIF, you cannot: rent a property on a long-term contract, open an account in a Portuguese bank, sign an employment contract, purchase a SIM on contract, register for SNS, buy a car/property.

Typical scams in Portugal and how to avoid them

Scams targeting newcomers in PT: fake rental ads, fake 'representatives' for NIF, phishing from AIMA/Finanças, SMS attacks with QR codes, ATM theft. Consumer protection in the EU is strong — report to PSP, DECO, Banco de Portugal, CICDR. Never pay upfront for something you haven't seen in person.
One of the most common types. They target foreigners looking for housing from abroad.

Advertisement: good apartment at below-market price. The 'owner' claims to live in the UK/DE/US, asks for a deposit for 'reservation' via Western Union/crypto/bank transfer before viewing. After the transfer — silence.
RULE: never pay for an apartment you haven't seen in person. Always check that the owner holds the property (extract from Conservatória do Registo Predial).
The 'Agent' shows an apartment they have no right to rent — they pass the keys, take a caução (deposit), and disappear. The real owner comes by a week later.
RULE: check the agent's licence in AMI (Associação dos Mediadores Imobiliários, ami.pt). Professional agents have an AMI number, which they indicate in the advertisement.
Substitution on the day of signing
The contract is agreed, but on the day of signing, the 'conditions change' — a higher caução, a shorter term, additional fees.
RULE: read ALL clauses of the contract. Refuse to sign on the spot — take 24 hours to review. By law, you have the right to do so.
Officially, NIF from Finanças is free; a fiscal representative for third-country citizens costs €60-150. Some 'services' ask for €300-500. This is overcharging.
'Get your NIF in an hour' from suspicious websites. The issued NIF may be fake or not registered with AT. Without registration in AT, the number is useless.
CHECK: log into Portal das Finanças (tax portal) with the issued NIF and temporary password — if the login works, the NIF is genuine.

The 'Representative' continues to receive tax correspondence after obtaining your residence permit — and hides it or extorts you. After obtaining the residence permit, the representative must be officially cancelled on the Portal das Finanças.
⚠️ SMS «AIMA: your residence permit has been suspended, click to renew» — phishing. AIMA never sends links via SMS
⚠️ Email «Finanças: you owe XXX €, pay via the link» — phishing. AT sends official letters by CTT mail
⚠️ SMS «your bank: suspicious transaction, please confirm» — phishing. Never click on links from SMS
⚠️ «MB Way: confirm payment with code XXXX» — if you did not initiate it, DO NOT confirm. The scammer has your number + is trying to withdraw money
✅ ALWAYS access the portal officially (via bookmark or Google), not through a link
✅ For the bank — call the number on the back of your card
⚠️ Skimmers (card reading devices) — check the keyboard and card slot before use
⚠️ «Helpers» near Multibanco — never accept help, politely decline
⚠️ «Your card is stuck» — the scammer asks you to enter your PIN again, then takes the card. Never enter your PIN in such a scenario
✅ Cover the keyboard with your hand when entering your PIN
✅ If blocked — call the bank immediately
✅ Withdrawal limits €200-400/day → scam damage is limited
🎫 ‘Lottery’ / ‘Promotion’ on the street — they ask you to sign a petition/survey → distracting you to pickpocket
💰 ‘Drug sales’ in Bairro Alto / Cais do Sodré — often fake and staged by police
🚕 Taxi traps at the airport — fixed fares in central Lisboa ~€15-20; if more — it's a scam. Better to use Uber/Bolt/FreeNow
👛 Pickpockets on tram 28 (Lisboa), metro Baixa-Chiado, Rossio — keep your bag in front of you
🏧 ‘Currency exchange’ at unofficial spots — they always cheat on the rate. Use banks or official exchange boxes
🎭 Fake fado/restaurant hosts on the streets — luring you into an ‘authentic’ place where the bill is 3-5 times higher
📱 Wi-Fi ‘free_airport’ — it may be fake. Use a VPN or mobile data
❌ ‘I’ll exchange Sberbank → Revolut, 5% fee’ on Telegram/Facebook — in 80% of cases, it's a scam
❌ Unverified crypto exchanges — only use regulated exchanges
❌ ‘Family and friends’ transfers to strangers — PayPal/Revolut do not protect you
✅ Official channels: Wise (Transferwise), Revolut, regulated banks
✅ Crypto: use exchanges with KYC (Kraken, Bitstamp, Coinbase)
✅ In case of sanctions — consult a PT lawyer for compliance, not ‘representatives’ in chats
🚨 PSP — 112 (emergency) or the nearest police station. For police report
🌐 Cibercrime — report at cibercrime.mj.pt for online fraud
💰 Banco de Portugal — portal for complaints about banks and financial services
⚖️ Provedor do Cliente — omnibus for complaints about banks, insurance
🏠 Instituto da Habitação (IHRU) — for housing disputes
📧 Portal da Queixa (portaldaqueixa.com) — general portal for public complaints
🛡️ The '24-hour rule': never make major financial decisions on the same day. Always sleep on it
🔍 Check through official registers: AMI (real estate), RAM (vehicles), Conservatória (property), OA (lawyers)
👨‍👩‍👦 Trust verified connections, not chats. A private lawyer or attorney in PT is cheaper than losing €10,000
💼 Document EVERYTHING: emails, SMS, screenshots, photos. In case of court — this is evidence
💳 Do not use your main card for online purchases. Use a secondary card with a limit of €1,000
📱 SIM-swapping protection — PIN on SIM and on operator account

Support for victims of domestic violence

SOS Line: **800 202 148** (free, 24/7, anonymously, multilingual). APAV (Associação Portuguesa de Apoio à Vítima): 116 006. The law Lei 112/2009 recognises domestic violence as a crime — charges can be made even without the victim's consent (procedimento pública). You have the right to: emergency shelter (casa-abrigo, free), legal assistance, medical examination, protective measures (medidas de coação) against the perpetrator, immigration status if your residence permit depended on your partner. Service in 4+ languages.
☎️ **112** — if there is an immediate threat. The police will come and take the perpetrator (medida de coação)
☎️ **800 202 148** — National Social Emergency Line (free, 24/7, anonymously, EN/RU/UK sometimes)
☎️ **GNR / PSP** — local criminal participation (reporting to the police)
🏥 **Hospital emergency** — medical examination + medical-legal report (mandatory for criminal cases). Say that you want to participate; they will call a social worker
💊 **Hospital de São José, Júlio de Matos** in Lisbon / **Hospital de São João** in Porto have GAV (Gabinete de Apoio à Vítima)
Lei 112/2009 (Regulating law on domestic violence) and Código Penal art. 152.

🏛️ **Procedimento pública**: police / prosecution can initiate a case **without the victim's consent**, if they learn about the violence. This means — even if you later 'withdraw' your statement, the case continues.
🛡️ **Restraining measures**: proximity restriction, removal from shared accommodation, prohibition of weapon possession, electronic bracelet monitoring
🏠 **Shelter**: secret-address shelter, free of charge, up to 1 year. Includes food, lawyer, psychologist. Transport provided by APAV / Cruz Vermelha
👨‍👩‍👧 **Children**: remain with the victim parent; temporary custody is decided in court. School is notified, does not disclose address
💰 **Advance compensation**: the state pays compensation until the court decision (Lei 104/2009)

🛂 **Immigration protection**: if your residence permit depended on an abusive partner (reagrupamento familiar) — you receive an **autonomous residence permit** regardless of them (art. 107 Lei 23/2007)

Do not disclose destination — even to close ones (they might reveal it under pressure)

Compensation for cancelled or delayed flights (EU 261/2004)

Regulation EU 261/2004 provides fixed compensation of €250 / €400 / €600 for cancelled flights or delays of ≥ 3 hours, as well as the right to care (food, hotel) for flights from PT or to PT on EU airlines. Claims must be submitted to the airline within 30 days. Refusal / silence — complaint to ANAC (National Civil Aviation Authority). Can also be done through services like AirHelp / Flightright for a 25% fee.
✈️ Flight from an airport in the EU (any carrier) OR to an EU airport on an EU carrier
✋ Airline responsibility (not extraordinary circumstances — lightning, strikes not involving its staff)
Force majeure (storm, earthquake, political strikes) — compensation is not due, but the 'care right' still applies.
🎟️ These are fixed amounts, NOT dependent on the ticket price
🔁 Plus: ticket refund OR rebooking + right to care
Right to care — while you wait
In delays of ≥ 2 hours, the airline is OBLIGED to provide:
🍔 Meals and drinks proportionate to the wait
🔄 If denied — keep the receipts, then reimbursement
Photo of the delay board / screenshot of email
Receipts for food / transfer / hotel (for care compensation)
Witnesses (name, contact details of nearby passengers)
Livro de Reclamações (complaints book) — paper version at the airport
AirHelp, Flightright, ClaimCompass — take a 25–35% commission, but handle everything for you. No-win-no-fee. Good for complex cases or if your time is more valuable.
Julgado de Paz (small claims court) for claims < €15,000. Fee €70. Airlines often settle before court.
Distance ~1550 km → compensation €400 per pax. If the reason is technical — yes. If it's a thunderstorm — no.
No direct flights since 2022. Flight Lisbon → Istanbul (3000 km) → compensation €400. For Istanbul → Moscow — not EU 261, no compensation.
< 1500 km → €250. Plus choice: ticket refund OR rebooking on the next flight.
Legal assistance in Portugal — how to find and where to get it for free

In PT, access to legal assistance is a constitutional right. Free legal assistance (Apoio Judiciário) for low-income individuals through Segurança Social (social security). Fully qualified lawyer, licensed by Ordem dos Advogados. Can represent in any court. Mandatory 5-year education + 18 months internship + exam.
Lawyer with a more limited practice: cannot represent in higher courts. Good for real estate, inheritance, registration. Usually cheaper. Licence — Ordem dos Solicitadores (OSAE).
Cannot represent in court. Provides consultations. Often specialist lawyers (immigration, tax) work in this role for foreigners.
According to Lei 34/2004. Entitled individuals have an income below the threshold. Applies in civil, administrative, and criminal cases.

Livro de Reclamações — how to complain properly

Livro de Reclamações (Complaints Book) — every establishment in PT is required to have it and provide it upon request. The complaint is automatically sent to the regulator (ANAC, ASAE, ERSE, BdP, ANACOM by sector) — this is a powerful tool, unlike Google reviews. Electronic version: livroreclamacoes.pt — submit online, without visit. Response is mandatory within **15 days**. Establishment fines: up to €25,000 for legal entities. Very effective — many malpractices are corrected after a complaint.

According to **DL 156/2005** — every business providing services to consumers is required to have a Livro de Reclamações (a physical book with standard forms or an electronic version) and to provide it upon the customer's first request.
📚 **Physical Livro**: paper forms in triplicate — the original remains in the book, 1 copy for you, 1 copy sent to the regulator.
💻 **Electronic Livro**: available at livroreclamacoes.pt — online submission after a visit.
🚫 **Refusal to provide** — this is a violation, fines of €250-3,750 (individuals), €5,000-25,000 (companies).
⏰ **Response is mandatory within 15 working days** or the business will be fined.

🏢 **Who is required**: restaurants, hotels, banks, insurance companies, telecoms, gyms, medical centres, supermarkets, airlines, taxis — everything except for a few exceptions (government entities have a different system).
🍽️ **Restaurant**: spoiled food, refusal to accept Multibanco / valid means, additional 'service' fees not per contract.
🏨 **Hotel**: not the room that was booked, broken amenities, false advertising.
🏦 **Bank**: misleading presentation of financial products, hidden fees, refusal of service.
📱 **Telecoms (NOS, MEO, Vodafone)**: incorrect bills, non-functioning internet, refusal to recreate a sub-account.
🛡️ **Insurance**: refusal to pay a claim, slow processing, misleading terms.
🚗 **Car rentals**: hidden charges, damage claims after vehicle return.
🛒 **Stores**: refusal to accept returns within 14 days (online), false advertising, defective products.
✈️ **Airlines**: cancellation, delay, baggage issues — separate EU 261/2004 process also applies
⚠️ **For public services**: Livro is not working; use Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman)
Request from the manager: «Can you bring me the Livro de Reclamações?»
Fill it out on the spot: your name, NIF, contact, description of the situation, date, time
**Be specific**: what exactly happened, what you want (refund, apology, fix)
Manager takes the original; copy — for you, copy — will go to the regulator
If the manager refuses to provide it — that is already a violation; note their name, photograph the refusal, continue online
**livroreclamacoes.pt** → find business by NIF / name / address

If using recibos verdes (self-employed): therapy expenses for work-related stress — may be counted as a business expense (consult accountant)
How to avoid waiting in urgência all day

Urgência in PT operates under the Manchester Triage System — a colour is assigned based on the severity of the condition. You will be attended to within 10 minutes (red) — 240 minutes (blue). To reduce waiting time: (1) first contact SNS 24 (**808 24 24 24**) — a doctor will guide you to the appropriate level; (2) use SNS 24 Atendimento Complementar (at night / on weekends — via the app); (3) private urgências (CUF, Lusíadas, Luz) €20-80 per visit; (4) local medical post; (5) check online queue status through the "Tempos de Espera SNS" app.
SNS 24 (**808 24 24 24**) — government health line. Operates 24/7, free of charge. A doctor/nurse will determine what to do over the phone:
🟢 Not urgent → book an appointment with a family doctor at the health centre
🟡 Need help, but not right now → private clinic / non-urgent atendimento
🟠 Urgent, but not emergency → special urgência (paediatric, child, gynaecological)
🔴 Emergency → call INEM (112) or go to urgência
SNS 24 app: chat with a doctor, video call — no waiting for a telephone operator
💡 SNS 24 is often less busy than urgência — 15 minutes for diagnosis
🎨 Manchester Triage System — how you will be assessed
Upon arrival at urgência, you will first be sorted by the enfermeiro triagem — a 3-5 minute questionnaire → a colour will be assigned.
🔴 **Vermelho (red) — EMERGÊNCIA**: treatment **immediate**. Cardiac arrest, stroke, severe injury, loss of consciousness
🟠 **Laranja (orange) — MUITO URGENTE**: within **≤ 10 min**. Severe chest pain, difficulty breathing, major bleeding
🟡 **Amarelo (yellow) — URGENTE**: within **≤ 60 min**. Moderate pain, fever with deterioration, broken limb
🟢 **Verde (green) — POUCO URGENTE**: within **≤ 120 min**. Wound without bleeding, moderate pain, nausea
🔵 **Azul (blue) — NÃO URGENTE**: within **≤ 240 min** (4 hours!). Symptoms ≤ 7 days, mild cold, slight pain
⚪ **Branco** — does not require urgent care (should go to family doctor) — sent home
80% of patients in PT arrive with yellow/green/blue — and wait 2-4 hours. If you are blue — it’s more realistic to book an appointment at centro de saúde or go to a private clinic.
Health centres have dedicated **Atendimento Complementar** for situations that do not require hospitalisation
They operate until 20:00 in some centres, some are 24/7 (Cascais, Almada)
Appointment through SNS 24 (call/app) — arrive at your scheduled slot
In Lisbon: Hospital Beatriz Ângelo (Loures) — usually faster than Santa Maria
Hospital de Cascais (if you live in Cascais/Estoril) — often quieter
Porto: Hospital de São João — 4-6 hours; Hospital de Pedro Hispano (Matosinhos) — sometimes 2-3 hours
📱 **App “Tempos de Espera SNS”** — real waiting times for all hospitals!
💡 Check before heading out — go to a less busy one
Avoid: Sunday evening (everything that hasn't opened over the weekend comes)
Cost: €20-80 per visit (no insurance), €0-15 co-payment with private insurance
Network: **CUF, Lusíadas, Luz, Trofa, Hospital da Misericórdia**
Open 24/7 in large centres, from 8:00 to 24:00 in smaller ones
Prescriptions can be taken directly to the pharmacy (the same Lusíadas Farmácia nearby)
**Pediatric** (children up to 15-18 years): separate departments, usually less crowded. Hospital Dona Estefânia in Lisbon, Maria Pia in Porto
**Gynaecological / Obstetric**: Maternidade Alfredo da Costa (Lisbon), Maternidade Júlio Dinis (Porto)
**Psychiatric**: Hospital Magalhães Lemos (Porto), Hospital Júlio de Matos (Lisbon)
**Dental**: Hospital São José and private clinics (IMD, Dental Family)
📇 **Número de Utente (SNS)** — they will accept you without it, but the process will take longer
💊 **List of current medications** — written or a photo on your phone
💳 **Bank card**: in case they prescribe paid medications (part is covered by SNS, the rest is out-of-pocket)
📚 **Book / charger / food** — waiting can take 2-4 hours
🗣️ **PT dictionary / translator**: 90% of doctors understand EN, but the nursing staff often do not
🔁 **Family doctor** (médico de família) — the main key. Registration at the health centre where you live, free of charge. Each resident with an utente has the right to one
📝 **Rezipência (prescription)**: the family doctor issues it for 3-6 months for chronic conditions — you don’t need to go to urgência every time
📞 **SNS 24 first, always** — they will decide if urgência is needed
💻 **MySNS portal** (mysns.min-saude.pt): view your prescriptions, appointments, test results
🚑 **INEM (112)**: only for life-threatening conditions. False calls are fined
🌙 **Night hours in urgência**: if you can wait until morning — do so. 2-4 am is the most difficult time
INEM — National Institute of Medical Emergency
Divorce in Portugal — procedures and costs

Two paths: **Divórcio por mútuo consentimento** (mutual consent) — at the Conservatória, 1 day, €280, no lawyer needed if there are no conflicts. **Divórcio sem consentimento** (through court) — 3-12 months, lawyer required, ~€1,500-€5,000. The mandatory separation period has been abolished since 2008 (Lei 61/2008). For foreigners — the law of the country of marriage applies; PT court follows PT rules if both are residents. Alimony + division of property + child custody — main points of contention. Online divorce platform since 2022.
✅ **Agreement**: joint plan regarding property, custody, alimony, use of housing
🏛️ **Where**: Conservatória do Registo Civil (any) — no court required
💰 **Cost**: €280 standard + €200 if there are children (acta CPCJ)
🗓️ **Online platform**: divorcio.pt (2022+) for simple cases with filling out
⚖️ **When**: one party disagrees; there are children — contested custody; disputes over property
⏱️ **Duration**: 3-12 months typical, up to 24 months for complex cases

An intermediate step — official separation without full divorce. Retains inheritance rights, status for immigration. Conservatória process. Less popular since 2008 (when mutual divorce was simplified).
📋 Process of mutual divorce — step by step
**Acordo de partilha** — who gets what property
**Acordo de Regulação Parental** (if children): custody, visitation, alimony, decisions
**Casa de morada de família**: who stays, under what conditions, for how long
**Pensão de alimentos para ex-cônjuge** (if financially dependent)
Can be done by oneself or with a lawyer (drafting €200-500)
Pre-nup contract (if applicable) determines property regime — read it!
Documents: CC of both, marriage certificate, children's CCs, agreement papers
**If children** — agreement goes first to CPCJ for approval (3-4 weeks)
Both must be present (or with notarized power of attorney)
Conservadora reviews agreement, asks for confirmation
Notify Finanças about property transfer (for IMT exemption if applicable between former spouses)
CC update — if surname changed, it can be reverted
Children: new joint custody schedule starts
Alimony: starts on agreed date, paid via SS Direta or directly
**Guarda partilhada** (joint custody) — default since 2024 reforms; child living alternately in two households (often for 1-2 weeks)
**Guarda exclusiva**: one parent custody, other — visitation
**Guarda residente**: child resides only with one parent, other — visitation
PT courts generally favour shared custody as ideal
**Until 18** standard; up to 25 if in education
**FGADM (Fundo de Garantia de Alimentos)**: state payment if parent does not pay — up to 1 IAS/month
Major decisions (school, religion, big medical): joint
Travel abroad: BOTH parents must agree (signed authorisation for emigration / passport)
**Property regime defined at the time of marriage**:
- **Comunhão de adquiridos** (default): post-wedding property split 50/50; pre-wedding — separate
🏠 **Family housing**: the court may award one spouse, especially if they are the custodian of the children
🚗 **Car, furniture**: negotiated by themselves (if mutual) or court delegates
🌍 **Foreign assets**: declared in PT court, may be a jurisdictional issue
📊 **IMT exemption**: transferring property between ex-spouses — without IMT (Lei 24/2009)
**Married abroad, both PT residents**: PT court or conservadora
**Mixed nationality**: case can be in PT (if residents) or in the country of marriage
EU regulation 1259/2010 — choice of law for divorce
**Recognition in other countries**: PT divorce + apostille — recognised worldwide (Hague countries)
**Visa implications**: if the residence permit depended on the partner — you receive an autonomous permit (art. 107)
**Brazilian nationals**: under CPLP, often easier — can use Embaixada services
⚠️ **Do not sign without a lawyer** if significant finances / property are involved
⚠️ **Offshore accounts / hidden assets**: if suspected, forensic accountant — €2,000-5,000, court mandates
⚠️ **International custody (one parent is leaving)**: under the Hague Convention, abduction process if without consent
⚠️ **Embezzlement of work**: sometimes a parent stops working to avoid alimony — court may impute income
⚠️ **Mediation**: before courts, mandatory mediation in some cases (Casa de Mediação) — €100-300, can sometimes save a lot
⚠️ **Mental health**: 60% of diverted people experience clinical depression in the first 6 months — get support range
Civil Code — Marriage and Divorce (book IV)

Process to bring spouse, children, parents after obtaining your own residence permit. Submitted through AIMA (portal-rf.aima.gov.pt). Important: from 2024 (Lei 9/2024 + Lei 61/2025 of 23.10.2025) requires a minimum of 2 years of legal residence in PT BEFORE application. AIMA decision period — up to 9 months (was 90 days). Exceptions: holders of Golden Visa, D3 (EU Blue Card), and several other qualified statuses.
Spouse (official marriage or união de facto — cohabitation for ≥ 2 years, requires registration at Junta)
Adult children-students up to 27 years old, financially dependent
Parents of the applicant and spouse (if financial dependency is proven)
Valid residence permit for ≥ 2 years at the time of application (introduced by Lei 9/2024; Lei 61/2025 confirmed)
Exceptions to the 2-year rule: holders of Golden Visa, D3 (highly qualified), EU Blue Card, refugees, researchers with short-term contracts, minor children, spouses with ≥ 15 months of cohabitation
Suitable housing (rental agreement for ≥ 12 months or ownership, area ≥ 30 m² for 1 person + 15 m²/additional)
Stable income — minimum 100% SMN (€920 in 2026) for the applicant + 50% for spouse (€460) + 30% for child (€276/child)
Health insurance for all family members until obtaining SNS
No debts with Finanças and Segurança Social

Lei 9/2024 (came into effect in 2024, confirmed by Lei 61/2025): a minimum of 2 years of legal residence BEFORE applying for reagrupamento. Previously, applications could be submitted immediately after obtaining a residence permit. This is a key change for new residents.
Criminal record certificates for spouse/adult children
Through the specialised portal portal-rf.aima.gov.pt (launched 2024) or in person. Select the type "Reagrupamento Familiar".

Up to 9 months according to Lei 61/2025 (previously "up to 90 days" — before the reform). Realistically for 2026 — 6–12 months. Upon approval, AIMA sends a notification to the consulate of the family’s country of residence.
4. Family obtains visa at the consulate
Based on AIMA's approval. Fee ~€90 per person.
5. Entry and processing of residence permit at AIMA
Within 4 months from the date of visa issuance. Biometrics at AIMA. Card valid for 30-90 days.
Portugal recognises civil partnerships. After 2 years of cohabitation, you can register the união at Junta de Freguesia and use it as a basis for family reunification.
Insufficient housing size is a common reason for refusal. Check before applying.
Expired apostilles or translations without certification. Translations are only accepted from tradutores ajuramentados or certified by the consulate.
In case of refusal, an appeal through the courts is possible, but it takes 6-18 months.
Lei 9/2024 — 2-year residence condition for family reunification
União de facto — civil partnership in Portugal

União de facto — legal status of cohabitation without marriage, recognised after 2 years of cohabitation per Lei 7/2001. Grants most rights of spouses: joint tax filing, inheritance, SNS beneficiary, right to family reunification, spouse's citizenship after 3 years. Registered at the Conservatória do Registo Civil for €10 + 2 witnesses or through a certificate from the Junta de Freguesia. Applicable for both hetero and same-sex couples.
What it is and how it works
União de facto — legal recognition of cohabiting couple. Governed by Lei 7/2001 (with amendments 23/2010 for same-sex). Does not require a ceremony, is not marriage, but grants comparable rights.
💑 Couple of any gender and nationality (hetero + same-sex)
🏠 Shared residence — verifiable by a rental contract for both, utility bills, shared address in Finanças
👫 Both must be legally competent adults
❌ Must not be married to another person
❌ Must not be relatives (direct ancestors, siblings, nieces/nephews)
Deductibility of alimony, medical and educational expenses of the partner
Inheritance — no inheritance tax (Imposto do Selo 10% exempt for união)
Right to survivor's pension (pensão de sobrevivência) upon partner's death
Right to alimony after separation (if dependent partner)
Beneficiary of Segurança Social through partner (if they have NISS)
SNS beneficiary — access to healthcare through resident partner
**Reagrupamento Familiar** through a citizen/resident of PT — applicable to spouses
**Citizenship by união** after 3 years (similar to the marriage route, under the same art. 3 Lei 37/81)
**Visa for partner**: if a PT citizen goes to work abroad, the foreign partner obtains immigration status as a spouse
For EU citizens: an unregistered partner (just cohabiting) has limited rights; registered — full rights
Rental: partner co-signer, right to continue the lease upon the death of the titular
Ownership: joint acquisition without additional taxes
Social housing: the couple is considered an ‘agregado familiar’
Right to prohibit the sale of joint property without consent
Option A — certificate from Junta de Freguesia
At the Junta in your area with: CC/residence permit of both, 2 witnesses (neighbours, friends), atestado de residência
Witnesses under oath confirm that you have lived together for ≥ 2 years
They issue **Certificado de União de Facto** on the day of the visit, ~€10-15
Valid for all government purposes: Finanças, SNS, AIMA, IRN
For complete formalisation, it is entered in the Assento de Nascimento of both
Note: does not grant additional rights beyond the certificado — just a more official record
At Conservatória do Registo Civil with CC + atestado + certificado Junta
Cost ~€10, on the day of the visit

If both declared themselves as 'união de facto' in IRS for 2 consecutive years — AT automatically recognises the união. For most purposes (reagrupamento, citizenship) this is INSUFFICIENT — a certificado Junta is still needed.
Does not require a court procedure (unlike divorce)
Simply ceases by fact — moving, new partner, mutual decision
Certificado de dissolução — to be submitted at Junta / Conservatória, €10
After termination: alimony (if one depended on the other), division of property under common law (not as in marriage)
Important: for taxes / pension — inform Finanças / Seg. Social
Does not automatically terminate if not reported — you may remain as união in documents and this blocks new relationships
🔸 **União**: easier to create/terminate, without ceremony, without judicial divorce
🔸 **Marriage**: more rights — automatic joint property (meação), full inheritance without tax, proxy rights in healthcare
🔸 **For citizenship**: both paths provide a minimum of 3 years; marriage is more predictable for IRN
🔸 **For immigration**: marriage gives slightly stronger status at the border; união sometimes requires additional proof
🔸 **For social**: practically the same — pensão de sobrevivência, SS benefits apply
If you plan to apply for citizenship/reagrupamento — registered união at the Conservatória (not just certificado Junta) is preferable to avoid questions from AIMA/IRN.
IRN — Registo de União de Facto

Portuguese people drink 3-5 coffees a day. Bica (Lisbon) / Cimbalino (Porto) = small espresso for €0.70-1.00. Main variations — meia de leite, galão, abatanado, pingado. Best chains: Nicola, A Brasileira, Fábrica. Coffee chains (Starbucks) are more expensive and less respected.
Café cheio / curto — slightly longer / very short
Pingado — espresso with a drop of milk
Meia de Leite — espresso + milk 50/50, in a cup
Galão — less coffee + more milk, in a glass
Carioca de Limão — lemon peel tea (not coffee, but often ordered)

In the morning — galão or meia de leite with pastel de nata. After lunch — bica (locals consider galão 'children's' coffee). In the evening — espresso is not forbidden, Portuguese people drink coffee at any time.
On the menu, the word 'café' usually means 'espresso'. If you want what is typically called 'coffee' — ask for abatanado.
A Brasileira (Chiado) — historic, statue of Pessoa
Café Martinho da Arcada — the oldest in Lisbon (1782)

Pastelaria — the foundation of daily life. A combination of café, bakery, bar, often serving breakfasts and lunches. Fresh pastries in the morning, pastéis de nata all day, tosta mista (ham and cheese sandwich), bifana (pork), prato do dia at lunch.
Each neighbourhood has its favourite — ask your neighbours.
Ethnic food — where to buy Russian, Georgian, Brazilian products

In Lisbon and Porto, there are shops with CIS products (Славянка, Bazar Russo, Euromercado), Georgian restaurants (Karater Lisboa, Batumi, Adjara), Brazilian supermarkets (Brasa, Brasileirinho). Delivery of 'Russian' products from Spain — Tiendas de Rusia (Madrid), 2-3 days. In Continente / Pingo Doce, there is an 'International corner' with a basic selection of buckwheat, herring, canned fish. Georgian wines — Gartinha, Lidl has a promotion once a year.
**Slavyanka** (Av. Almirante Reis, Arroios) — the largest. Herring, buckwheat, cottage cheese, sour cream, sausages, sweets, Stolichnaya vodka
**Bazar Russo** (Rua Gonçalo Mendes da Maia, Picoas) — smaller, but closer to the centre
**Euromercado** (Amadora) — large with a wide selection, lower prices
**Minimal selection**: at Continente (Arroios, Cascais), Pingo Doce (Saldanha) — ‘International corner’
Typical prices: buckwheat €4-6/kg, herring €8-10/kg, black caviar (imitation) €5-15/jar, sweets €15-25/kg
In Braga, Coimbra — limited selection, sourced from Lisbon/Porto
Quartas de Avila (Lagoa), Lidl/Continente Albufeira have an International corner. There is no full Russian shop — many expats order online from Lisbon with CTT delivery.
**Tiendas de Rusia** (Madrid, Spain) — tiendasdeRusia.es, delivery to PT in 2-3 days, free from €30
**RusKiosk** (Lisbon) — online orders with delivery across PT
**Glovo** — Slavyanka has delivery within a 5-10 km radius
The Georgian diaspora in Portugal is relatively small but active — there are 5-7 restaurants open in Lisbon and Porto.
**Karater** (Príncipe Real) — the most famous, khachapuri, khinkali, pkhali, Kvevri wine, €20-35/person
Spices (khmeli-suneli, utsho-suneli): in Slavanka, eastern shops Martim Moniz
**Georgian wines**: Gartinha (available in shops), Lidl Plus annual Irish promo with Kindzmarauli, Saperavi
Tkemali (plum), Adjika — in online shops + Continente gourmet section
Grushal khachapuri dough: not sold ready-made, needs to be made from scratch
Brazilian diaspora >500k — products and restaurants are everywhere.
**Brasileirinho** (Almada, Porto Barredo) — full range of Brazilian products
**In Continente / Pingo Doce** — significant **«Brasileirinho» corner**: farofa, tapioca, feijão preto, pão de queijo mix, guaraná Antarctica, caju, manioc
**Meats**: picanha, fraldinha in all meat sections
**Restaurants**: Copacabana (Saldanha), Rodízio do Brasil (many locations) — all-you-can-eat churrasco €20-30
**Açaí shops**: Guavaberry, Berri — popularity is growing
🇺🇦 **Ukrainian**: in many Russian shops (borscht set, cheese 'Korolivskyi'). A separate shop 'Ukraina' in Almada
🇮🇳 **Indian**: Martim Moniz / Intendente — ~10 shops (masala, dal, basmati, paneer). Restaurants everywhere
🇨🇳 **Chinese**: Martim Moniz / Chelas — supermarkets China-Plus, spices, sauces, noodles
🇲🇽 **Mexican**: Continente gourmet + Amazon; tortillas are a must-have
🇮🇹 **Italian**: gourmet sections in Continente + specialised (Flavio in Chiado)
🇪🇹 **African** (Cape Verdean, Angolan): Mercado da Galiza, Bairro Alto
🇰🇷 **Korean**: Muru (Bairro Alto), Korean Grocery (Saldanha) — kimchi, soju, ramen
📅 **Festivals**: Festa do Brasil (July), Festival Grátis Tbilisi (varies), Slava in March with Ukrainian products
👨‍👩‍👧 **Martim Moniz** — a multi-ethnic district of Lisbon — products from all continents, best prices
💰 **Prices**: ethnic products are ~30-70% more expensive than in the country of origin (import costs)
🚚 **Large orders** from Spain (Madrid) — often via bus/car-share, saving 20-30%
📱 **Facebook groups**: 'Russian Products in Portugal', 'Georgian Community Portugal' — buying/selling/exchanging
🍷 **Georgian wine at Lidl** — Kindzmarauli and Saperavi are usually available in mid-August, keep an eye on the folheto
Delivery from Tiendas de Rusia from ES
Tiendas de Rusia (ES delivery to PT)
Fado — the soul of Portugal and where to hear it

Fado — a genre of Portuguese music, included in UNESCO Intangible Heritage (2011). 'Fado' means 'fate'. Characteristically melancholic, it expresses saudade (longing, nostalgia). Two styles: Lisboa (female/male) and Coimbra (only male, academic). In Lisbon — Alfama, Mouraria, Bairro Alto. Concert with dinner €30-80.

Fado (from Latin 'fatum' — fate) is a genre that originated in Lisbon in the 1820s, with roots in maritime and port songs, Afro-Brazilian rhythms like lundum, and romances. Instruments: classical guitar (viola) + Portuguese guitar (guitarra portuguesa, mandolin-like with 12 strings).

Themes: unrequited love, the sea, longing for the deceased, nostalgia for the past, poverty, Lisbon. Overall tone — melancholic, but sometimes proud and passionate. Key emotion — saudade (untranslatable: a longing mixed with love).
More well-known. Sung by men and women. Dressed in black (women in mourning dresses). The audience must be quiet, applause is only at the end. Guitarist(s) sit, fadista stands with closed eyes.
Academic, from the university town of Coimbra. Sung ONLY by men — students and graduates, in black cloaks (capas e batinas). More formal. Themes: student life, women, the city, farewells.
👑 Amália Rodrigues (1920-1999) — queen of fado, symbol of Portugal in the 20th century. National pantheon
🎵 Carlos do Carmo (1939-2021) — modernised the genre, recognised worldwide
🎤 Cuca Roseta, Raquel Tavares, Cristina Branco — modern stars
🎸 Ricardo Rocha, Custódio Castelo — legendary guitarra portuguesa
Restaurants with live fado during dinner. Quality varies greatly — from authentic to touristy. Average price — €30-80 with dinner.
Clube de Fado (Alfama) — authentic, frequent stars
Mesa de Frades (Alfama) — in a former chapel with azulejos
Tasca do Chico (Bairro Alto) — small, for fado vadio (amateur)
Senhor Vinho (Lapa) — elite, with legendary fadistas
In small tascas of the Alfama and Mouraria districts, amateurs sing in a circle. More authentic, cheaper, but unpredictable. Places come and go — try Tasca do Jaime, A Baiuca.
Centro Cultural de Belém — current events
In Porto: Café Guarany, Coliseu do Porto
Fado de Coimbra — where to listen
🎓 Fado ao Centro — daily evening concerts in a historic house, €10 with port wine
🎓 á Capella — in a former chapel from the 14th century
🎪 Queima das Fitas (May, 8 days) — the main festival featuring fado
🎤 Students sometimes perform at street concerts during Queima or other events, for free
🤫 SILENCE during singing — do not talk, do not eat noisily, do not photograph with flash
🎵 Pause after each song = applause. DO NOT applaud after verses
🍷 Ordering wine or port wine — is the norm
👔 Smart-casual attire is required, but ultra-formal is not necessary
⏰ Singing usually starts at 21:00-22:00 and lasts for 3-4 hours
💰 The programme often includes dinner and singing — menu prices
🎫 Reservations at popular Casas are essential, especially in season
📝 The price on the door is proforma; the actual bill includes tips

In Alfama (Largo do Chafariz de Dentro 1). A state museum, €5 entry. Collection of instruments, photographs, audio-video archive, temporary exhibitions. Recommended BEFORE attending a live concert — it helps to understand the context.
There are live fado sessions (on schedule), and a shop with recordings.
UNESCO — Fado, Urban Popular Song of Portugal

Football in Portugal is a national religion. “Os Três Grandes” (The Big Three): SL Benfica, FC Porto, Sporting CP. Derby de Lisboa (Benfica vs Sporting) and O Clássico (Benfica vs Porto) are the events of the year. Tickets for regular matches range from €15-60, and for the clássico up to €200. Sóócios (club members) — ~200,000 at Benfica, a world record.

Founded in 1904. The most decorated — 38 championships in Portugal. Stadium Estádio da Luz (64,642 seats) in Lisbon. Colours are red and white. Mascot — eagle Vítor. Anthem — 'Ser Benfiquista'. Over 250,000 socios — Guinness World Record.
Founded in 1893. 30 championships. Stadium Estádio do Dragão (50,033). Colours are blue and white. Mascot — dragon. The only Portuguese club in Clube dos 21 (UEFA). UEFA Champions League 1987, 2004.

Founded in 1906. 21 championships. Stadium Estádio José Alvalade (50,095). Colours are green and white (striped). Mascot — lion. Alma de Leão. The Alcochete academy is one of the best in the world: produced Cristiano Ronaldo, Luís Figo, João Félix, Nani.
🔥 O Clássico — Benfica vs FC Porto. The main match in Portugal. 2-3 times a season (Liga + Taça)
🔥 Derby de Lisboa — Benfica vs Sporting. The capital rivalry. The city is divided red-green
🔥 Derby do Norte / Invicta — FC Porto vs Sporting (less heated, but still a classic)
🔥 Clássico Eterno — Benfica vs Sporting historical feeling
On derby days — crowds in the streets, buses with fans, televisions in all cafes. The official RTP commentator cries with emotion — a tradition.

Main channels: SL Benfica at slbenfica.pt, FC Porto at fcporto.pt, Sporting CP at sporting.pt. Registration is required. Regular Liga Betclic matches — 1-2 weeks in advance. Clássico and Champions League — one month in advance.
Being a sócio gives priority for tickets and discounts of 20-50%. Membership starts from €30/month (Benfica). A must for true fans.
Tickets are resold (with the clubs' consent through their platforms). Other secondary sites — risk of fraud.
Regular Liga match: €15-60 (depending on the sector)
🚇 The metro is packed 1.5-2 hours before. Arrive early.
🍻 Bars near the stadium are chaotic, but fun
🥪 Bifana / cachorro (hot dog) / sardinhas — traditional food
🚫 Flares and fireworks are prohibited, but sometimes appear (fanáticos)
🧣 Scarf (cachecol) — must-have, buy one near the stadium for €10-15
🍺 No alcohol inside the stadium (by law). Available at the bar near the stadium and afterwards
⚠️ Football police PSP are serious, stadium security follows UEFA standards
🎉 After the match — traffic jams of fans. Hard to catch a taxi
Selecção das Quinas (the red-green quinas in the coat of arms). Kit — red and green. Stadiums: Estádio Nacional do Jamor (classic Taça final), Estádio da Luz, Estádio do Dragão.
🌟 Legends: Eusébio, Figo, Rui Costa, Deco, Cristiano Ronaldo
👑 Ronaldo — 5× Ballon d'Or, all-time top scorer for the national team (130+)
📅 2026 World Cup (USA-Canada-Mexico) — Portugal among the favourites
🎫 Tickets for national team matches — through FPF (Federation)

Football in PT — is not just a sport. Club affiliation is passed down from father to son (not chosen). Asking: “Are you from Benfica, Porto or Sporting?” — is normal small talk. 80% of the population supports one of the “Três Grandes”.

Regional clubs: FC Braga, Vitória SC (Guimarães), Rio Ave, Boavista — form the “Primeira Liga”, but have never won the championship (except Boavista in 2001 — the only time it was NOT Benfica/Porto/Sporting).

What to definitely try in Portugal — by region

Portuguese cuisine — region by region. Lisbon: sardines, ameijoas à bulhão pato. Porto: francesinha, tripas. Alentejo: cozido, açorda. North: caldo verde. Madeira: espetadas. Azores: cozido das Furnas. The iconic dish is bacalhau (cod), with '365 recipes'. And pastéis de nata for dessert — everywhere.
🐟 Sardinhas assadas — grilled sardines, season June–September. Feast Santo António (13 June) — sardine day

The European Health Insurance Card (CESD), also known as EHIC, provides access to public healthcare in all EU countries, EEA, Switzerland, and the UK under the same conditions as local residents. Free, valid for 5 years. Issued to residents of PT with Segurança Social (NISS) through SS Direta in 10 minutes. The plastic card arrives by post in 10-14 days, but a temporary certificate can be downloaded as a PDF immediately.

✅ Emergency care in public hospitals (urgência) — according to local conditions

CESD complements, but does not replace travel insurance. For a comfortable trip (repatriation, private clinics, luggage), still purchase a policy for €10-30 from an insurer.

Paediatrician in Portugal — SNS, private, what to expect

In the SNS, each child is assigned a **médico de família** (not necessarily a paediatrician — usually a family doctor). Scheduled check-ups are on the timetable (Saúde Infantil): 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 24 months, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 13, 15, 18 years — free of charge. Specialist paediatricians (pediatria) — via referral from a family doctor or private clinics (Lusíadas, CUF, Luz) €60-120/visit without insurance. Children's emergency: Hospital Dona Estefânia (Lisbon), Hospital de São João Pediatric (Porto). Scheduling: SNS 24 app or through the health centre.

An important difference from Russia/Brazil: in Portugal, children are monitored by a **médico de família** (family doctor) — not by a paediatrician as a separate doctor. Paediatricians are specialists for complex cases.
1. After birth / arrival — at the **Centro de Saúde** in your area of residence
2. Documents: passport / CC of the child, NIF, NISS, birth certificate, atestado de residência
3. They will assign a **Número de Utente** (lifetime)
4. They will assign a médico de família (family doctor) — who will care for the child until 18 years old
5. Automatically enrolled in the Saúde Infantil programme — scheduled visits according to DGS timetable
⚠️ If your health centre has no available doctors — you will be placed on a wait-list. In 2025, approximately 800,000 children in Portugal will be without a family doctor.
Free for utentes, according to the DGS timetable:
🍼 **< 1 year**: 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12 months — weight/height, development, vaccinations, nutrition
🧒 **1-5 years**: 15, 18, 24 months, 3-4-5 years — language, motor skills, vaccinations

PT provides quality pregnancy care through SNS free of charge for residents with utente. 9 mandatory consultations + 3 ultrasounds. Births in public maternity hospitals are free, in private ones €3,500-€8,000. Maternity leave is 120-180 days with full or partial pay. The child is registered at the Conservatória on day 20.
Pregnancy care in SNS — free for all legal residents with Número de Utente (SNS patient number). The quality of medical care is high; most maternity hospitals are public with modern facilities.

Alternative — private clinics (Luz Saúde, CUF, Lusíadas, Trofa Saúde). Pros: individual obstetrician-gynaecologist, comfortable rooms, shorter waiting times. Cons: high cost, sometimes overuse of procedures.
Positive test → appointment with a family doctor at Centro de Saúde (public health clinic) or with a private gynaecologist (€25-100 for the first appointment).
The family doctor refers to the maternity department at Centro. A ‘Processo Clínico’ is opened in SNS24, and a boletim de grávida is issued — a green booklet that records all appointments and tests.

From 23 weeks, you can submit a preliminary application for Subsídio Parental (Parental Allowance) to Segurança Social Direta (online social security portal) so that payments start immediately after birth.
1st: up to 12 weeks — initial, tests, early ultrasound
+ Post-partum: up to 42 days after delivery
Additional tests automatically: TORCH, blood type, HIV, syphilis, hepatitis, urine monthly.

Immediately after birth — basic procedures: Apgar, measurements, first skin-to-skin contact, vitamin K. In PT, the government standard is skin-to-skin contact (contato pele-a-pele) for a minimum of 1 hour.

Private health insurance — how the market works

Private insurance in PT — a supplement to SNS, not a replacement. The main point: quick access to specialists in private clinic networks. The market is regulated by ASF (Autoridade de Supervisão de Seguros). Typical cost for one adult: €25-40/month for a basic plan, €40-80 for average, €80-150 for comprehensive. For a family of four — from €120/month. Key parameters for comparison — annual limit, co-payments, and waiting periods.
How SNS and private insurance complement each other

SNS — a universal system, funded by taxes, free for legal residents. Strong for emergencies, chronic illnesses, hospitalisations. Weak on waiting times for scheduled appointments with specialists — 1–12 months depending on the region and specialty.

Private insurance covers visits to private clinics (largest networks: Luz Saúde, CUF, Lusíadas, HPA, Trofa Saúde). Appointments with specialists usually within 1-3 days. Many services that SNS hardly covers (full dental care, psychotherapy, ophthalmology) are available in private insurance.

As of 2026, approximately 10 insurers specialising in health are licensed and active. ASF publishes a list at asf.com.pt. The largest market shares are held by several insurers, partially affiliated with banking groups:
Medical insurers affiliated with banking groups (Médis / Millennium BCP; Multicare / Fidelidade)
Specialised international: Allianz Care, Cigna Global (for expats)
Reputable insurers for families: Tranquilidade Saúde (Generali), Liberty Saúde
B2B products for employees: Advancecare (through Mercer)
When searching, look for «seguro de saúde» in search. All licensed insurers are listed in the public ASF register.
Plafond anual — annual payout limit (from €10,000 to unlimited)
Co-payment — patient co-pay for each service (€15-35 per appointment — typical)
Período de carência — waiting period before accessing services: usually 30-60 days for outpatient care, 180-365 days for pregnancy, oncology, dentistry
Rede de prestadores — network of clinics: the wider, the better
Dentistry — often a separate module with a limit
International coverage — usually an option for families travelling
Chronic conditions — must be declared; often excluded from coverage
Age surcharges — policy costs 20-50% more after age 60
Through insurance company websites directly or via aggregator brokers. ASF requires that all quotes are complete and comparable. Get at least 3 quotes for comparison.

The medical questionnaire is mandatory. Declare all chronic illnesses, surgeries, and medications taken. Falsehood is grounds for denial of payment in the event of a claim and cancellation of the policy.
Based on the questionnaire, the insurer may: accept standard; accept with a surcharge (agravamento); exclude specific illnesses (exclusões); deny.
Usually online. Payment by card or direct debit. The policy is active from the next day (but a waiting period applies).
The physical card arrives by post, the digital one in the insurer's app. It is shown at the clinic along with your CC/residence permit.
The 'rede convencionada' model is the most convenient: the clinic is part of the insurer's network, you only pay a co-payment, the rest is billed directly.
Out of network — 'reembolso' model: you pay in full, then submit receipts and a request to the insurer, receiving a refund to your account usually within 30 days.

A key point — the contract is governed by the Ley do Contrato de Seguro (Decreto-Lei 72/2008). The insurer is obliged to explain the denial in writing. Appeals are through ASF and Provedor do Cliente.
Do not take out a policy ‘online in a minute’ without reading the **Condições Gerais** — exceptions are often overlooked.
For families with children, check the vaccination and paediatric coverage.
If you are planning a pregnancy, the policy should be arranged at least 10 months in advance.
For travelling expats, a policy with an international component (Allianz, Cigna) may be more advantageous.
If your employer offers B2B insurance, it is usually cheaper than individual plans and without questionnaires.
IRS deduction: insurance contributions are partially deductible (category ‘**despesas de saúde**’).
When changing jobs, maintain your insurance, as the **carência** resets with a new purchase.
ASF — Autoridade de Supervisão de Seguros

Número de utente — personal number in the Portuguese public healthcare system. Grants access to free appointments with a family doctor, hospitalisation, and subsidised prescriptions. It is issued at the Centro de Saúde (public health centre) in your area after obtaining a residence permit and NISS.
Número de utente — a 9-digit identifier in SNS. Without it, appointments are charged as for non-residents.

Social assistance in Portugal — RSI, subsidies, benefits

Portugal offers a wide range of social benefits to residents: **RSI** (rendimento social de inserção) — minimum for incomes <€242/month; **Subsídio de desemprego** (unemployment benefit) — 65% of the average salary for up to 12-30 months; **Subsídio de habitação** (housing subsidy) — up to €200/month; **Tarifa Social Energia** — 33% discount on electricity; **Social Housing** (habitação social) — rent based on income. Requirements: NISS, minimum SS contribution period (depends on the benefit), legal residence.
RSI — basic benefit for individuals/families with income below the minimum. The state guarantees a minimum income (social contract 'you provide work, we provide basic income').
📊 **Amount**: ~€242.30/month for the first adult (2026, linked to SS reference value). +50% for a spouse (~€121), +30% for a child
🧾 **Conditions**: total family income < threshold amount; no significant assets; willingness to work (there is an obligation to seek work through IEFP)
📅 **Duration**: 12 months, renewable each year if conditions are met
👨‍👩‍👧 **For foreigners**: legal residence in PT ≥ 1 year (3 years for non-EU). Exception: refugees, TPD — from the moment of obtaining status
📍 **Application**: SS Direta → 'Prestações' → 'Rendimento Social de Inserção', or at the ISS office
📋 **Obligations**: registration with IEFP as a job seeker, attending mandatory courses/interviews, not refusing 'suitable' work
RSI is incompatible with: subsídio de desemprego, full-time work (>RSI threshold), significant assets (>4× RSI in the bank). Non-compliance with rules = loss for 2 years.

Vaccinations in Portugal — PNV, children, adults, travel

PNV (Plano Nacional de Vacinação) — a government programme, free in SNS for residents with utente. Children receive a set of mandatory vaccinations according to the schedule (0-18 years). For adults: Tdap (tetanus) every 10 years, flu annually for those aged 65+ and risk groups, COVID booster as recommended by DGS. Travel vaccines (malaria, Hep A, yellow fever) — available at private travel medicine centres or CINAP.

For tenants — not mandatory, but recommended (seguro de recheio, €50-150/year). Covers: fire, flooding, theft, civil liability (€50-300k), electronics, repairs. Best insurers 2026: Fidelidade, Generali Tranquilidade, Allianz, Zurich, Liberty. Annual cost: €150-500 for a 70-100 m² apartment depending on coverage.

Recommended if your equipment totals over €3,000

**Capital seguro do recheio** — assess your belongings honestly (do not copy from insurer examples)
