The insurance company sends an appraiser (perito) within 3-7 days. I recommend being at home to show everything and discuss the details. You can invite an independent appraiser (perito) ( €100-300) if the insurance undervalues.
**Disputes**: Provedor do Cliente of the insurance company → Provedor dos Segurados (ASF) → court
Free assistance: DECO Proteste (consumer protection) for disputes
📝 **Inventory with photos**: photograph all serious items (electronics, jewellery, art) with serial numbers. For a claim — gold standard
💾 **Digital backup**: save the inventory in the cloud (not on the device)
🔒 **Panic button / alarm system**: 10-15% discount from the insurance
🎂 **Update date**: annually review — your belongings have changed (bought new equipment, moved)

🌊 **Natural disasters**: quake coverage is cheap in PT (little active zone), but if your region is at risk (Algarve, Azores) — add it
🏠 **Alojamento Local (AL)**: if you are renting out — separate commercial insurance (many standard policies exclude it)
💰 **Annual vs monthly payment**: annual is cheaper by 3-5%
🔄 **Switch insurer**: compare every year; 10-20% savings are typical

Long-term rental in Portugal — legal framework and process

Long-term rental (arrendamento habitacional) is regulated by the New Urban Lease Regime (NRAU, Lei 6/2006 with numerous amendments). The minimum contract duration is 1 year unless otherwise specified. Deposit (caução) is typically 1-3 months' rent. A guarantor (fiador) or prepayment of 3-12 months is often required. The contract must be registered with Finanças by the owner within 30 days.

Key documents: Código Civil (articles on rental), Lei 6/2006 (NRAU — Novo Regime do Arrendamento Urbano), numerous Leis and Decretos-Lei with amendments. Important 2024: Lei 56/2023 «Mais Habitação» PARTIALLY REPEALS Decreto-Lei 81/2024 (November 2024, «Construir Portugal»): moratorium on new Alojamento Local (AL) licences lifted, CAE (forced rental of vacant apartments) abolished, forced expiration of AL 2030 removed, IRS reform on rental income.

Key principles: tenant protection (eviction only through court), major repairs — owner's responsibility, rent indexing — no more than once a year and not above the official INE coefficient (in 2026: **2.16%**, INE publication October 2025).
Caução under Lei 13/2019: maximum of 2 months' rent + 1 month in advance. More than this is illegal.
Contrato de arrendamento habitacional — residential lease agreement
Caução — security deposit, under Lei 13/2019 maximum 2 months' rent + 1 month in advance, returned upon departure
Fiador — guarantor: a resident individual in PT with stable income, who secures the tenant's obligations with their property
Seguro de fiança — insurance company replaces the fiador, ~5-7% of annual rent
IMI — municipal property tax, legally paid by the owner
Condomínio — fee for maintaining common areas of the building (usually not included in the rent)
Recibo de renda — monthly receipt from the owner with NIF of the parties, basis for tax declaration
Prices have risen significantly from 2022 to 2025. According to INE and Banco de Portugal statistics:
Algarve seasonally: T1 €700-€1,500 (winter), up to x2 in summer
Symbols: T0 = studio, T1 = 1 bedroom + living room, T2 = 2 bedrooms + living room, etc.
In Portugal, there are several categories of housing search channels:
Online portal aggregators (largest: Idealista, Imovirtual, Casa Sapo, Supercasa) — the main channel, hundreds of thousands of listings
OLX Imóveis — many listings directly from owners (meno fees)
Real estate agencies (imobiliárias) — major networks: RE/MAX, Century 21, ERA, Engel & Völkers. Commission is 1 month's rent, often paid by the owner.
Local Facebook groups (by cities and neighbourhoods)
Medium-term rental (6-12 months) for expats: platforms without fiador, but 20-40% more expensive
Junta de Freguesia (parish council) — sometimes has social housing programmes

Typical scam scheme: an advertisement with photos of a nice apartment, the 'owner' is abroad, asking for a deposit to 'reserve' without viewing. NEVER pay for an apartment you haven't seen in person or through a reliable representative.
The rental process — step by step
Prepare proof of income: employment contract, payslips for 3 months, bank statement, or IRS declaration
Find a fiador (if available) — their CC + income
If no fiador: budget for a deposit of 3-12 months or seguro de fiança

Create a shortlist. During the first viewing check: wall and window dampness (white marks in corners = mould), heating (critical in winter — Portugal is one of the worst in the EU for housing insulation), hot water (gas water heater esquentador or termoacumulador boiler), noise from the street and neighbours, water pressure.

Discussed points: duration (12/24/36 months), amount of caução (security deposit), who pays IMI and condomínio (owners' association), furniture (mobilado (furnished) / mobilado parcialmente / sem mobília (unfurnished)), pet restrictions, rent indexing, preliminary obligation of the owner to rectify noted defects before move-in.

At least in 2 copies. Mandatory elements according to Lei 6/2006: names and NIF (Portuguese tax ID) of the parties, detailed address of the property, matricial number (artigo matricial) and freguesia (parish council), duration, rent amount and indexing method, amount of caução, start date.
Prepare a condition report (inventário) — a list of existing furniture, condition of walls, floors, plumbing. Photograph everything. This will protect you when returning the caução.

Within 30 days, the owner is required to register the contract on the Portal das Finanças (tax authority online portal). They receive a comprovativo (confirmation), and a copy is given to you. Without registration: you cannot use the contract for atestado de residência (proof of residence), for AIMA, or for rent deduction in IRS (personal income tax).
If the owner refuses to register — this is a serious red flag (possible tax evasion). Insist or choose another apartment.
Newly arrived foreigners often do not have access to a fiador (guarantor). Options:
Prepayment of 3-12 months in advance — a common practice for international tenants
Seguro de fiança — an insurance company guarantees obligations, costing 5-7% of annual rent
Medium-term rental (6-12 months) through intermediary services — usually without fiador, but 20-40% more expensive
Social housing fund (for certain categories) — through the municipality
Room rental (shared accommodation) — often a simplified process
Peaceful possession of the apartment for the duration of the contract
Protection against arbitrary rent increases — only once a year and not above the INE coefficient
Protection against eviction — only by court order, excluding cases of non-payment for 2+ months or grave violations
Major repairs — at the owner's expense (roof, electrical wiring, structural defects)
Return of the caução (security deposit) within 30 days after the end of the contract (minus documented damages)
IRS deduction for rent — 15% up to €700–800/year (threshold raised in OE 2024/25; check the exact amount at portaldasfinancas.gov.pt)
Timely payment of rent (usually by the 8th of the month)
Payment of utilities (electricity, gas, water — bills go directly to you as the tenant)
Ongoing maintenance (cosmetic, changing light bulbs, minor bricolage)
Conditions for maintaining the apartment — keeping it clean, without damage
Notification to the owner 120 days in advance if you wish to terminate the contract (the period varies by type)
Non-refundable **caução** — written complaint to the owner, then to **Balcão Nacional do Arrendamento** (BNA) or **Julgados de Paz**
Problems with technical condition — request by mail with notification, photos, deadlines for resolution
Noise and neighbours — complaint to **Junta de Freguesia**, police (112 for loud nights)
Unwanted rent increase — contest at BNA; coefficient above **INE** is illegal
Eviction before term — free legal aid through **Ordem dos Advogados** (if low income) or commercial **advogado**
**DECO Proteste** — consumer rights protection, provides advice on rental contracts
both parties — mandatory in the contract
their CC, NIF, proof of income

Portal das Finanças — Registo de Contrato de Arrendamento (rental contract)

Living in an apartment building — neighbours, noise, complaints

Portugal — a country of quiet daily protocol, but neighbours can be different. Noise rules: Regulamento Geral do Ruído (DL 9/2007) — quiet period 22:00-08:00 (in some regions 23:00-07:00). Where to complain: GNR/PSP (one-off) → Câmara Municipal (persistent violation) → tribunal (if everything else fails). Noise fines: €200-€2500 for individuals. In a condominium — the general assembly adopts internal rules, assembleia at least once a year. Mandatory Fundo Comum de Reserva.
🔊 Noise Law — Regulamento Geral do Ruído
📏 **Limit**: L_den ≤ 55 dB in residential areas, L_night ≤ 45 dB
🎵 Music in the apartment from 08:00-20:00 — within reason. In the evening — quieter. At night — should not be heard through the wall
Loud music / TV audible in neighbouring apartments after 22:00
Construction / renovation during non-allowed hours (usually 09:00-19:00 Mon-Fri, 10:00-18:00 Sat, prohibited on Sundays and holidays)
Cries, arguments, baby crying (children are a reasonable exception in court)
Dog barking on the balcony (owner is responsible)
Water noise in the shower after 11:00 PM (less common, but complaints can occur in old buildings)
Be friendly — this is appreciated in PT
Write a note in the mailbox or in person
Convince that the problem is not personal, but for the common good
📝 80% of cases are resolved without further steps
If there is an administrador — send an official letter (email is sufficient)
The administrator will call the offender to the assembleia condomínio
Resolution: written warning → fine from the common budget
If the administrator ignores — the next step
Step 3: GNR / PSP — call the police
For a **one-off** problem (loud party right now) — GNR (regions) or PSP (cities)
**112** — only if there is no other number / aggression
Local PSP / GNR number — google "posto territorial + neighbourhood name"
The police will arrive, ask to lower it — usually resolves on the spot
📋 Get a reference for the request — for subsequent steps
Submission: online on the municipality's website or in person at attendance
**Noise measurement**: a municipal technician will come and measure the sound level
If exceeded — a fine is issued to the offender (€200-2500 for individuals, up to €25,000 for companies)
If everything else has failed and there are damages (you can't sleep, health issues)
Claim for damages through tribunal civil
Rarely applied for noise — more for construction / serious cases
In Portugal, Lei 8/2022 regulates propriedade horizontal — each multi-apartment building is a condomínio.
**Assembleia de condóminos** — meeting of all owners, voting on quotas (share)
**Administrador** — manager (elected owner or hired company), mandatory
**Regulamento interno** — house rules (pets, balconies, noise, repairs)
**Fundo Comum de Reserva** — 10% of the monthly fee goes into a reserve for major repairs (roof, lift)
**Ordinária**: annually between 1 January and 30 June — budget approval, elections
**Extraordinária**: at the request of 1/4 of owners or the administrator
Voting: by apartment size (permilagens). 1 apartment ≠ 1 vote
Decisions are made by majority of permilagens; some (major renovations) require 2/3
Typical condomínio: **€15-80/month** for a 70-100 m² apartment
Factors: building size, presence of lift, pool, security, garden
Covers: cleaning, lighting of common areas, lift maintenance, building insurance, administration, Fundo Reserva
The owner is obliged to pay, the tenant only if stated in the contract (usually the owner pays)
Debt: **administrative** debt → court for rent recovery; legal lien on the apartment
60 days to appeal the decision of the assembleia
Fines for regulamento conditions: from €50-500 for violations
Major changes to the apartment (breaking walls): assembleia approval required
Balcony: cannot hang laundry outside (in some condomínios), install air conditioning (without approval)
Pets: if regulamento prohibits — not allowed; if it allows — there are rules (leash in common areas)
Columns, party — often prohibited after 10:00 PM
🏠 If you are a tenant — your rights
**Peaceful enjoyment**: Lei 6/2006 (NRAU) — the owner guarantees peaceful use
If neighbours disturb you and the owner does not help → you can demand a rent reduction or exit the contract without penalty
**Renovations by neighbours**: the owner must notify you 8 days in advance of planned works
**Common areas**: you have the right to use them equally with the owners (garden, pool, etc.)
**Assembleia**: you do not vote, but you can attend and speak
⚠️ **EV charging**: you have the right to install it in your parking space (Lei 39/2021) — the owner cannot refuse, only set conditions
**Neighbour's dog barks all day** → Câmara municipal + municipal veterinarian (possible seizure under welfare conditions)
**Neighbours smoke on the balcony, smoke enters your apartment** → not a direct violation (smoking on a private balcony is allowed); but if the condomínio has prohibited it — fine
**Renovation below you has been going on for more than 3 months** → check permits at the municipality, often illegal
**Neighbouring hostel / short-term rental — constant noise** → AL (Alojamento Local) has special rules; complaint to Câmara + ASAE; in some areas (Bairro Alto, Baixa Lisbon) AL is restricted
**Leak from the ceiling**: notify the neighbour in writing, then the condomínio, then the insurance
**Aggressive neighbour**: PSP 112 in case of physical threat; Provedor de Justiça in case of repeated conflict
Portuguese A2 — free courses, school programmes and CIPLE exam

A level A2 certificate is only required for naturalisation after 5 years (under the new law 2026 — 7 years for CPLP+EU / 10 years for others) of residence. For marriage to a Portuguese citizen, união de facto, and family reunification — it is NOT required. The cheapest options are state PLA courses (0 €, 150 hours) or IEFP programmes for registered unemployed (0 € + scholarship). Public adult schools — enrolment for about €20/semester. The CIPLE exam — €85 (2026), with monthly national sessions + international ones in May/July/November. If you do not wish to take the exam — a certificate of completion of 150 hours of PLA is sufficient.
When is the certificate required and when is it not
Level A2 of Portuguese is only needed in a few cases:
✅ Naturalisation after 5 years of residence (under the new law 2026 — 7 for CPLP/EU or 10 for others; the law was passed by parliament on 1 April 2026, awaiting the signature of President Segura)
✅ For admission to certain IEFP programmes and state colleges
When the certificate is NOT required:
❌ Citizenship through marriage to a Portuguese citizen or through união de facto — Lei 37/81 does not require language
❌ Citizenship by descent (grandfather/grandmother being Portuguese) — does not require
❌ Family reunification (reagrupamento familiar) with a resident of PT — AIMA does not ask for language
❌ Renewal of residence permit — AIMA does not require
❌ For CPLP citizens (Brazil, Angola, Cape Verde, etc.) during naturalisation — Portuguese is considered native
Free and paid (with scholarship) courses — the cheapest way
1. IEFP — job centre: course + scholarship (for registered unemployed)

The Institute for Employment and Professional Training (IEFP, iefp.pt) organises Portuguese courses for foreign residents. If you are registered with IEFP as a job seeker — the course is not only free but also includes a subsidy (bolsa de formação): covering transport, meals, and sometimes a scholarship.
You can register at the nearest Centro de Emprego (list available at iefp.pt)
Required: NIF, NISS, residence permit, proof of address
Format: group classes for 4–6 months, several times a week
Scholarship (bolsa de formação): partially IAS 2026 (€537.13) — usually 50–80% of IAS depending on the programme. Check with the Centro de Emprego
Transport (subsídio de transporte): based on actual costs
Upon completion — certificate of ≥ 150 hours, recognised for naturalisation
If employed during the course, the scholarship remains until the end (partially)

This is literally the best option: you learn the language, receive money, and they pay for your meals + transport. It is suitable even for those who are not actively seeking work — just being registered is enough.

The state programme 'Portuguese as a Language of Reception' is organised by Agência para a Integração, Migrações e Asilo (AIMA, formerly ACM) in collaboration with public schools. Completely free for all foreigners with any status (including refugees).
150 hours for level A2 (sufficient for naturalisation)
Register through Centro Qualifica at your place of residence (list available at qualifica.gov.pt) or through AIMA
Format: evening or weekend classes (usually 2-3 times a week for 3 hours)
Upon completion — Certificado PLA, recognised by IRN for naturalisation on par with CIPLE
3. Public schools for adults (Escola Secundária) — symbolic registration
Many state Escolas Secundárias offer evening courses for adults, including Português para Estrangeiros as part of PLNM (Português Língua Não Materna).
Registration through the school (matrícula) — usually around €10–25 per semester
The course itself is free (state education)
Format: full classes with a teacher, certificate from the school
List of schools with PLNM: on the municipality's website under Educação
4. Junta de Freguesia (parish council) — amateur courses
Some Juntas de Freguesia organise free conversation courses with volunteers. No certificate is provided, but it's excellent for conversational practice and exam preparation. Ask in your area.
Tandem / HelloTalk — practice with native speakers for free
Audio: Said No One Ever Podcast, Portuguese Lab
6. Private language schools (the most expensive option)
Only if you need maximum speed and a personalised approach. Starting from €300 for intensive courses, full A1→A2 course ~€700–€1,200. Notable schools: CIAL, CLIC, Fast Forward.
CIPLE exam — if you are going through CAPLE
CIPLE (Certificado Inicial de Português Língua Estrangeira) — A2 level exam from CAPLE (Universidade de Lisboa). Required if you don't have a certificate from courses (PLA, IEFP or school).
4 texts with questions (multiple choice + short answers). A short letter (70–90 words): email, note, greeting.
4 audio: dialogue, announcement, instruction, conversation. Questions with multiple-choice answers.
Individually or in pairs. 3 tasks: self-introduction, role play, picture description/conversation.
Passing score — 55% of total points. Certificate is issued 6–8 weeks after the exam, valid indefinitely.
📅 Sessions 2026: monthly national CIPLE-P (February–November excluding August) + international in May/July/November. Current calendar at caple.letras.ulisboa.pt
💻 Online registration on the CAPLE website (email required)
🏢 Centres in PT: Lisbon, Porto, Coimbra, Braga, Faro; abroad — Instituto Camões in capitals
💶 Cost: €85 for 2026 (up to €95 in some international centres). Payment by card or MB reference
📧 Convocatória (invitation with exact time and room) arrives one week in advance
📄 Results — in the CAPLE personal account within 6–8 weeks; PDF certificate can be downloaded there
📚 Textbooks: “Português XXI — Nível A2” (Lidel), “Passaporte Para Português A2” (Lidel), “Aprender Português” (Texto Editora)
📝 Practice tests: CAPLE publishes Exemplos de Provas on caple.letras.ulisboa.pt — 5 past sets for free
🎧 Listening podcasts: “Practice Portuguese”, “Said No One Ever”, “Portuguesepod101”
🎬 Portuguese series with subtitles: RTP Play (free, rtp.pt), Netflix PT
🗣️ Conversational practice: meetings with a tandem partner, 'café fala português' at Junta de Freguesia
📅 100–150 hours of classes are sufficient at the beginner level. If you already have A1, 50–80 hours
IEFP — course + scholarship for registered individuals
Private school (if you need to learn quickly)
CAPLE — Centro de Avaliação de Português Língua Estrangeira

Available to grandchildren/great-grandchildren of Portuguese citizens, if the connection is confirmed. Grandparent is Portuguese + proof of connection = right to citizenship. For grandchildren — automatic at birth + registration. For great-grandchildren — a more complex process.
Child of a Portuguese citizen (any generation)
If one of the parents is Portuguese (acquired citizenship by the time of the child's birth) — the child automatically has the right to Portuguese citizenship. Registration at the Conservatória.

If the grandmother or grandfather is a citizen of Portugal (and this connection is documented), the grandchild can apply for citizenship. Requires 'connection to the Portuguese community' — A2 language or cultural connection.
Possible, but more difficult — requires stronger proof of connection (residence in PT, language proficiency, family roots).
Parent's birth certificate (through which kinship is established)
Ancestor's citizenship certificate (entry in Conservatória or passport)
Absence of military service in an opposing country (rare requirement)
The chain of certificates must be continuous. If the ancestor lost citizenship, or records are lost — the process is more complicated.
Apostille + sworn translation of all certificates. For records from Portuguese archives — request through Arquivo Nacional.
On the IRN website or by requesting at Conservatória — ensure that the record exists.
Except for children of Portuguese nationals — they are not required to.
Conservatória dos Registos Centrais (Lisbon) or the consulate.
Sephardic Jews — regime at risk of cancellation

An accelerated route for Sephardic Jews (naturalisation without long-term residence upon proof of kinship) was introduced by Lei 43/2013 (through Decreto-Lei 30-A/2015 — Nationality Regulation) and was in effect for nearly 10 years. From 1 September 2022, the rules were tightened by Lei 2/2022 — requiring proof of a real connection to Portugal (residence, economic/cultural ties). After this, the number of applications sharply declined.

Decreto AR 17/XVII (re-adopted by Parliament on 01.04.2026) cancels this route. HOWEVER: due to Marcelo's December veto + TC Acórdão 1133/2025 + the expected decision from President Segura — as of 22.04.2026, the law has NOT yet been promulgated. This means the Sephardic route technically still exists in the current text of Lei 37/81 under the regime of Lei 2/2022. Once Segura signs it — the route will close.
If you plan to apply — do so BEFORE the promulgation of the new law. After cancellation, it will be practically impossible to restore the route.
IRN — Nationality by effect of will or origin
Lei 2/2022 — tightening of the Sephardic route
Citizenship of Portugal through marriage or de facto union

Spouses of Portuguese citizens can apply for citizenship after 3 years of marriage (or 3 years of registered união de facto). A CIPLE language certificate is formally NOT required for this route. However, up to 6 years of marriage, the IRN (Instituto dos Registos e Notariado) / Ministério Público may request proof of 'ligação efetiva à comunidade nacional' (effective connection) — language informally helps. It is not necessary to reside in PT — one can live abroad with a Portuguese spouse. The new law (passed on 1 April 2026, awaiting the signature of President Seguro) will increase the marriage duration to 5 years and strengthen the requirement for actual cohabitation.

Decreto AR 17/XVII, re-adopted by Parliament on 1 April 2026 after a veto by Marcelo (December 2025) and unconstitutionality by TC (Acórdão 1133/2025). It proposes to extend the marriage duration from 3 to 5 years, require proof of actual cohabitation (mandatory cohabitation proof), and raise the threshold for presumption of 'effective connections' from 6 to 7–8 years. Awaiting the signature of President Seguro (PS, in office since January 2026). Until promulgation, the rules of Lei 37/81 (3 years) apply.
The process is the same for all countries

Unlike naturalisation (after 5/7/10 years of residence), the marriage or união de facto process does NOT depend on the applicant's country of origin. Citizens of CPLP, EU, and third countries — the conditions are the same. There are no 'accelerated' routes for Brazilians or Italians — 3 years of marriage is required for everyone.
💍 Marriage to a Portuguese citizen for ≥ 3 years at the time of application (or união de facto ≥ 3 years, registered at the Conservatória)
📜 Valid marriage at the time of application (no divorce, no separation with legal status)
⚖️ No criminal convictions in any country with a penalty of ≥ 3 years imprisonment
Formally NOT required for this route:

❌ CIPLE A2 certificate — formally NOT required (language only for naturalisation after 5+ years of residence). In practice, the IRN may assess language proficiency as an indirect indicator of 'effective connection' for marriages <6 years
❌ Residence in Portugal — not mandatory, the foreign spouse can live with the Portuguese partner abroad
❌ 'Connection to Portugal' as a formal checklist — not requested at the application stage. But see below about presumption

An important nuance regarding Lei 37/81 art. 3: for marriage of 3–6 years, it is MANDATORY to prove "ligação efetiva à comunidade nacional" (effective connection to the Portuguese community) — this is requested by the Ministério Público in case of opposition. For marriages of ≥6 years OR when there are joint Portuguese children, the presumption applies, and proving the connection is not necessary. In other words, "not required" is a simplification for cases of 6+ years.
Portugal recognises união de facto (de facto union) as equivalent to marriage for citizenship purposes. Registration:
📋 Cohabitation of ≥ 2 years (proven by atestado de residência from Junta de Freguesia + joint bills, rental agreement in both names)
📝 Sworn declaration at the Conservatória do Registo Civil (not at Junta — this is a notarial act)
🕒 Registration is instant; certificado de união de facto is issued on the same day

After registration, the 3-year period is counted from the start date of cohabitation (according to witness statements), not from the date of registration. Therefore, if you have already lived together for 5 years but are only registering now — the "3 years" requirement has already been fulfilled.
📄 Marriage certificate (if the marriage was not concluded in Portugal — apostille + sworn translation into Portuguese). If the marriage is in PT — the assento number from Conservatória is sufficient
📄 For união — certificado de união de facto
📄 Birth certificate of the applicant (apostille + sworn translation; for naturalised citizens, a full certificate with parents is required)
📄 Título de residência (residence permit) — to be attached if available, but NOT mandatory. For submission abroad (via consulate) — not required.
📄 Criminal record certificate from Portugal (certidão do registo criminal) — free at eportugal.gov.pt
📄 Criminal record certificate from the country of citizenship and all countries of residence for ≥ 1 year in the last 5 years — apostille + sworn translation
📄 Birth certificate of the Portuguese spouse (assento de nascimento — requested automatically through IRN, or attached)
📝 Completed form (modelo 1-B) — download from irn.pt, signed by the applicant
💶 Proof of payment of the fee — €250

The applicant does NOT have a Cartão de Cidadão (CC) — this is a Portuguese citizen document; it is issued only after obtaining citizenship. The documents should include a título de residência (if available) or passport.
🏛️ Conservatória dos Registos Centrais (Lisbon) — main channel
🏛️ Any Conservatória do Registo Civil in the place of residence (Porto, Coimbra, Faro, etc.)
🏢 Espaço Registos in Loja do Cidadão
🌍 Portuguese consulate abroad — if living outside PT
💻 Online through the IRN portal (from 2025 it will operate in test mode for CPLP citizens and some marriage cases)
🧑‍💼 Through a lawyer-advogado by power of attorney — without personal presence
Official legal timeframe — 1 year. In practice, in 2025–2026: 8–24 months. Depends on the completeness of documents and the workload of Conservatórias. Online submissions are processed faster.

After a positive decision, IRN registers you as a citizen in the Assento de Nascimento. Only after this can you request a Cartão de Cidadão at any Loja do Cidadão and obtain a Portuguese passport from IRN.
❗ Different transliteration of the name in the passport and marriage certificate — an affidavit from a notary confirming identity will be required
❗ Marriage concluded in a third country is not registered in the Conservatória — first, register the marriage in PT, then citizenship
❗ Portuguese spouse recently obtained citizenship (naturalisation) — the '3 years of marriage' period is counted from the date the spouse acquired citizenship, not from the wedding date
❗ Divorce in process — cannot apply; wait for a decision
❗ Partner renounced Portuguese citizenship — the right to naturalisation through marriage is lost
IRN — Nationality by marriage or união de facto
Civil marriage in Portugal — how to register a marriage

Civil marriage at the Conservatória do Registo Civil. Available to residents and non-residents. Duration: 30+ days process (from request to ceremony); urgency for tourists 5-10 days. Requirements: passport, birth certificate (with apostille + translation for foreigners), certificado de capacidade matrimonial from your country (if non-PT), divorce certificate (if previously married). Cost: €120 standard, €200 urgent. Same-sex marriage — same procedure since 2010. Religious marriage (Catholic/Evangelical) — also has civil effect.
✅ Must not be currently married (any country)
⚠️ Bigamy is a crime in PT, up to 2 years imprisonment
🌍 Marriage in PT is recognised in most countries after apostille
⚠️ Some countries (UAE, Saudi, etc.) do NOT recognise same-sex marriages — keep this in mind
👨‍👩‍👧 Children: legal in all parental capacities (joint custody, inheritance, insurance)
At the Conservatória do Registo Civil (any in PT)
- CC / residence permit / passport of both
- Divorce or death certificates (if previous marriage)
The Conservadora opens the process — 30+ days of public announcement

30-day period during which anyone can contest the marriage (for example, if someone knows that one partner is already married). 99% of cases go uncontested. After this, a date for the ceremony is set.
Conservatória or certified venue (hotel, winery — extra fee)
The Conservadora performs the registration — 15-20 minutes
Signing the book, exchanging (optional) rings, kissing (optional 😄)
Cost of ceremony at conservatória: included; outside venue: +€50-150
If you / your partner are a foreigner without PT residency, more documents are required.
«Capacity to marry» certificate from your country
Confirms: you are single + legally capable of marrying
Obtain from the registry office of your home country / consulate
PT consulates do not issue for PT citizens going abroad to marry
From BR: civil registry → apostille at MINRE
Apostille + translation to confirm single status. Valid ≤ 6 months.
Possible — a foreign tourist can marry in PT
Additional documents: proof of address (at least in a hotel), copies of stamps
Brazil tourists: easier — speak PT, have automatic right under the Treaty of Friendship
PT does not provide fast-track wedding tourism like Vegas

If 30 days is too long — apply for urgency
Grounds: pregnancy, severe illness, planned travel
Reduction of the prohibition period from 30 to 5-10 days
Approval at the discretion of the Conservadora
Without grounds — possible but not guaranteed approval
Beach wedding setup: contact the Conservatória + book ahead 2-3 months
Catholic: Concordata Santa Sé-Portugal (1940, revised 2004) — automatic civil recognition
Evangelical / Protestant / Other: registered minister can perform with civil effect (Lei 16/2001)
Muslim, Jewish, Hindu, and Buddhist religions are also recognised
Pre-requisite: the same Processo Preliminar at the Conservatória (30 days + document verification)
After the religious ceremony: the minister sends the signed book back to the Conservatória → registration in the civil book
Same-sex religious ceremonies — only accepted by certain denominations (some Methodist, Quakers, secular humanists)
🆔 Update CC / residence permit — surname change (optional in PT, common for women but not required)
📑 Update Finanças (if you want joint IRS)
🌍 If an international wedding: register in both countries (for recognition in the other)

**Antenupcial Agreement**: pre-nuptial agreement. Assigns property regime.
**Default (without pre-nup)**: comunhão de adquiridos — property purchased after marriage shared, before — separate
**Comunhão geral**: everything shared, even pre-marriage assets
**Separação total**: nothing shared, each owns their own
Pre-nup must be **signed BEFORE marriage** at notary / Conservatória
Recommended: separação total if significant disparate assets, business owners
Modify regime after marriage — possible but more complex (notarial conditions)

Standard route: legal residence for 5 years + knowledge of Portuguese A2 + no criminal record. 'Connection to Portugal' is officially NOT a mandatory requirement (the law does not mention it); IRN may assess it in contentious cases, but the fact of 5 years of legal residence is already considered sufficient. Important: at the time of application, you do not yet have a Cartão de Cidadão — it is issued only after obtaining citizenship. A título de residência is attached.

The Parliament re-adopted the law on 1 April 2026 (after Marcelo's December veto and TC's unconstitutionality). Awaiting the signature of President Seguro. PS sent a companion decree to TC. Below are both scenarios.

Decreto AR 17/XVII — national reform, re-adopted by Parliament on 1 April 2026 after Marcelo's veto (December 2025) and partial unconstitutionality TC Acórdão 1133/2025. Awaiting the signature of President António José Seguro (PS, in office since January 2026). If signed, the naturalisation period will increase: 7 years for CPLP+EU, 10 years for others; waiting time for MdI / first residence permit will NO longer be counted; a cultural integration test will be added; the fast Sephardic route has been cancelled. PS has already sent a separate companion decree on 'loss of citizenship as an additional penalty' — regarding fiscalisation on 17 April 2026 to TC. Applications submitted BEFORE promulgation will follow the old rules.
According to art. 6 Lei 37/81 (Nationality Law) and Decreto-Lei 237-A/2006, the requirements are as follows:
📅 Legal residence in Portugal ≥ 5 years (continuously or with breaks)

🗣️ Knowledge of Portuguese A2 — confirmed by a certificate (CIPLE, DEPLE, school diploma ≥ 150 hours, diploma from a Portuguese university, completion of 6th grade in a PT school). Not required for CPLP citizens.
⚖️ No criminal record with a sentence of ≥ 3 years imprisonment — in any country of residence
🛡️ Does not pose a threat to national security (rarely applied in practice)
What is NOT in the law (contrary to popular belief):

❌ 'Connection to Portugal' is NOT a formal requirement in art. 6. It may only be requested in cases of obvious signs of fictitious residence. 5 years of legal residence permit is considered sufficient evidence.
❌ A cultural/historical test is not required (until the new law is promulgated in 2026)
❌ Renouncing previous citizenship is not required — Portugal allows dual citizenship
How to count 5 years — a key question

The period of '5 years of legal residence' is counted from the date of the first ISSUANCE of the residence permit (título de residência / autorização de residência), and NOT from the date of entry or the date of application for the residence permit.
✅ Since 2018 (Lei 2/2020): the entire period of 'legal stay' is counted, including the waiting time for the first residence permit, if the residence permit was subsequently issued

✅ Manifestação de Interesse (expression of interest): waiting periods with comprovativo de submissão from AIMA are also counted towards these 5 years — this has been confirmed by the case law of the Supremo Tribunal Administrativo
✅ Short trips (holidays, business trips) do not interrupt the period
⚠️ A long absence of > 6 consecutive months or > 8 months in total over 2 years may interrupt — it depends on the type of residence permit
⚠️ If the residence permit is expired and not renewed in time — there is a break in legality. After renewal, the period is counted anew from the date of restoration

For accurate counting, it is useful (but NOT mandatory) to obtain a 'Certidão de residência legal' from AIMA — it confirms the dates of all residence permits and waiting periods. The certificate can be requested online at portal.aima.gov.pt or by e-mail to the regional delegation.

The law has changed several times. If your 5 years fall under different versions — clarify with a lawyer who has immigration practice. For example: the rules for Manifestação differed in 2018/2022/2025, and how these periods are counted — is sometimes a matter of dispute.
If President Seguro signs the new law (status as of 22 April 2026)

Brief timeline: 28 October 2025 — Parliament passed Decreto AR 17/XVII. 15 December 2025 — TC Acórdão 1133/2025 declared 4 provisions unconstitutional. End of December 2025 — President Marcelo vetoed. January 2026 — new president António José Seguro (PS). 1 April 2026 — Parliament re-passed the law (152 votes). 17 April 2026 — PS sent a companion decree (loss of citizenship as an additional penalty) to TC. The main decree as of 22 April 2026 is still on Seguro's desk.
Changes compared to the current regime:
⏱️ The waiting time for Manifestação de Interesse / first residence permit is no longer counted — the period is only counted from the date of issuance of the first título de residência
📝 Mandatory cultural integration test (history, Constitution, society)
🏠 Stricter requirements for actual presence — it is not enough to simply have a residence permit, you must physically live in PT
🚫 Cancellation of the expedited Sephardic route (Lei 43/2013 + DL 30-A/2015)
🔴 New ground for 'loss' of citizenship upon conviction for ≥5 years for particularly serious crimes (separate companion decree in TC on fiscalisation from 17 April 2026)
⚡ For applications submitted BEFORE promulgation, the old rules apply
Recommendation: if you have already completed your 5 years — apply immediately, without delay. Submitting halts the case under the current legislative regime.
How to prove knowledge of Portuguese
Any of the following documents is sufficient:
📜 CIPLE certificate (level A2) from CAPLE — €85 (2026), monthly sessions
📜 DEPLE (B1), DIPLE (B2), DAPLE (C1), DUPLE (C2) certificates — from CAPLE, any above A2
📜 Certificate from a school with ≥ 150 hours in the PLA programme (free state courses — see below)
🎓 Diploma from a Portuguese higher education institution (any field)
🏫 Certificate of completion of the 6th grade of a Portuguese school or higher
🇵🇹 For CPLP citizens (Brazil, Angola, Cape Verde, Mozambique, São Tomé, East Timor, Guinea-Bissau, Equatorial Guinea) — a certificate is NOT required: Portuguese is considered native/official
More details about preparation, free courses, and the exam can be found in a separate guide 'CIPLE A2'.
📝 Application — formulário modelo 1-A (for naturalisation) — download at irn.pt, fill out by hand or online
📄 Valid foreign passport — copy of all filled pages
📄 Título de residência (residence permit) — valid. At this stage, you do NOT yet have a Cartão de Cidadão — it is issued only after citizenship approval
📄 Birth certificate — apostille + sworn translation into Portuguese (full, with parents' names)
📄 Criminal record certificate PT (certidão do registo criminal) — free at eportugal.gov.pt
📄 Criminal record certificate from the country of citizenship + each country of residence ≥ 1 year for the last 5 years — with apostille and sworn translation
📜 Certificate of Portuguese language proficiency (for non-CPLP)
💶 Fee €250 — paid at IRN (Multibanco reference is provided upon submission)
What is advisable to attach, but not officially mandatory:

📜 Certidão de residência legal from AIMA — confirms exact dates of residence permit and waiting periods. Helps IRN to verify 5 years faster; without it, IRN requests from AIMA themselves (plus 1–3 months to the timeframe)

📋 Confirmation of 'connection to Portugal': IRS tax returns, employment contracts, marriage/birth certificates, atestado de residência. This is NOT a mandatory requirement by law; but if attached — it speeds up processing and reduces the risk of additional requests

All foreign documents must be apostilled (or legalised by the consulate) + sworn translation into Portuguese (tradutor-intérprete ajuramentado). A list of sworn translators: on the website of the Ministério dos Negócios Estrangeiros.
🏛️ Conservatória dos Registos Centrais (Lisbon) — main channel
🏛️ Any Conservatória do Registo Civil in your place of residence
🏢 Espaço Registos at Loja do Cidadão (by appointment)
🌍 Through the consulate of Portugal (if you live abroad, for example, based on 5 years of residency in the past)
💻 Online through the Portal da Nacionalidade (irn.justica.gov.pt) — from 2025 for some cases
🧑‍💼 Through a lawyer by proxy — application without personal presence
Official deadline — 1 year from the date of application. Actual 2026 — 24–48 months (on average 36 months). Backlog at IRN >200,000 applications. Online applications are processed faster.

After approval, IRN publishes the decision in the Diário da República. Only from the date of publication are you a citizen of Portugal. After that: request Cartão de Cidadão at any Loja do Cidadão (portrait + biometrics, €18 standard issue / €33 urgent / €53 very urgent — IRN rates 2026), order a Portuguese passport at IRN (€85), register newborn children as PT citizens.

For CPLP citizens: under the current law — 5 years, under the new law 2026 — 7 years. Language A2 is automatically counted (Portuguese — native/official in the country of origin, CIPLE is not required).

For EU/EEA/Swiss citizens: 5 years under the current law, under the new law 2026 — 7 years (EU are equated to CPLP). Language A2 — CIPLE or an equivalent certificate is required. Dual citizenship is allowed.

For third-country citizens: 5 years under the current law, 10 years under the new law 2026. Language A2 (CIPLE or school certificate from 150 hours). Dual citizenship is permitted by most countries — check your country's law.
Cartão de Cidadão after obtaining citizenship

Navegante — unified travel pass for the Lisbon area: metro, Carris buses, CP trains, Transtejo river ferries, Fertagus, MTS. Two main types: monthly Metropolitano (€40) or Municipal (€30). Children under 12 — free (from 2024), children 13–18 and students — €20. Purchased at metro machines, Carris kiosks or via app.

Valid throughout the entire metropolitan area of Lisbon (Lisboa + Sintra + Cascais + Almada + Loures + Odivelas + Vila Franca + Mafra + 10 others). Covers metro, Carris, CP suburbano, Transtejo, Fertagus, MTS Seixal. The best choice if you travel between municipalities.
Only in YOUR municipality. For a resident of Lisboa — only Lisboa (including metro + Carris). For a resident of Cascais — only Cascais + CP within. NOT valid outside the municipality.
Prepaid balance on the Navegante card. Trip ~€1.80 metro, €2 bus. 15% discount off the regular fare. For infrequent trips.
Go to any Metro Lisboa kiosk or Carris loja

From 2025, Navegante will be integrated. Navegante Wallet Pass on iPhone/Android — just tap at the turnstile, and you're through. Convenient, replaces plastic.

How to contact AIMA — all channels

AIMA is overwhelmed: the line 808 202 653 is often busy, email responses take 2–6 weeks, and in-person appointments are by prior arrangement only. The real way: AIMA Portal (portal.aima.gov.pt), book through Espaço Cidadão, or contact the linha dedicada 800 203 373 for residents who already have a process. For urgent cases — email by region, list below.
Main channel from 2026. Access via Chave Móvel Digital (national e-ID) or NIF+password. Case status, document submission, notifications, appointment booking. Covers 80% of inquiries without calls.
Some Espaço Cidadão have AIMA windows. Book at eportugal.gov.pt → 'Agendamento (appointment booking) AIMA'. Usually 2–4 weeks waiting time, but specific assistance for the case.
Often busy; the best chance is the first 30 minutes after opening
Speak clearly in Portuguese, English is possible but slower
For repeat calls — alternate the number with the area code
Free line for those with an OPEN process in AIMA. A process number (processo) is needed. The hours are the same. Useful for status inquiries, complaints, emergency assistance.
Always include the processo, full name, NIF (Portuguese tax ID), and submission date. Attach PDF documents.
The AIMA line 808 202 653 is often busy — the community has come up with workarounds. These tools are unofficial but widely used and save hours of repeated calls:

🤖 Telegram bot @callaimabot — automatic call to SEF/AIMA. You send the bot your number and process, it calls the AIMA line every N minutes until it gets through, and connects you with an operator. Free of charge
📱 Community @aimacall — publishes successful call windows (message statistics)
💬 Chat @aimasefgroup — typical questions are resolved collectively: checking DUC, CN codes, payment errors
⚠️ These services are NOT official, AIMA does not regulate them. Your personal data is shared with a third party — decide if you are comfortable with that
Practice: how to get a response
📅 Booking at AIMA through the Portal — average waiting time is 15–60 days. Book in advance
☎️ It’s better to call on Tuesday–Thursday. Monday and Friday are dead. The first 30 minutes after opening is the highest chance to get through
🗣️ Script: “Bom dia, tenho o processo número X e gostaria de saber o estado” (good morning, I have process X, I would like to know the status)
📧 Emails without a process are often ignored. Always attach documents or a screenshot from the Portal
📮 Petition to the Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman) for long delays > 90 days — really speeds things up
⚖️ Injunction / Mandado de Execução in court — a last resort if AIMA does not respond > 180 days
📍 Lisbon Central — Av. António Augusto de Aguiar, 20
📍 Porto Central — Rua D. João IV, 863
📍 Funchal — Rua Nova da Rochinha 1, Madeira
📍 Ponta Delgada — Rua Eng. José Cordeiro, Azores
⚠️ Without prior appointment, they do not accept. Walk-in is not available from 2024.
AIMA refusal → appeal to court within 30 days (Tribunal Administrativo)
Silence > 90 days on petition → complaint to Provedor de Justiça (provedor-jus.pt)
Employee misconduct → Inspecção-Geral dos Serviços (IGAI) of the ministry
Issues with residence permit and risk of deportation → urgent lawyer from Ordem dos Advogados (Bar Association of Portugal) with immigration practice
Vulnerable group (refugees, minors) → CPR (Conselho Português para os Refugiados)
Renewal of residence permit through the AIMA online portal

From 1 January 2026, the renewal of the residence permit will be done through portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt. No visit required if all documents are in order. Applications can be submitted 30 days before the card expires, but it is advisable to do so 60–90 days in advance. The residence permit letter will be sent by post, and while you wait, your legal status is confirmed by the comprovativo de submissão.
The launch of portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt is on 01.01.2026. The deadline for renewing expired residence permits until 30.06.2025 is 15 April 2026.
In 2026, renewals go through several portals — it is important to choose the right one, otherwise the system will not 'see' your process:
✅ portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt — cards expiring from 1 July 2025 to 30 June 2026 (main channel)
✅ portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt — ARI / Golden Visa, expiring from 22 February 2020 to 30 June 2026
⚠️ Cards that expired BEFORE 30 June 2025 — through the AIMA main services portal (old channel), not through renovações
❌ Cartão de Residência UE (for EU citizens) — renewal through this portal is currently unavailable; please contact Junta de Freguesia (for CRUE) or directly AIMA
Do not confuse porta-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt with the general porta.aima.gov.pt (single portal). It is renovações that contains the renewal form.
🗓️ Officially — from 30 days before the expiration of the residence permit
✅ Recommendation: 60–90 days before expiration (less stress, more time to correct mistakes)
⚠️ Less than 30 days — the portal may accept it, but no guarantee it will be processed before expiration
⏰ If the residence permit has already expired: apply immediately. Formally, the fine is €75–300 (art. 192 Lei 23/2007), but in 2025–2026 when renewing online, it is rarely enforced
🛡️ Decreto-Lei 41/2023: if AIMA has not processed your request on time — you remain in legal status with the comprovativo

All residence permits expiring before 30 June 2025 must be renewed by 15 April 2026. After this date, the status is considered invalid, and restoration is through a complicated procedure with a lawyer.
🔐 Chave Móvel Digital activated with signing function (not just authorisation). Check at autenticacao.gov.pt
📱 Current PT phone number in CMD (not foreign) — SMS codes will be sent to this number
📄 Passport valid for at least 3 months after the planned date of receiving the new residence permit card
🏠 Address matches: in AIMA, Finanças, NISS, and on the atestado de residência (if you plan to attach it)
💼 NISS is active and without missed payments (mandatory from April 2025)
💸 No debts with Finanças (certidão de não dívida) and Segurança Social
📷 Biometric data — if it has been more than 5 years since your last visit, an in-person appointment for new biometric data may be required (rare, but it happens)

Authorisation via Chave Móvel Digital (government electronic signature). For PT citizens — via Cartão de Cidadão (Portuguese citizen card). Important: this is a separate portal, not portal.aima.gov.pt (general portal) — the link is specifically for renewals.
2. Select the type of residence permit

The system will suggest the type based on your current residence permit (automatically detected). Check — if incorrect, your process may still be in the old SEF database, a separate ticket is needed via email to the regional delegation.
Passport — all filled pages, good quality JPG/PDF
Atestado de residência (recent, not older than 3 months) from Junta de Freguesia (parish council) OR rental contract registered with Finanças, OR utility bill in your name
Criminal record certificate PT (certidão do registo criminal) — free at eportugal.gov.pt, valid for 3 months
For D7/D8 — proof of income: bank statement for 3 months, employment/client contract, pension documents
For reagrupamento familiar — marriage certificate/birth certificates of children
Proof of NISS + absence of debts (Segurança Social Direta → Comprovativo de situação contributiva)
Proof from Finanças — certidão de não dívida (free at Portal das Finanças)
Proof of health insurance or SNS utente (if required for your type of residence permit)
Practice: file size limit of 5 MB per document. If it doesn't fit — compress it at ilovepdf.com or a similar site. All scans must be in colour, not photos, and readable.

The portal generates a Multibanco reference (DUC — Documento Único de Cobrança) or allows payment by card. From 1 March 2026, a new AIMA fee table will be in effect (+33% to the old one): application + analysis €133, granting/renewal of residence permit (art. 75) ~€307.20, permanent (art. 76) ~€351.10, second copy of the card — 50% of issuance. Payment is confirmed within 1–3 hours, but sometimes takes up to a day.

If the amount is paid partially (a typical mistake is to fall short due to the exchange rate): pay the remainder using the SAME DUC, but through a DIFFERENT bank (not the one from which the first payment was made). Avoid making additional payments through CTT or Payshop — they often do not register. You cannot create a new DUC for additional payment.
The final step is the electronic signature of the application using Chave Móvel Digital. You will receive an SMS code or a push notification in the Autenticação.gov app.

After submission, the portal issues a comprovativo de submissão in PDF. THIS IS A CRUCIAL DOCUMENT. Save it everywhere: cloud, email, print it out. It confirms your legal status until you receive the new card and is accepted instead of the residence permit by banks, schools, SNS, and at the border when returning to PT.

Standard time is 30–90 days. The maximum by law is up to 120 days. Lei 61/2025 (in force from 23 October 2025) established a general deadline for AIMA decisions of 9 months for complex cases. The EU directive on the single permit requires PT to decide on single-permit applications within 90 days from 22 May 2026. Notification is sent to your email + in your personal account. Statuses: “Em análise”, “Notificado para entrevista”, “Aprovado”, “Indeferido”.
7a. Comprovativo de Deferimento — your temporary residence permit

As soon as the process is approved, a button “Comprovativo de Deferimento” appears in your personal account — a PDF with a QR code for authenticity verification. This document confirms the positive decision BEFORE the physical card arrives.
🪪 Accepted by banks, schools, SNS, and employers as proof of legal status
✈️ At the EU border and with airlines — together with the old card (or without it, if it is the first issuance) and your passport
📱 Save the PDF in the cloud and on your phone — it is accessed more often than the card itself
⚖️ The QR code allows the verifier to instantly validate the document through AIMA

The new plastic residence permit arrives by CTT registered mail at the address specified in the application. If you have not received it within 30 days after the status 'Aprovado' — write to the regional AIMA delegation.

The CTT tracking number is NOT displayed in the AIMA personal account after approval — you have to find out by calling the regional delegation. It is better to call two weeks after the status 'Aprovado'.
🖥️ Submit from a desktop in Chrome — there are often file upload issues on mobile
⏰ Do not submit at 08:00 / 09:00 on Monday morning — the portal is overloaded, increasing the chance of technical errors. The best hours are Tuesday to Thursday, 14:00–17:00
📸 If the process 'hangs' on one screen — take a screenshot, DO NOT close the tab, wait 2 minutes
🚫 Do not attempt to submit again if the first attempt 'froze' — check your personal account after 24 hours, often the application was still sent
📋 Atestado de residência (issued by Junta) is sometimes given without witnesses — check with your Junta. A utility bill or rental contract is often sufficient as an alternative
📷 Passport photos are not needed in scans — AIMA uses the biometrics from the previous appointment or calls for a new one if necessary
🧾 Order a certidão de não dívida from Finanças and Segurança Social in advance — they may take 1–2 days to generate
⚡ For residence permits under D7/D8 — the bank statement must show STABLE income (not a one-off large sum before submission)
👨‍👩‍👦 For reagrupamento familiar: children under 16 are automatically included, teenagers aged 16–18 apply separately for their extension

If there is an error in your data in the AIMA database (for example, incorrect transliteration of your name, incorrect address from the old SEF process) — submission through the portal is often blocked. The solution is to email the regional delegation requesting to correct the data, then submit.
Rejection or request for additional documents
If after submission you receive a letter requesting additional documents (Notificação para aperfeiçoamento):
📬 Deadline for response — 10 working days from the date of notification
💻 Additional documents must be uploaded through the same portal-renovacoes, tab 'Processos em curso'
📧 If the deadline is insufficient — you can request an extension via email to the regional delegation
🚫 Missing the deadline = automatic rejection (indeferimento por falta de elementos)
⚖️ Deadline for appeal — 30 days from the date of notification (recurso hierárquico in AIMA or judicial in Tribunal Administrativo)
🧑‍💼 We recommend hiring an advogado with immigration practice — the chance of reconsideration is higher
🏛️ In parallel — complaint to the Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman), especially if the rejection is formal
⏰ While the appeal is ongoing — your status is usually maintained by comprovativo
AIMA has delayed the process — what to do
Standard timeframe — 30–90 days. If 90 days have passed without a response — it’s time to take action. The maximum by law is 120 days, after which AIMA violates the procedural deadline.
🛡️ You remain in legal status — Decreto-Lei 41/2023 directly protects you, as long as you have comprovativo
📧 90+ days — email the regional delegation of AIMA (see the guide 'How to Contact AIMA') with the processo number and submission date
🏛️ 120+ days — complaint to the Provedor de Justiça at provedor-jus.pt (actually speeds things up, AIMA is obliged to respond to the ombudsman)
⚖️ 180+ days — Intimação para prestação de informações at the Tribunal Administrativo. Paid procedure (~€200), but forces AIMA to respond within 2 months
✈️ For trips abroad — comprovativo PDF + old residence permit card. Airlines and EU border officials are obliged to accept. In case of issues — contact the Portuguese consulate in the transit country
Special cases — not through the main portal
Some categories require a separate procedure, not through portal-renovacoes:
Submission via AIMA feedback form (not through renovações)
Request a slot for biometrics — only through lista de espera
Documents: passport, old residence permit, IRS tax declaration, employment contract, NISS, certidão de não dívida
Waiting time for a slot — 2–6 months
Register in lista de espera through the form on the AIMA website
Documents: copy of passport, proof of enrolment in the course (IEFP / certified centre), payment receipt
Later, AIMA will schedule a personal appointment — wait for the call via email

Renewal is possible through portal-renovacoes for ARI that expired between 22 February 2020 and 30 June 2026. Minimum physical presence in PT is 7 days per year (confirmed by stamps or comprovativo de alojamento).
CRUE is issued and renewed through Junta de Freguesia or Câmara Municipal, not through AIMA. The renewal portal currently does not support CRUE.
Features of the first renewal (immediately after visa D)
The first renewal differs from subsequent ones: it is formally not a 'renewal', but an 'initial issuance' (emissão inicial) based on visa D. The process is the same, but the scrutiny is stricter:
📄 They check the compliance of visa D and the current situation (D7 → passive income; D8 → remote work; etc.)
💰 Requirement to confirm income FOR THE ENTIRE TIME since entry (full bank statement)
🏠 Atestado de residência — recent (not older than 2 months)
📝 If there was a condition in visa D (for example, 'open bank account in 90 days') — attach proof of compliance
⏰ The issuance period for the first card is up to 120 days (longer than usual renewal)
no older than 3 months, from Junta de Freguesia

Legalisation through articles 88/89 Lei 23/2007 — when and how

Articles 88 and 89 of Lei 23/2007 — "extraordinary" legalisation for individuals already in Portugal: art. 88 for employees (trabalho subordinado), art. 89 for self-employed individuals and investors. Lei 61/2025 (in force from 23 October 2025) abolished the 88.2 / 89.2 route for NEW applicants. Applicable only if you had a comprovativo de MdI before 3 June 2024 (Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024). The new law requires visa D before entry.
The Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) regime — the mechanism for applying under art. 88.2 from within PT without a visa — was closed in two phases:
📅 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 ceased accepting new MdI applications
📅 23 October 2025 — Lei 61/2025 came into force; 88.2 / 89.2 routes revoked for new applicants
✅ Those who submitted MdI before 3 June 2024 with documents (employment contract + NISS + NIF + atestado) — procedures for art. 88/89 continue to operate
❌ Those who entered after 3 June 2024 — only through visa D at the consulate
⏳ AIMA is gradually processing the MdI backlog — appointments will continue until 2027.
Do not confuse this route with new residence permits (D7, D8, CPLP) — they operate from abroad through the consulate.
For individuals with a valid D2/D3 visa with the right to work. After entry and employment, application to AIMA.

For those who are already in Portugal legally (tourist visa / Schengen / visa-free). Requirements: valid employment contract ≥ 12 months, NISS, proof of residence, apostilled criminal record certificates, NIF.
From December 2025, new applications under 88.2 will NOT be accepted
Those who applied before the repeal are in the AIMA queue for consideration
During this time, legal residence is confirmed by comprovativo (confirmation) de submissão (submission confirmation)

Crossing the border with a residence permit for Portugal — Schengen and third countries

With a Portuguese residence permit (cartão de residência), you can travel freely throughout the Schengen Area for up to 90 days within a 180-day period without a separate visa. For entry — passport + valid residence card (and if the residence permit has expired but is in the process of renewal — maintenance certificate). For entry into Portugal from non-EU countries — take your boarding pass and residence permit; no stamp is issued. Upon returning from Schengen, officers usually have no questions.

According to Regulation (EU) 2016/399 (Schengen Borders Code), holders of a valid residence permit from a Schengen member state can move freely to other Schengen countries for up to 90 days within a 180-day period. This rule applies to short trips (tourism, family, business trips).
Documents: passport + cartão de residência (physical card)
Passport validity — at least 3 months after return
Insurance is mandatory for certain countries (Italy, Switzerland)
If the residence permit has expired but there is confirmation of extension — take the comprovativo (confirmation) from AIMA
No more than 90 days in total in other Schengen countries over 180 days
The residence permit does NOT grant the right to work in other Schengen countries. Only in Portugal (or in exceptions — ICT-card, EU Blue Card with rights for cross-border movement).

Passport + residence permit (exit stamp is issued)

Transit through another Schengen airport is considered as crossing the Schengen border. For travel to non-EU with a layover in Frankfurt/Madrid, a residence permit is sufficient.

For Brazilians: visa-free entry + application for residence permit from within Portugal. For other CPLP citizens: consular visa is mandatory (after Law 61/2025).

The new citizenship law (1 April 2026, awaiting the signature of President Seguro) will increase the residency requirement for citizenship from 5 to 7 years for all CPLP citizens.
A Brazilian passport grants 90 days in the Schengen Area. Declare the purpose at the border — residence under CPLP mobility.

EU/EEA/Swiss citizens do not need a visa but must register within 30 days of arrival — obtain the Certificado de Registo de Cidadão da União Europeia (CRUE). This is done at the Câmara Municipal (municipality) of residence. ~€15, issued on the day of the visit.
CRUE = Certificado de Registo de Cidadão da União Europeia. Confirms your right to reside in Portugal as an EU citizen. Valid for 5 years, after which it converts to permanent residence.
This is NOT equivalent to a third-country residence permit — CRUE does not grant more rights than you have as an EU citizen. It is simply a registration of residence.
Within 30 days after 3 months of staying in Portugal (a total of 4 months after entry). The fine for non-compliance is €400-1500.
In practice, many municipalities accept registration immediately after entry.
At the Câmara Municipal (city hall) of your residence. In some municipalities — at the Junta de Freguesia (parish council).
Registration at www.portaldoeleitor.pt or directly at the city hall. In larger cities (Lisbon, Porto) registration is mandatory.
