Manifestação de Interesse (expression of interest) has been cancelled — what to do instead

Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) — the mechanism for applying for a residence permit from within Portugal without a visa — has been phased out in two stages. 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 stopped accepting new MdI applications. Lei 61/2025 came into effect on 23 October 2025 and definitively cancelled the scheme; 31 December 2025 — deadline for applications during the transition period for those who had a comprovativo before 3 June 2024. Now all non-CPLP third countries need a visa at the consulate BEFORE entry. Previously submitted cases are processed under the old rules.

Lei 61/2025 came into effect on 23 October 2025 and cancelled the MdI scheme. New applications were no longer accepted as of 3 June 2024 under Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024. 31 December 2025 — deadline for the transition period. Cases submitted before 3 June 2024 are processed under the old rules.
What it was and the timeline of cancellation

Manifestação de Interesse (MdI) — a tool that allowed foreigners who entered Portugal on a tourist visa or visa-free (with Schengen) to apply for a residence permit from within the country without returning home for a visa. It was in operation since 2017.
Problem: by the end of 2023 AIMA had a backlog of over 400,000 applications — the bureaucracy was overwhelmed. Therefore, the scheme was phased out in two stages:
📅 3 June 2024 — Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024: AIMA stopped accepting new MdI applications. Applications submitted BEFORE this date remained in the queue for processing
📅 23 October 2025 — Lei 61/2025 came into effect: final cancellation of the MdI scheme in the new text of the law
📅 31 December 2025 — deadline for submission during the transition period for those who had an action comprovativo before 3 June 2024 (chain of transitional application processing)

After 3 June 2024, it is not possible to submit an MdI. After 31 December 2025, the transitional route is closed; the scheme is legally dead. Reason: aligning Portugal with EU standards and reducing the backlog.
All non-CPLP third countries: go through a consular visa BEFORE entry.
Brazilians: still visa-free + application to AIMA for CPLP residence.
Other CPLP: now only through a consular visa.
If Manifestação de Interesse is submitted before 03.06.2024
Cases submitted before 03.06.2024 are processed under the old procedure
AIMA must complete processing and either issue a residence permit or refuse
Processing time — continues even after 31.12.2025, until AIMA closes the backlog
If refused — you can appeal or leave and apply for a consular visa
Process status — via the official AIMA portal or email of the regional AIMA delegation

Long waiting ≠ legal presence. If 90 days of Schengen have expired and you continue to stay in PT without comprovativo de agendamento (appointment confirmation) — this is a violation. Old cases only protect those who submitted MdI before 1 June 2024 and have comprovativo de submissão in hand.
‘There will be an amnesty’ — no. The government has publicly stated that no amnesty is planned.
‘The old process will be restored’ — no, Law 61/2025 has come into force and does not provide for reinstatement.
Current changes are published on the official AIMA website (aima.gov.pt) and in Diário da República (dre.pt).
Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 (DRE) — cessation of MdI acceptance 1 June 2024
The Portugal News — Manifestação de Interesse abolished

Overstay is a serious violation. For exceeding 90 days of Schengen / visa / residence permit, fines of €80–700 apply, entry in the Schengen Information System (SIS II), risk of refusal for extensions and future visas for 1-5 years, in severe cases — entry ban to Schengen. The worst is cumulative overstay: the longer you are out of status, the lower the chance of legalising back.
Visa-free tourists (BR, US, UK, CA, ...) — 90 days in any 180 days in the Schengen Area
Leaving for 1 day DOES NOT reset the counter — the 180-day window is rolling
Visa D allows 4 months for entry and meeting with AIMA. If you missed the AIMA agendamento and the visa has expired — you are in overstay even while in PT.
Residence permit has expired, renewal not submitted — overstay
Residence permit has expired, but there is a comprovativo de submissão (submitted for renewal before expiration) — legal status is maintained
Comprovativo da MdI (until 03.06.2024) — protected against overstay during the transitional period
Important: comprovativo must be shown at the border when returning to PT
TPD extended until 04.03.2027. After that, without transitioning to a D visa / national status — overstay. While TPD is in effect — overstay is not possible.
According to Lei 23/2007 art. 192–193 and DL 37-A/2024:
Overstay 90+ days: fine €300–700 + possible entry ban
Border control with overstay — fine to be paid on the spot (or later at PSP); recorded in SIS II
With two or more recorded overstays — possible inclusion in Entry Ban Schengen-wide (1-5 years)
No fines for children under 16, but overstay is recorded
Your willingness to leave voluntarily and pay — mitigates the fine

Overstay blocks: new D visas (AIMA checks history), applications for residence permit from within the country (under art. 88/89 legal entry was required — after DL 37-A/2024 option 88.2 is closed, 89.2 — only under transitional regime). There are three ways out of overstay: (1) voluntary departure + payment of fine + new visa from the consulate; (2) applying for CPLP status/refugee/humanitarian status; (3) risk: deportation by court decision.
Option A — leave and apply from abroad (recommended)
Go through exit control — the fine will be recorded; pay at the airport or later through Portal AT
Apply for a D visa from your home country through the consulate
The consul sees the overstay in your history — may ask questions/refuse; a convincing justification (work, family, contract) is essential
If overstay ≤30 days and no other violations — high chances; 90+ days — significant risks
Option B — CPLP mobility (if a citizen of a CPLP country)

Brazilians, Angolans, Mozambicans, etc. can apply for a residence permit under CPLP while in PT, even with an overstay. AIMA prefers to grant permission instead of deportation if there is a contrato / funds.
Option C — marriage / partnership with a PT/EU citizen

A valid marriage / registered união de facto grants the right to reagrupamento familiar or residence permit as an EU citizen family member. Overstay does not block this, but AIMA may require the fine to be paid first.
Option D — applying on humanitarian grounds (rarely)
Medical treatment, pregnancy, asylum, family circumstances. Through advogado → AIMA / court. Long, complicated, requires justification.
🛂 Stamps in your passport — dates of entry/exit from Schengen
🏛️ AIMA — call 808 257 257 and request a status check using your NIF/CC
📧 Schedule an appointment with AIMA regarding your status
⚖️ In disputed cases — advogado (Ordem dos Advogados, portal.oa.pt), consultation €50-150
Tips — how NOT to end up in overstay
🗓️ Mark the expiry date of your visa/residence permit + minus 90 days in your calendar (this is how long AIMA takes to process extensions)
📝 Residence permit extension — apply through portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt as early as possible
📋 Keep ALL comprovativo PDFs — without them, the border does not understand that you are in the process
✈️ Do not leave the Schengen Area with an expired residence permit and without a comprovativo — you may not be allowed back in
⚠️ 90 days as a Schengen tourist + applying for a residence permit = a bad idea: if AIMA does not approve within 90 days, you will be in overstay
🔔 If you change your address or lose your documents — inform AIMA within 60 days
📞 If in doubt about your status — call AIMA / consult a lawyer; overstay is worse than prevention
Permanent residency in Portugal (art. 125) and EU Long-Term Resident

After 5 years of legal residence, you can apply for **Autorização de Residência Permanente** (art. 125 Lei 23/2007) — does not expire, renewal every 10 years. Alternative: **EU Long-Term Resident** (DL 29/2012 transposes Directive 2003/109) — grants the right to live and work freely in any EU country. Both — without the need to obtain citizenship. Requirements: stable income ≥ SMN, health insurance, A2 Portuguese (art. 7), no criminal record. Application to AIMA.
Two types of PMJ — which to choose
Right to work, live, study in PT without restrictions
Cannot be lost during long absences (up to 24 months consecutively)
Path to citizenship after 5 years (general case) or 7 under the new law 2026
Family members receive a temporary residence permit, then can pursue their own path to PMJ
Does not grant rights in other EU countries
Right to work in any EU country (with notification to its authorities)
Right to move and obtain a residence permit in another EU country is easier
Requirements are slightly stricter — stable income above
Absence of ≥ 12 months in the EU is not allowed (otherwise loss)
**Advantage** for those planning mobility in the EU (Germany, Netherlands)

For most residents — **national PMJ**: the procedure is simpler, fewer documents, does not expire. EULTR — if you genuinely plan to move to another EU country within 1-3 years. It is possible to obtain both simultaneously (it is recommended not to abuse this — AIMA may delay).
📅 **5 years of legal residence** in PT (as for citizenship — including waiting periods for MdI under the old regime and transitional moments)
🗣️ **Portuguese A2** — CIPLE certificate or PLA school/Certificado 150 hours. Exemption: CPLP citizens, holders of a PT diploma
💰 **Stable income** ≥ SMN (€920 in 2026) for the last 12 months
🏠 **Suitable housing** — rental contract / ownership, area per family
⚕️ **Health insurance or SNS utente** — works if registered
⚖️ **No criminal record** with a sentence of ≥ 1 year imprisonment
📋 **No debts** with Finanças and Segurança Social
🛡️ No violations of immigration law (overstay, illegal work)
📝 Modelo de requerimento (on the AIMA website)
📄 Passport (original + copies of all filled pages)
💼 Proof of income: IRS Anexo A for 3 years, 12 monthly payslips, or Anexo B (for self-employed)
🏠 Atestado de residência from Junta + rental contract
💳 Certificates of no debt: Finanças + Segurança Social (free on e-portals)
⚖️ Criminal record: PT (free on eportugal.gov.pt) + country of residence ≥ 1 year in the last 5 years
Through portal-renovacoes.aima.gov.pt with CMD / CC. Select ‘Autorização de Residência Permanente’. Upload document scans.
AIMA — concession art. 76 = **€351.10** (table 01.03.2026). Additionally, appointment + analysis ~€133.
Usually not required for national PMJ, sometimes called. For EU Long-Term — interview is mandatory.
6-12 months (under Lei 61/2025 — up to 9 months legal). In reality, in 2026 — up to 18 months due to backlog.
The PMJ card arrives by post. Valid for 10 years (national) or 5 years (EULTR).
🎂 There is no mandatory 'counting' of time — renewal every 10 years is simple
🌍 You can leave for ≤ 24 months consecutively (national) / 12 months (EULTR) without loss
🎓 Access to public education on the same basis
🏛️ Voting in local elections (municipal) — for EU citizens and some CPLP
🎫 Citizenship: 5+ years of PMJ (combined with the temporary period = 5-10 years depending on nationality law 2026)
PMJ does NOT automatically grant citizenship — you must apply separately through IRN. However, it simplifies the process, as all documents are more stable + language has already been confirmed.
Submitted with the same application to AIMA, but select 'Estatuto de residente de longa duração (UE)'
Need an **integration programme**: A2 + civics course (5-10 hours "Cidadão PT")
Permissible absence from the EU: ≤ 12 months consecutively / ≤ 6 years in total
Mobility: after obtaining — notify another EU country 30 days in advance, residency there 1-3 months
**Not** applicable: refugees with PT status, students, diplomats
Loss: ≥ 12 months outside the EU / 6 years outside a specific EU country → converted to a national permit of the country where you reside
Visa Procuro Trabalho — job search in Portugal

Procuro Trabalho — visa for 120 days (+60 extension) for job searching. Confirmation of funds 4× IAS for 4 months ≈ €2,148.52 (IAS 2026 = €537.13/month) + return ticket. Popular among graduates of PT universities and highly qualified individuals. After hiring — transition to D3 from within the country.
Graduate from a Portuguese university (after D4, you can stay for 1 year)
Candidate for in-demand positions (IT, engineering, medicine, R&D)
Proof of funds for living for 4 months (≈ €2,148.52 = 4× IAS, €537.13/month for 2026)
Accommodation in Portugal (bookings / contract for 120 days)
Vistos.mne.gov.pt → Visto para Procura de Trabalho. Processing time 15-60 days.
The visa allows for 120 days in PT. You need to register with AIMA within this period.
Obtain a residence permit for the job search period (120 days + extension of 60 days if necessary).
LinkedIn, Landing.Jobs, ITJobs.pt, NetJobs, direct contact with companies. 180 days is a realistic timeframe.
5. Found a job — transition to D3/D1
With a contract in hand — apply for D3 (qualified) or D1 (ordinary worker) from within the country without leaving. At AIMA.
After 180 days without a contract — mandatory departure. Reapplication is possible after 1 year.
If you are finishing your studies with D4
Graduates of Portuguese universities have SIMPLIFIED access to Procuro Trabalho — application from within PT without the consulate. It is recommended to apply 3-6 months before graduation.
Vistos MNE — Visa for Job Search
Startup Visa — for founders of certified startups

Startup Visa — for founders of innovative startups. Requires approval from one of ~94 certified incubators in Portugal (list IAPMEI 2026) + viable business plan. Grants residence permit for 2 years, access to IFICI. Application through the Startup Visa portal + consular visa.
Startup Visa = residence visa for founders of startups with a technological/innovative product. Managed by IAPMEI in conjunction with Startup Portugal.
The main difference from D2: confirmation is required FROM ONE OF THE CERTIFIED incubators (Beta-i, Startup Lisboa, Fábrica de Startups, etc.) that your project is innovation-driven.
Revenue forecast ≥ €325,000/year within 5 years OR 10 jobs OR high-tech R&D
Own funds ≥ €6,445.56 (12× IAS at €537.13 in 2026) for the applicant's living expenses + 50% IAS for spouse, 30% IAS for child
1. Choose an incubator from the list
2. Contact the incubator and submit the project
Each incubator has its own evaluation process (pitches, interviews, documents).
3. Sign a contract with the incubator
Acceptance declaration from the incubator = golden key.
4. Submit an application through the Startup Visa portal
With the incubator declaration + business plan + finances. IAPMEI reviews within 30-60 days.
5. After approval — visa at the consulate
With IAPMEI approval, the visa procedure is quicker (30-60 days).
Standard: residence permit, biometrics, company registration.
Support from the incubator (mentor, coworking, network)
Ability to bring a team (co-founders can apply together)
many are free, some charge a fee
Check the status of visa D and registration in AIMA

After entering with visa D, you need to get an appointment at AIMA within 120 days. Status is checked through the AIMA Portal (portal.aima.gov.pt — main channel). For cases initiated while still at SEF (before October 2023), some information is in the old database — sometimes a request by email to the regional delegation of AIMA is needed. If there is no registration within 30 days after entry — contact by email.
Timeline from visa issuance to residence permit
1. Received a D visa at the consulate — it is valid for 120 days from the date of entry
2. Entry into Portugal — passport stamp with date
3. AIMA must issue an appointment for biometric collection and obtaining a residence permit within 120 days
4. Historically, AIMA used to make the appointment itself, now it is not automatic — sometimes you need to initiate it
5. At the appointment — biometrics, document verification, signing of apostille
6. The residence permit card arrives by post within 2–4 weeks (sometimes up to 90 days)

The expiration of 120 days without an appointment does NOT automatically make you illegal — the law protects you if you have done everything in your power. But it is better to seek an appointment in advance.
1. Check through the AIMA Portal (main channel)
Login via Chave Móvel Digital (government electronic signature) or NIF+password
If you do not have CMD — activate it at autenticacao.gov.pt
Section 'Meus Processos' — list of all your cases
The date and place of the appointment appears in the 'Agendamento (appointment booking)' field
2. Old cases from the SEF database (before October 2023)
Most SEF processes will have migrated to the AIMA Portal by 2026. If your processo is not visible at portal.aima.gov.pt, it likely hasn't been transferred yet, but AIMA is obliged to process it.
An old processo without a trace in the Portal — request via email to the regional AIMA delegation (see the guide 'How to contact AIMA')
In the application, include: full name, NIF (Portuguese tax ID), Nº do processo (SEF or AIMA — whichever you have), date of submission
No record after 30 days of entry — what to do
Check the AIMA Portal again — sometimes a record exists, but without an email notification.
Email to lisboa@aima.gov.pt (or your region) with the subject 'Solicitação de marcação — Visto D'. Attach: visa D (PDF), entry stamp, passport, NIF, proof of address.
Provedor de Justiça (ombudsman) accepts online complaints. It really speeds things up.
Written confirmation of the waiting period — distributed via email. This protects you legally if the 120 days expire without your fault.
❌ "Process not found" — system error, try again in 24 hours; if not, email the region
❌ Appointment booked 6 months in advance — accept as is, AIMA is overloaded
❌ Appointment in another city — you can request a transfer for a valid reason (work, study)
❌ Lost appointment ticket — reprint in Portal or email AIMA
❌ Delay of 120 days — immediately email + complaint to Provedor; the law protects you

Until 2022, crypto was not taxed for individuals — a 'tax haven'. From 2023 (OE 2023), the following was introduced: **28% tax on profits for sales within <1 year**; **0% for ≥365 days of holding** (capital gains long-term); **staking, mining, professional trading** — taxed as operational income. IVA 0% on exchanges. Declaration in IRS via Anexo G and category E for staking. IFICI does not cover crypto. Exchanges and P2P — available, major banks accept transfers from Bitstamp, Kraken, Bitfinex with AML checks.
Profit = sell price − buy price − fees
Losses can be offset against profits for the same year; carry forward for 5 years
Applies to: BTC, ETH, all altcoins, NFTs, derivatives
Condition: the token was held for ≥ 365 consecutive days by one owner
Swapping for another token is considered a sale → counter resets
Transfers between your own wallets are NOT considered sales, the counter continues
FIFO: you sell the first purchased first (for tax purposes)
If the AT considers your activity professional (regular, significant volume) — it is taxed as **Categoria B** (business income)
Professional criteria: regularity, volume, infrastructure (API, automation), main activity
Registering atividade in Finanças with CAE for financial services
Mining: if significant — as Categoria B (business)
Airdrops: not taxed upon receipt; tax upon sale
Crypto is declared annually (April-June for the previous tax year). Works in the standard IRS declaration through Portal das Finanças.
Export the transaction history from each exchange for the year (CSV / Excel)
P&L for each closed position: sell − buy − fees
Conversion of USDT/USDC to € at the rate on the transaction day
Tools: **Koinly**, **CoinTracking**, **Accointing** — €50-200/year, automatic import from all exchanges, generate report for IRS PT
Code 'R02' — long-term (0%, but must declare for accounting)
Specify: amount valor de realização, valor de aquisição, dates
Losses: separately in Quadro 20 for carry forward
Separate from Anexo G. Code 'E21' — income from crypto-assets. Amount received in € on the date of receipt.
✅ **ActivoBank, Millennium BCP, Novobanco** — accept transfers from Bitstamp, Kraken, Bitfinex, Coinbase with correct description 'crypto sale' + AML documents
⚠️ **Caixa Geral, Santander, BPI** — often block / require explanation of the source
📋 When transferring €5,000+: the bank will request a **Declaração de Origem dos Fundos** (Declaration of Funds Origin), wallet screenshots, transaction hash, exchange statements
💳 Prepaid cards from exchanges (Wirex, Crypto.com): work with Apple Pay in PT
🏦 **Banco Invest** and digital-only banks — more open to crypto clients
⚖️ Suspicious patterns (P2P without documents, mixer services) → the bank will close the account due to AML
**Binance**: registered with BaFin (Germany) — available in PT. Verify KYC with PT residence permit
**Kraken**: licensed in the EU, PT-friendly, fast SEPA withdrawals
**Mercado Bitcoin**: Brazilian exchange, not always convenient for PT residents
🛑 **For Russian citizens**: sanctions — Binance closed accounts with Russian KYC in 2024. Use with PT residence permit / non-RU passport, check regulatory status

IFICI (formerly NHR) **does NOT cover** crypto income. If you are under IFICI — crypto is taxed at the general rate (28% short-term). This distinguishes PT from other jurisdictions (e.g. Switzerland is fully crypto-friendly), but the regime with 365+ days holding = 0% — is still one of the best in the EU.
📅 For long-term 0% regime: use **tax-loss harvesting** only considering the 365-day rule. Do not swap unnecessarily.
📊 Keep a **chronological record** of purchases/sales — IRS may request proof during an audit.
🧾 Keep: CSVs from exchanges, screenshots of wallet addresses, tx hashes — for at least 4 years (audit period in AT).
💼 **An accountant is recommended** if your portfolio >€50,000 or many active trades — €300-800/year.
🚫 **Do not withdraw** large amounts without documentation — risk of permanent bank account blocking.
🌍 **Double taxation**: if you are a resident of PT but hold crypto on a US exchange — declaration in PT (country of residence). Fill out US W-8BEN for non-resident status in the USA.

From 2024, the old NHR is abolished. In its place — IFICI (Incentivo Fiscal à Investigação Científica e Inovação) — 20% flat rate on income from qualified activities (science, high technology, startups) for 10 years. The list of professions is narrow. For most remote workers and pensioners, IFICI is NOT suitable.
The old NHR is closed as of 01.01.2024. Those who applied before this date retain NHR for 10 years from the start. New applicants — only IFICI (narrow list of professions).
NHR (Non-Habitual Resident) — the old regime from 2009-2023, offered 10% on pensions + 20% on qualified work + 0% on foreign income. Repealed in 2024.
IFICI — the new regime from 2024 onwards. 20% on income from qualified activities + exemption from tax on foreign income (except pensions).
Key point: IFICI requires 'qualified activity' from a narrow list. Freelance remote workers without such status pay IRS on a progressive scale (11.97%–48% in 9 brackets for 2026).
Research scientists (at a university / research organisation in PT)
Highly qualified professions in technology companies (according to the Portaria list)
Specialists in startups with certified Startup Portugal status
Work in R&D centres with approved status
Freelance developers without a contract with a qualified employer do NOT qualify for IFICI.
Become a tax resident in PT (live 183+ days or have the centre of vital interests)
NOT be a tax resident in PT for the last 5 years
Activity falls under the IFICI list
Submit the application by 15 January of the year following the year of residence (Portaria 352/2024; 2025 — exclusive window until 15 March)
Through Portal das Finanças (tax portal) → change tax address to Portuguese.
Contract with a qualified employer, confirmation of the company's R&D status (certificate from ANI), diploma.
Through Portal das Finanças by 15 January of the following year (Portaria 352/2024 set the deadline).
May take up to 6 months. If approved — the regime is applied retroactively from the beginning of the application year.
If you do not qualify — what to do
Pay the regular IRS on a progressive scale (9 brackets, OE 2026 with +3.51% indexing + reduction of rates in brackets 2–5):

For self-employed individuals (recibos verdes), a simplified regime is possible: 75% of turnover — taxable base. Plus social contributions of 21.4% on 70% of the corresponding income (cap: 12× IAS = €6,445.56). The first 12 months for newly registered — full exemption from contributions.
Portaria 352/2024 — IFICI procedure and deadline 15 January
DRE — Lei do OE/2024 (repeal of NHR)
Filing IRS — personal income tax return

IRS = Imposto sobre o Rendimento das Pessoas Singulares. It is submitted annually from 1 April to 30 June in Portal das Finanças (tax portal). For residents, it is mandatory regardless of the source of income. There is an automatic pre-filled version (IRS Automático) for simple cases.
All tax residents of Portugal (those who have lived ≥ 183 days or have their centre of interests in PT)
Non-residents with income from PT — only submit for Portuguese income
IFICI entities — submit with special forms
Residents declare ALL worldwide income, including foreign income. NHR/IFICI provides exemptions.
The declaration for year N is submitted between 1 April and 30 June of year N+1. Regardless of income category (salary, recibos verdes (self-employed electronic invoices), rental income, etc.).
Missing the deadline — fine of €150-3750. It is better to submit with an error and correct it (modelo de substituição).
Category G — capital gains (sale of shares, crypto)
For category A (salary) without other sources, Finanças prepares the declaration. You simply check and approve. Check on Portal das Finanças → IRS → IRS Automático.
If there are additional sources — filing through Portal das Finanças → IRS → Entregar. Fill in Modelo 3 + necessary annexes (Anexos).
Annex H — deductions (health, education, food, housing)
Automatic validation. After submission — comprovativo (proof) de entrega. Status on Portal.
By July-August — calculation (liquidação). If overpayment — refund. If underpayment — payment deadline by 31 August.
Mortgage — 15% up to €296 (only for mortgages before 2011)
To ensure deductions are counted — request your NIF on all receipts (Faturas.pt collects automatically).
NHR (until 2024): 20% on qualified work + 0% on foreign income. For active subjects — until the end of the 10-year period.
IFICI (from 2024): 20% on qualified activities. Submission through Portal → IFICI profile + Modelo 3 with marking.
IRS checklist for the end of the year — what to prepare

Preparation for the IRS declaration starts in December: check the coverage of deduction categories (health, education, housing, restaurants) in Faturas.pt, activate deductions by 31 December, gather documents for foreign income. For all others. Portal das Finanças → IRS → Entregar. Select the necessary Anexos.

How to issue a recibo verde (self-employed electronic receipt) — step by step

Recibo verde — electronic receipt for self-employed / freelancer. Issued in Portal das Finanças (tax portal) → “Recibos Verdes Eletrónicos”. Required: open atividade (business activity), NIF of the client, amount, description of the service. IVA depends on the regime (normal 23% / exemption < €14,500/year). Example for freelancer in the first year: usually without IVA (regime especial). Deadline for issuance — by the 5th day of the following month after payment.
What you need before issuing your first recibo
✅ Open atividade at Finanças (Início de Atividade)
✅ NIF and password for Portal Finanças (or CMD)
✅ VAT decision: special regime (if first year + turnover < €14,500) or nominal 23%
✅ Active NISS for Segurança Social (social security)
Issuing a recibo — step by step
portaldasfinancas.gov.pt → «Os Seus Serviços» → «Emitir Faturas e Recibos Eletrónicos» → «Recibos».
If the client is outside the EU — «Não sujeito a IVA» autoselect
Special regime: 0% VAT, note «IVA — regime de isenção art. 53»
Exemption: client in another EU country with VAT code → 0% VAT (reverse charge)
Client legal entity PT withholds 11.5% (article 101 CIRS)
IFICI status — retention 11.5% but refund annually

Exchange of driving licences for Portuguese ones

EU/EEA citizens — automatic recognition until expiry (without exchange). CPLP (Brazil, Cape Verde, etc.) — exchange without exam according to IMT. From third countries based on bilateral agreements (UA, KZ, MD, etc.) — exchange without exam. There is currently NO reciprocity with Russia and Belarus — only full theory + practical test. Ukrainian licences are recognised for the entire duration of Proteção Temporária (until 4 March 2027). The deadline for exchange after becoming a resident (non-EU) is 2 years (DL 138/2012).

According to Decreto-Lei 138/2012, foreign driving licences (non-EU) are valid in Portugal **for 2 years from the date of becoming a resident**. After that, exchange or re-registration is mandatory, otherwise, there will be a fine and violation. For Ukrainians with Proteção Temporária — validity for the entire protection period (until 4 March 2027 under Regulation EU 2022/1280).
Medical certificate from an IMT-accredited pharmacy (~€30)

60-120 days. Upon completion — an invitation to collect the new card or receive it by post.

Theory (código da estrada) + practical driving with an instructor. Course at a driving school €500-900. Exams are paid (~€30-50).
Portuguese driving licences are valid until the age of 70, thereafter — re-examination every 2 years. Replacement of category B — indefinite (with periodic medical checks from age 50).

EU/EEA/Switzerland: automatically valid in Portugal. If desired, you can obtain Portuguese ones (registration at IMT). It is mandatory to register the licence at IMT within 60 days of becoming a resident.
CPLP (with agreement): exchange without exam. Translation is not required for Brazilians. IMT processing time — 60-120 days.

Third countries with a valid bilateral agreement (Ukraine since 2019, several others) — exchange without exam. Russia and Belarus — no reciprocity, full theory + practical tests required again. Kazakhstan/Moldova/Azerbaijan — check the current IMT list (imt-ip.pt).

Cromeleque dos Almendres — a megalithic complex 15 km west of Évora. Older than Stonehenge by 2000 years (built around 6000-5000 BC). 95 granite menhirs arranged in circles and ovals, aligned with the solstices. One of the largest in Europe. Free access, open 24/7, accessible only by car.

Cromlech (circular group of standing stones) from the Neolithic era, built between 6000 and 3000 BC in stages (4 construction phases). Of the 95 remaining menhirs, some reach 3 m in height. Many are decorated with carvings — solar symbols, anthropomorphic figures, spirals.
The orientation of the stones is related to astronomical events — especially the sunrise at the equinox and solstice. It is believed to have been a cult site and/or observatory.
🚗 By car from Évora — 15-20 minutes (15 km west)
📍 Coordinates: 38.5579°N, 8.0606°W, enter "Cromeleque dos Almendres" in Google Maps
🛣️ The last 4 km is a dirt road through a cork forest. Passable for cars in dry weather; after rain — only for 4x4 vehicles
🚴 By bike from Évora takes 1 hour on the roads
🚶 From Évora ~3 hours on foot, there is a route along tracks; no water supply
Two intersecting circles of 95 menhirs (diameter 60-80 m)
The engravings are explained on information boards (EN/PT)
Nearby — Menhir dos Almendres (a solitary 4.5-metre menhir, 1.4 km to the north)
Carries — dolmens (tombs) scattered around the area (Anta Grande do Zambujeiro — the largest in the EU, 10 km to the south)
⏰ Best time: dawn (summer 6:00-7:00) — the stones are illuminated, no tourists
🌅 On the equinox (March, September) and solstice (June, December) — unofficial gatherings of esotericists and archaeologists

Aveiro and Costa Nova — the 'Portuguese Venice'

Aveiro — a coastal city with a lagoon and canals, where painted moliceiros boats sail. The neighbouring Costa Nova is a fishing village with striped houses. Ovos moles — a signature dessert. 35 minutes by train from Porto, 2-2.5 hours from Lisbon. Half a day to a day is enough.

The Azores — a volcanic archipelago 1500 km from the mainland, consisting of 9 islands. The main airport is Ponta Delgada on São Miguel. Green craters, whales, hot springs, unique nature. Plan for at least 7-10 days for 2-3 islands. PT residents receive a subsidy for flights.

The Azores — an autonomous region of PT. 9 volcanic islands, divided into 3 groups: eastern (São Miguel, Santa Maria), central (Terceira, Graciosa, São Jorge, Pico, Faial), western (Flores, Corvo). Total area 2333 km².
The climate is mild subtropical (+14-24°C year-round), but changeable: “there can be 4 seasons in one day.” Plenty of greenery, lakes, waterfalls.
The largest island (760 km²), with the main airport. Half of the archipelago's population lives here. A minimum of 4-5 days is recommended.
🌋 Lagoa das Sete Cidades — a crater with blue and green lakes. Miradouro da Vista do Rei
♨️ Furnas — a valley with hot springs. Cozido das Furnas — meat cooked in a volcano for 6 hours

The 'Blue Island' due to the hydrangeas. Capelinhos — volcano from 1957-58, lunar landscape. Horta port — a stop for sailors.
An elongated island with cliffs (fajãs — coastal plateaus). São Jorge DOP cheese, Verdelho grape, gastronomic festivals.
The westernmost island, over 50 waterfalls, untouched nature, hydrangeas.
The oldest island (8.2 million years), red sand — a rarity in the Azores. Santa Maria festival every 5 years.
UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Sunny distinctions — the driest island. Furna do Enxofre — cave with a sulphurous lake.
The smallest island (17 km²), 400 inhabitants. Caldeirão — a giant crater.

Azores and Madeira — subsidies for residents + how to buy

PT residents receive the **‘Social Territorial de Passageiros’** (STP) subsidy for flights to the Azores and Madeira — up to €134 reimbursement per ticket (2026). This applies to PT residents with NIF + NISS. They purchase a full ticket, then claim the subsidy through SATA Azores / TAP / Ryanair (some routes). Application: online at sata.pt. Compensated for up to 4 trips/year. For residents of the islands — even more subsidies through Governo Regional.
A flight subsidy programme between mainland Portugal and the autonomous regions (Azores, Madeira) — compensates for the price difference between the mainland and the islands.
✅ Who is eligible: **residents of Portugal** with NIF (foreigners with a residence permit — also!)
✅ **Residents mainland → islands** (as tourists): lower subsidy, depending on the route
✅ **Max 4 trips per year** for residents of the islands; 2 for tourists
❌ Not applicable for: business trips (if paid by a company), non-residents
💰 Example: Lisbon-Ponta Delgada ticket €210 → subsidy €80 → net cost €130

The system automatically applies the 'residente' tariff

Berlengas — nature reserve and fortress island

Arquipélago das Berlengas — a group of granite islands 10 km from Peniche. A UNESCO biosphere reserve. The main island, Berlenga Grande, is open from May to September, with 550 visitors per day (booking is mandatory). On the island: fort São João Baptista (17th century, overnight stays in the 'cells' are possible), beaches, diving, trekking, sea caves. 45 minutes by fast boat, 1 hour 15 minutes by regular boat.
Booking for the visit is mandatory
Since 2011, ICNF (Instituto da Conservação da Natureza e das Florestas) has limited access — 550 people per day on Berlenga Grande, to preserve the ecosystem.

berlengasreservas.icnf.pt → select the date and number of people. Registration (without payment) opens ≥ 30 days before the visit. For July-August, book on the opening day of the window, as spots fill up within hours.
Feeling Berlenga, Viamar, Julius Berlengas — the main operators (available at Peniche port)
Fast boat: €22-28 round-trip, 45 minutes one way
In case of storms, trips are cancelled — you will be refunded or rescheduled
3. On the day of the trip
Porto de Recreio pier (there's a large car park nearby)
Show ICNF reservation on paper or from your phone
Buy sunscreen, water, snacks in Peniche (expensive and limited on the island)
What to see on Berlenga Grande

Built in 1651 on a separate islet, connected to the main island by a stone bridge. You can stay overnight in the fort's guesthouse (from €40/night, book 6 months in advance). Sunset photos with the bridge are the archipelago's postcard.
The only sandy beach. Clear water, 18-22°C in season. Diving and snorkelling — crystal clear water, lots of fish.
Tour on a local boat (€10-15, 45 min): they circle the island, visiting Gruta Azul, Cova do Sonho, and the huge tunnel Furado Grande. Book with fishermen right on the beach.

The only path from the pier to the fort is 1.5 km on foot, a trail over granite cliffs. There is no transport on the island. Routes: to the Duque de Bragança lighthouse (summit, 85 m), to the Farol (lighthouse), circular trail 3 km.
🏰 Forte São João Baptista — guesthouse in former monastic cells. €40-60/night. Book 5 months in advance.
🏕️ Campismo da Berlenga — tent camping. €10-15/night + landing fee. You need to register separately at the ICNF (10 spots/night)
🐟 There is one restaurant, Mar e Sol (salted fish, sea urchins). €15-25/person
❌ No shops, no ATM, no mobile signal (occasionally Vodafone works)
⚡ Electricity only from the fort's solar panels — from 18:00 to 23:00
When is the best time to go
Season: mid-May to end of September. Off-season, boats do not operate
Weather: July-August — warm, but many tourists. May/June and September — ideal
Storms in spring = frequent cancellations. Check the forecast for Peniche
The wind is often strong — take a windbreaker even in the heat
☀️ A day on Berlengas = 5-6 hours (arrival at 11:00, return at 16:00-17:00)
⚠️ Take a sea sickness tablet (Vertigin, Dramamine) 30 minutes before — the boat rocks
🐟 Fishing on the island is prohibited (nature reserve)
🐛 There are seagulls — they behave aggressively if food is open; do not feed them
🏊 Swim only in designated areas — dangerous currents beyond
📷 Phone in a plastic zip-lock — sea spray

Braga and Guimarães — the 'cradle of Portugal'

Braga — the 'Portuguese Rome', religious capital, 11th-century cathedral, famous Bom Jesus do Monte (UNESCO). Guimarães (UNESCO) — the place where Portugal was born in 1128, the castle of the first king Afonso Henriques. One hour by train from Porto. Both cities can be visited in a day with Porto as a base.
🚆 Between Braga and Guimarães: 35 min train, €3.15
🚗 By car: Porto-Braga 50 min A3, Porto-Guimarães 50 min A7
The oldest city in the country (Roman Bracara Augusta, 2nd century BC). Archiepiscopal capital, 35+ churches. Religious festivals (Semana Santa, processions) — the best season.
⛪ Sé de Braga — the oldest cathedral in the country (11th century), a blend of Romanesque, Gothic, and Manueline styles. €5
⛪ Bom Jesus do Monte (UNESCO) — baroque sanctuary on a hill 5 km from the centre. The legendary 'staircase of the 5 senses', funicular from 1882 on a water counterweight. €3 funicular
⛪ Santuário do Sameiro — second hill sanctuary next to Bom Jesus
🏛️ Palácio do Raio — baroque palace with azulejo façade
🏛️ Museu dos Biscainhos — 17th-century palace with a garden
🌳 Parque de Santa Bárbara — central park with fountains
🎪 Semana Santa Braga — Holy Week with the largest processions in Iberia
Historical capital of Portugal until 1139. On the wall, the inscription reads «Aqui nasceu Portugal» («Here Portugal was born»). Compact historical centre — UNESCO.
🏰 Castelo de Guimarães (11th century) — the castle where Afonso Henriques, the first king, was born. €2
🏛️ Paço dos Duques de Bragança (15th century) — Burgundian castle-palace, residence of the dukes. €5
⛪ Igreja de São Miguel do Castelo — Romanesque chapel from the 12th century, the baptism site of Afonso
🏛️ Largo da Oliveira — main square with an ancient olive tree and a Gothic monument
⛪ Igreja da Nossa Senhora da Oliveira — Gothic monastery-church
🚡 Teleférico da Penha — cable car to Penha mountain, views. €5 round-trip
🏛️ Museu Alberto Sampaio — medieval art in monastery galleries
10:00-12:00 — Braga centre: Sé, Palácio do Raio, Largo do Paço
12:00-14:00 — Bom Jesus do Monte (bus 2 or taxi €10). Lunch at the café by the funicular
15:00-18:00 — castle, palace of the dukes, historic centre, Largo da Oliveira
🐟 Bacalhau à Braga — cod with potato croquettes
🥟 Pudim do Abade de Priscos — famous Braga dessert with pork fat
🥖 Bola de Berlim — doughnut, but special in Braga
🍷 Vinho Verde — native region: light whites
🍴 Histórico by Papaboa (Guimarães) — in a restored 13th-century palace.
✝️ Semana Santa Braga (March-April) — largest Passion Week processions

Coimbra — the oldest university in Portugal

Coimbra — university city in the centre of the country, 1 hour from Lisbon or Porto by train. Universidade de Coimbra (UNESCO, 1290) with the legendary Biblioteca Joanina. The atmosphere of an ancient academy, fado in a male style. One day is enough.
🚆 From Coimbra-B (the main intercity station) to Coimbra (city centre) — urban 6 min, €1.60
🚗 By car: via A1 Lisboa-Porto — 2 hours from Lisbon, 1.5 hours from Porto
The main attraction — the oldest university in the country (1290), one of the oldest in Europe. On a hill in the city centre. Combined entry €14 including the library.
📚 Biblioteca Joanina (1728) — a baroque library with 60,000 volumes from the 16th-18th centuries. Special bats guard the books from insects! Slots of 20 minutes
🎓 Capela de São Miguel — university chapel with a baroque organ
🏛️ Paço das Escolas — courtyard with a monument to João III, terrace with a view of the Mondego River
🗼 Torre da Universidade — bell tower, entry €5
🏛️ Museu da Ciência — scientific collections from the 18th-19th centuries. €5
🎓 Sala dos Capelos — main hall for degree conferral ceremonies
⛪ Sé Velha — old cathedral from the 12th century, Romanesque style. €3
⛪ Sé Nova — new cathedral from the 16th century, Baroque
🏛️ Mosteiro de Santa Cruz — royal pantheon, tombs of the first kings Afonso I and Sancho I. €4
🌳 Jardim Botânico da Universidade — one of the oldest in Europe (18th century), free
🏛️ Portagem / Parque Manuel Braga — Mondego riverside, Santa Clara bridge
🛕 Mosteiro de Santa Clara-a-Velha — ruins of a Gothic monastery by the river. €4
🎨 Museu Nacional Machado de Castro — former episcopal palace + Roman cryptportico. €6
Students of Coimbra wear black cloaks (capas e batinas) — a tradition since the 13th century. In spring (May) — Queima das Fitas, the graduation celebration: processions, concerts, songs.
🎵 Fado de Coimbra — the male version of fado, performed only by men in cloaks. It differs from the Lisbon style. Concerts at Fado ao Centro or ao Capricho
🎓 Praxe — student initiation rituals for freshmen (September-October)
🎪 Queima das Fitas — 8 days in May, concerts at Parque Santa Cruz
🎭 Bar Académico — bars exclusively for students, but easy to enter with a pass
🏛️ Conímbriga (15 km) — ruins of the largest Roman city in Portugal, 2nd century BC. Mosaics, forum, baths. €5
🌲 Serra da Lousã (30 km) — mountains with slate villages (Talasnal, Candal, Cerdeira)
⛰️ Penela (30 km) — medieval castle from the 12th century.
🏖️ Figueira da Foz (45 km) — beach resort on the ocean
🥩 Chanfana — goat in red wine (regional dish)
🥖 Pastéis de Tentúgal — crispy pastry with cream (from 16th-century nuns)
🧁 Pastéis de Santa Clara — round pies with sweet bean filling

CP trains (Comboios de Portugal) — how to use them

CP — state railway operator. Types: Alfa Pendular (fast Lisboa-Porto 2.5h), Intercidades (medium), Regional (slow), Urbanos (suburban). Discount cards: Cartão Jovem, Cartão Amigo. Lisboa-Porto Alfa Pendular ~€25-40, Intercidades ~€20-30. Book at cp.pt.
CP flagship. Fast tilting trains. Lisboa-Porto in 2.5 hours. Wi-Fi, power sockets, café car. 2 classes: Conforto (economy), Primeira (first). Routes: Lisboa-Coimbra-Porto-Braga, Lisboa-Algarve.
Intercity, 30-50 minutes slower than AP, 20-30% cheaper. Same main routes + branches. Comfortable.
Lisboa-Madrid night Sud Expresso (no longer operates since 2020 — replaced by bus). There is a Porto-Vigo regional service across the border.
Slow, frequent stops. For small towns. Cheap.
Suburban trains of Lisbon (Cascais, Sintra, Tomar, Setúbal) and Porto (Braga, Aveiro, Guimarães). Payment via Navegante/Andante or regular ticket.
Lisboa ↔ Porto AP: 2 hours 45 minutes, €25-40 (discounts up to -65% for 5 days in advance)
cp.pt or CP app. Registration, NIF (preferably), payment card.
5 days in advance: -65% on AP (Lisbon-Porto from €40 to €14)
PDF or in the app. The controller scans the QR code. If Alfa Pendular — seat is reserved.
No reservation required. Can be purchased at the station, through the app or with a Navegante (Lisbon) / Andante (Porto) card.
Santa Apolónia — traditional, for AP/IC to Porto, north
Oriente — modern, Parque das Nações, all AP/IC + Urbanos
Gare do Oriente — synonymous with Oriente, metro red line
Cais do Sodré — Urbanos to Cascais
Domestic flights and ferries — Madeira, Azores, mainland

For mainland-island travel, a flight is mandatory. TAP and Ryanair fly Lisboa/Porto → Madeira (Funchal) / Azores (Ponta Delgada). Azores — subsidised prices for residents. Ferries Soflusa/Transtejo across the Tejo (Lisboa), Douro cruises (Porto), Azores between islands.
National carrier. Lisboa/Porto → Funchal 4-8 flights/day, 1.5h. Lisboa → Ponta Delgada daily, 2h 30min. Prices from €50 with early booking up to €300 in season.
Cheap tickets from €20 Lisboa-Funchal, Porto-Faro. But there are extra charges for baggage and seats. It's important to read the fine print.
Regional carrier of the Azores. Inter-island flights (10-30 min), €40-80. There are also flights from Lisboa to the Azores.
TAP flies 8-10 times a day, 50 min. However, train or bus is often cheaper considering the airport. Train is recommended.
Residents of the Azores and Madeira receive subsidised fares on domestic flights — up to 80% off. Proof of residency is required.
Buy through the 'tarifa residente' on SATA or TAP indicating the residence permit for Madeira/Azores.
Transtejo / Soflusa — ferries across the River Tejo. Main stops: Cacilhas (south bank, view of Lisbon), Barreiro, Seixal, Montijo, Trafaria. Payment via Navegante or ticket. From Cais do Sodré/Belém.
Short cruises (6 bridges, 45 min, €18-25) and longer ones (to Régua, full day, €60-100). For Porto residents — rabelo catamarans from Cais da Ribeira.
Atlânticoline — ferries between the islands. In season (April-October), all lines operate. In winter — limited service. Between neighbouring islands, 1-2 hours, €15-30.
Porto Santo Line. Daily ferry Funchal → Porto Santo (a small island with beaches), 2.5 hours, €30-60.
Madeira and the Azores have different climates. Madeira is consistently +20-25°C, the Azores have changeable weather.
For Madeira / Azores — book early, peak season July-August x2.
Ferries Lisboa — use the Navegante card, it's cheaper.
Airport → city: Lisboa Alameda metro €1.70 20 minutes; Porto Aeroporto metro (purple line) €2.25 30 minutes.
Ryanair — always with hand luggage, extra charge for checked baggage.
TAP Miles & Go — earn miles (can be done via Millennium BCP card).
Douro Valley — a UNESCO wine region

Douro Valley (Alto Douro Vinhateiro, UNESCO since 2001) — the oldest demarcated wine region in the world (1756). Terraced vineyards along the river, port wine, picturesque villages. From Porto: train to Régua/Pinhão (2-2.5 hours), car, river cruises, helicopter. Ideal for 2-3 days.

Douro is a river that flows into the Atlantic in Porto. Upstream is the Alto Douro region (250 km from Spain), where grapes for port wine and table wines are grown. The first protected regional designation in the world (1756, Marquis of Pombal).
Terraced vineyards (socalcos) — a man-made landscape listed by UNESCO. Slopes up to 60°. Grape varieties: Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, Tinta Roriz.
Porto-São Bento → Peso da Régua (2 hours, €11) → Pinhão (40 minutes, €4). The train runs along the riverbank, one of the most scenic in Europe. Sit on the right side (if coming from Porto).
From Porto 1.5-2 hours to Régua. Further to Pinhão, Favaios, São João da Pesqueira — all along picturesque roads. It is recommended to rent for 2 days.
From Porto (Cais da Estiva / Gaia) — day trips (to Régua, €60-80) or multi-day (3-8 days along the river) with CroisiEurope, Douro Azul, etc. Includes lunch, sometimes dinner + tasting.
Helitours from Viseu/Régua — 20-minute flight from €150. Car tours from Porto — €90-150 per person with a guide and visit to 2-3 quintas.
The main 'entrance' to Douro. Museu do Douro (history of the region, €7.50). Departure point for cruises. Many quintas nearby.
The heart of the region. Train station with azulejos — legendary. Panoramic views from Casal de Loivos (10 min by car). Numerous quintas within a 10 km radius.
An architectural gem — a well-preserved 18th-century village with barquinho (traditional bread), fountains, and heraldic facades.
The upper part, less touristy. Old quintas, churches, views.
Moscatel do Douro (sweet wine), famous since the 17th century. Village hugged into vineyards. Visit Adega Cooperativa de Favaios.
The dam with a navigation lock at 35 m is the highest in Europe. Cruise ships pass through.
Most large quintas are open to tourists with tours and tastings (€15-40 per person). Book 1-2 weeks in advance during the season. Many have restaurants and guest houses.

🍷 Quinta do Crasto — stunning signature pool, premium tours

🍷 Quinta de la Rosa — Anglo-Portuguese family, Relais & Châteaux

Évora and Alentejo — white towns and wineries

Évora (UNESCO) — the main city of Alentejo, 1.5 hours from Lisbon. Chapel of Bones (Capela dos Ossos), Roman temple of Diana, cathedral. Surrounding are the plains of Alentejo with whitewashed villages, olive groves, vineyards, rare authenticity. 1 day — Évora, 2-3 days — the region.
🚗 A6 motorway — 1.5h. A car is almost essential for the Alentejo region
🚐 Day tour from Lisbon €65-90 — not recommended, too rushed
🏛️ Templo Romano (Templo de Diana) — Roman temple from the 1st century, 14 Corinthian columns. Free entry
💀 Capela dos Ossos — chapel adorned with the bones of 5,000 monks, 16th century. Inscription above the entrance: 'Nós ossos que aqui estamos, pelos vossos esperamos'. €6
⛪ Sé de Évora — Gothic cathedral from the 13th century, treasury with the Holy Shroud. €5
🏛️ Praça do Giraldo — main square with a marble fountain
🕌 Igreja de São Francisco — above Capela dos Ossos
🏛️ Museu de Évora — in the bishop's palace, sacred art
🌊 Aqueduto da Água de Prata — aqueduct from the 16th century
🎓 Universidade de Évora — Jesuit university from the 16th century, the oldest after Coimbra
Alentejo is one of the largest wine regions in PT. Character: full-bodied reds (Aragonês, Trincadeira, Alicante Bouschet), aromatic whites (Antão Vaz). Tours range from €20 to €60.
🍷 Herdade do Esporão (Reguengos de Monsaraz) — large, with a restaurant on the hill. Tours + lunches
🍷 Adega Mayor (Campo Maior) — architecture by Álvaro Siza Vieira
🍷 Herdade do Rocim — experiments with amphora
Medieval 'deserto' on the hill, view of the Alqueva lake (artificial, largest in Europe). Castle, narrow whitewashed streets. 1-2 hours.
Mountain town at the Spanish border, castle, views of Serra de São Mamede. One of the most atmospheric places in PT.
UNESCO — the largest preserved bastion fortification complex in the world. Amoreira aqueduct, fortress.
Marble everywhere, palace of the Dukes of Braganza (former royal residence).
Saturday market with antiques, castle with pousada hotel.
Former Arab capital, former mosque-church, Moorish architecture.
🥣 Açorda alentejana — bread soup with garlic and coriander
🍄 Carne de porco à alentejana — pork with clams
🍮 Sericaia com ameixa de Elvas — sweet pudding with dried plum
🍷 Vinho da talha — wine aged in amphorae, a millennia-old tradition
🗓️ Best seasons: April-May (flowering), September-October (harvest, mild climate)
🔥 In summer +35-40°C — hot. Plan visits for the morning and evening.
🏨 Pousadas — a network of hotels in historical buildings (monasteries, fortresses). Pousada de Évora, Pousada de Estremoz, Pousada de Crato

Hiking and eco-trails in Portugal — from weekend to multi-day trips

Portugal has over 300 marked trails (PR, GR, levadas). The most popular are: Levadas in Madeira (350 km), Rota Vicentina in SW Alentejo (450 km), PR trails in Serra da Estrela, Gerês, Açores. Short day hikes (3-15 km, PR) are available in every natural park; multi-day GR trails are marked red-white, with accommodation in albergues and rural houses. Free entry, but some parks require online registration. Season: April-October mainland; Madeira year-round.
**Rota Vicentina** (GR 11) — 450 km from Cabo de São Vicente to Santiago do Cacém, SW Alentejo
**Caminho Português de Santiago** (GR 1) — 5 routes from PT to Santiago in Spain, 140-620 km
**Rota do Guadiana** — along the Guadiana River, 65 km
**GR 22 — Aldeias Históricas** — 540 km through 12 historic villages
Marked with the symbol 'PR' + number + municipality name
150+ PR trails registered with FCMP (Federação de Campismo e Montanhismo)

Equipment: non-slip boots, flashlight (for tunnels), waterproof

Boots or trekking shoes (not street sneakers)

🐍 **Snakes**: vípera seoanei is venomous — rarely encountered, usually flees. Check your boots in the morning

Historic Villages — 12 historic villages of Portugal

Aldeias Históricas de Portugal — programme for the preservation of 12 medieval villages in central Portugal: Belmonte, Castelo Mendo, Castelo Novo, Castelo Rodrigo, Idanha-a-Velha, Linhares da Beira, Marialva, Monsanto, Piódão, Sortelha, Trancoso, Almeida. Stone architecture, fortress walls, castles from the 13th to 16th centuries. A compact road trip from Porto or Lisbon — 2-3 days, route approximately 500 km.

A village literally built into giant granite boulders. The houses 'share' the rocks with their walls. In 1938, it was awarded the title 'Aldeia mais portuguesa de Portugal'. Filming for 'House of the Dragon' (HBO) took place here in 2021.
Regarding: 2 hours for sightseeing, another hour for the castelo (ruins at the top)
Restaurants: Petiscos e Granitos, Adega Típica — traditional cuisine in granite halls
The village of Pedro Álvares Cabral — the discoverer of Brazil (1500). Jewish heritage — the largest surviving Sephardic community in Europe.
Castelo de Belmonte — you can enter
Proximity: 1 hour to Serra da Estrela (ski resort in winter)
🌲 Piódão — slate village in the mountains
On the slope of Serra do Açor. All houses are made of black slate — creating a uniform landscape. Narrow stone streets, a small blue church Igreja Matriz.
Road: narrow winding road, 2-3 hours from Coimbra
Trekking: route Foz de Égua (river walk), Cascata de Benfeita
In winter, snow may close the roads
1. **Almeida** — star fortress of the 17th century (Vauban-style), an excellent example of military architecture
3. **Castelo Mendo** — tiny, with two city gates, 300 inhabitants
5. **Castelo Rodrigo** — on the border with Spain, well preserved, 16-year siege in history
6. **Idanha-a-Velha** — Roman town, one of the oldest villages in PT
8. **Marialva** — 'ghost town': the old town on the hill is abandoned, new settlement below
11. **Sortelha** — 13th-century fortress on a rock, the most 'fairy-tale' view
12. **Trancoso** — the birthplace of the prophet Bandarra, a medieval culture festival every July

Day 2: Sortelha → Belmonte → Castelo Novo → overnight in Monsanto
Day 3: Monsanto → Idanha-a-Velha → back to Lisboa (450 km from Monsanto)

Morning: Porto → Trancoso (2 hours) — lunch in the medieval town
Day 2: Almeida (star fort) → back to Porto through the Douro valley

Buses between cities in Portugal — Rede Expressos, FlixBus, Alsa

Main operators: Rede Expressos (main network PT, from Sete Rios), FlixBus (international + part of PT), Alsa (focus on Spain), EVA Transportes (south, Algarve). Lisboa-Porto ≈ 3.5 hours €15-25, Lisboa-Faro ≈ 3 hours €20-30. Book online, cheaper in advance.

The main network in Portugal. Covers all major and medium cities. From Lisbon — Sete Rios station (metro Jardim Zoológico). Booking at rede-expressos.pt or the app. Prices €10-30 depending on the time.
German operator. Strong coverage Lisbon-Porto, Lx-Faro, + international (Spain, France, Germany). Best prices with early booking (from €5). Wi-Fi + sockets.
Spanish operator. Good for routes to Spain (Madrid, Barcelona, Seville). Within Portugal — Lisboa-Porto, Lisboa-Faro. Often cheaper than FlixBus.
Regional operator in the Algarve. Good for moving around the south (Faro-Tavira-Lagos).
For trips to small towns and villages. Timetables are published at the bus station (rodoviária) of the respective city, as well as on the IMT website — the transport regulator.
Lisboa ↔ Porto: 3.5h, €15-25 (FlixBus from €5 a week in advance)
Compare prices on operators' websites directly — this always gives an up-to-date picture. Ticket prices change dynamically depending on the load of the service and the day of the week.
Booking directly on the operator's website is usually the most reliable. The best prices are 2-4 weeks before the trip; on the day of departure, rates are higher by 30-70%.
QR code in email or app. No need to print. Check the bus number/platform on the bus station display.
The earlier you book, the cheaper it is. 2-4 weeks in advance — from €5 for FlixBus
FlixBus/Alsa is more punctual than Rede Expressos, but all are fine.
Luggage: 1 hand luggage + 1 suitcase usually included. Extra charge for a 2nd suitcase
Wi-Fi on most modern buses, but unstable
Late evening/night buses — save on accommodation

In Porto — Campanhã and Rodoviária Campanhã

Intercity transport in Portugal — train, bus, plane

Three channels: CP trains (comfort + Lisbon–Porto–Braga network + Alentejo), Rede Expressos buses (cheap national network) and FlixBus (international). Islands — TAP, SATA, Ryanair flights and Atlanticoline ferries. Algarve — EVA buses. The aggregator Omio brings everything together. Book 2–4 weeks in advance — prices drop by 30–50%.
National railway. Covers Lisboa — Porto — Braga, Lisbon — Alentejo — Algarve, Coimbra, mountainous routes Douro. Does not cover the islands.
Alfa Pendular — high-speed (220 km/h), Lisboa–Porto 2:40, comfort ‘Conforto’ / ‘Turístico’
Discounts: 50% for youth aged 4-13, 25-50% for students, 50% for seniors 65+
Interrail Pass: available to residents of PT

International network. Within PT: Lisbon–Porto (€10–25), Lisbon–Faro (€15–35). Abroad: PT–Spain (Madrid €35), PT–France (Paris €100). Plus: cheap prices, downside: fewer routes than Rede Expressos.
Spanish operator, PT–Spain routes. Lisbon–Madrid, Porto–Santiago. From €25.
Algarve. Faro–Lagos, Albufeira and rural routes. €5–15.
Rodonorte (north), Rodoviária do Alentejo, Rodoviária do Tejo, and others. For remote villages and towns — often the only transport available.
